steps.m: Three special letters: Alif, Lam, Ra
steps.n: These are three separate Arabic letters written individually: Alif (ا), Lām (ل), and Rā (ر)

steps.m: Three special letters: Alif, Lam, Ra (opening of Surah)
steps.n: These letters are written together with a special mark (ٓ) to show they are mysterious opening letters of this chapter. While we can pronounce them as individual letters, their deeper meaning is known only to Allah.
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to something far away, usually feminine and singular
Added sukun (ْ) to show where the word pauses before the ending

steps.m: that (pointing to something far away) (describes ءَايٰتُ)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show we're pointing to something far away. In this ayah, it's pointing to ءَايٰتُ (verses) that come after it.
Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'sign'
The hamza (ء) was written separately at the start - same word and meaning, just a different way of writing it
Changed to plural form by removing ة and adding ات. The alif is written as ٰ (alif khanjariya)
Added damma to show this is the subject being pointed to by تِلْكَ (those)
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Changes from 'he wrote' (action) to 'book' (object) by changing middle vowel to 'i' and adding long 'aa'
Adding ال makes it specific - we're talking about a particular book
The kasra indicates this word is in the genitive case, often showing possession or connection
Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who has this quality strongly
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific wise one
Adding kasra shows this describes 'the book' (ٱلْكِتٰبِ) in the ayah
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter). This is a common change in Arabic when making past tense verbs.

steps.m: was he/it? (questioning the state of something in لِلنَّاسِ عَجَبًا)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a yes/no question. This word starts a question about whether something was strange/surprising to people.

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others

steps.n: Adding alif after ن changes it to mean 'one who is social/friendly' -> 'people'
Added ال to make it definite
Added لِ meaning 'for/to' and kasra at the end to show connection
Changes from verb 'to be amazed' into the noun 'amazement'
Adding ًا at the end makes this word describe how something is done - in this case, describing how strange or amazing something is

steps.m: that (introduces what was revealed in أَوْحَيْنَآ)
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces what was revealed/inspired. Here it connects 'We revealed' (أَوْحَيْنَآ) to what was actually revealed, which was the command to warn people.

steps.m: to reveal or inspire divine knowledge

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أ at the start to mean 'caused someone to do something'. The middle letter changed to و because of spelling rules.
Added نا to show 'we' did the action. This is often used in the Quran when Allah speaks about Himself.
Word is already complete with both Form IV pattern (أ) and 'we' suffix (نا)
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It's one of the core words in Arabic that isn't built from a root.
Added the vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce the word - إِ with a kasra and لَ with a fatha
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's an alif maksura. This connects to the word رَجُلٍ (a man) to show who/what is being directed towards.
The vowels make it into a noun meaning 'man'
Added tanween kasra to show this is the object of the preposition 'to' (إِلَىٰ) in the phrase 'to a man'
Starting with the basic preposition مِن which means 'from'
Added sukun on the noon to prepare for connecting with next part
Added هُم at the end to show who is being referred to (them)
Added shadda on initial meem due to assimilation of the noon from the previous word in the ayah
This is a connecting word that introduces what was revealed. It connects أَوْحَيْنَآ (we revealed) to what comes after it, like saying 'We revealed that...'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding أَ at start and changing vowels to kasra. This word is commanding someone to warn الناس (the people) in this ayah.
Changed the root into a word for those who are close/familiar with each other (people)
Added ال to specify these particular people and doubled the ن letter for pronunciation
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of warning in the phrase 'warn the people'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more intense/emphatic

steps.m: and give good news! (command to one person)
steps.n: Added 'and' (و) at start + changed to a command form telling one person to give good news
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people

steps.m: those who (plural, referring to believers in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because ذ is a sun letter, which makes the ل sound blend into it.
Added alif after first letter to strengthen the meaning from just trusting to having complete faith
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
This is a basic particle that connects parts of sentences, like saying 'that' in English

steps.m: that/indeed (emphasizes the statement about قَدَمَ صِدْقٍ)
steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger and more certain. In this ayah, it emphasizes that they definitely have a good status with their Lord.
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
Added هم at the end to show this is for 'them' (a group)
Adding the three fathas creates the simplest past tense verb form
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra on first letter and sukun on middle letter. This pattern turns an action into the concept itself.
Added tanwin kasra (two dots under) to show this is describing قَدَمَ (position/status) in the ayah, meaning 'a position of truth'
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning a place or state of being near something
Added fatha at the end to show this word connects to رَبِّهِمْ (their Lord) in the ayah, indicating something is 'in the presence of' their Lord
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (shadda) emphasizes the complete and constant nature of the nurturing, making it a title for one who nurtures perfectly
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by or connected to something

steps.m: their Lord (connected to عِندَ meaning 'with their Lord')
steps.n: Added the suffix هِمْ to show this is their Lord. This word is connected to عِندَ in the ayah, showing they have a high status with their Lord.
Added fathas to make basic past tense form
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common for hollow verbs
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and show past tense meaning
This form shows someone who does the action, like adding '-er' in English (teach → teacher). In this ayah, the disbelievers are the ones doing the action of saying (قال), so they are the subject.
Added the masculine plural suffix ون to create the plural form of كافِر
Added ال ('the') at the start to make it specific disbelievers rather than just any disbelievers
This is an emphatic particle that strengthens the statement that follows it. In this ayah, it emphasizes the disbelievers' claim 'this is clearly a magician.'
The base attention-drawing particle that starts many demonstrative pronouns
The alif is replaced with a small alif above the ه to shorten the word while keeping the same meaning
Adding ذَ changes it from just getting attention to pointing at something nearby
The final alif shows this is for masculine singular objects
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed the word to show the person who does the action. This word is describing what the disbelievers called 'this' (هذا) in the verse.
Added لَ at start to emphasize 'surely/indeed'
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: one who makes clear/that which makes clear
one who makes very clear (describing هٰذَا 'this' in the ayah)
steps.n: The shadda adds emphasis and this word is describing 'this' (هٰذَا) in the verse

steps.m: a clear one/one who makes very clear (describing هٰذَا 'this' in the ayah)
steps.n: Adds tanween dammah to make the word indefinite
This is a special word that adds emphasis to what follows. It's like adding an exclamation mark or saying 'Listen!' before important news. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is truly your Lord.

steps.m: Lord (the one who nurtures and cares for all)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
The fatha at the end shows this word is being described by إِنَّ (indeed/verily) which comes before it
Added كُمُ which means 'your' when talking to many people, showing this Lord belongs to all those being addressed
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one and only deity worthy of worship
Added damma to show this is the doer of the action (managing/arranging affairs) in this verse. The alif also changed to a wasl form since it connects to the previous word.

steps.m: who, which, that - used to describe something that was just mentioned
steps.n: This is a special word that connects descriptions to what they're describing, like saying 'the person WHO did something'. The ل of the definite article assimilates with the following ذ, creating a shadda.

steps.m: to create or bring something new into existence

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this is something Allah did in the past. This word describes what Allah did with the heavens and earth.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a word meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'the high place above us'
Makes the word plural to show multiple heavens
Makes it specific by adding 'the'
The word changed to show it is the object (thing being created) in relation to the verb خَلَقَ (created)
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make the word for 'earth'
Added ال to make it specific
Added و at beginning to connect it with previous word

steps.m: and the earth (as object of خَلَقَ/created)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show it's the object being created

steps.m: in (connects سِتَّةِ with what comes before it)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows location or time. Here it connects the word سِتَّةِ (six) to what comes before it, showing that something happened 'in' six days.
Adding the vowel mark makes it a proper number word
Adding ة makes it the standard feminine form of the number six
Added kasra at the end to show connection to the word أَيَّامٍ (days) that follows it
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make the basic singular word for 'day'
Changes the word to show multiple days by adding أَ at the start and doubling the middle letter
Added the -in sound at the end to show this word belongs to or comes after the number 'six'

steps.m: then (connects this part about Allah rising above the throne to the previous part about creation)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time or importance gap between them. Here it connects Allah's creation of the heavens and earth with Him rising above the throne.

steps.m: to be level, balanced, or complete

steps.n: Changed from root letters to past tense verb by adding vowel marks (fatha) to mean 'he made level'
Added است at the beginning to show doing something completely or taking full control of something
The alif at the start becomes a special connecting alif (wasl), and a small alif is added at the end. This word connects to 'the Throne' mentioned right after it.
This is a basic preposition showing position or relationship. In this ayah, it shows Allah's position of authority over the throne.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create a noun meaning the thing that is elevated/raised up
Added 'the' at the beginning to make it specific and kasra at the end to show it's being acted upon by the word عَلَى (upon) that comes before it in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or repeatedly
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. This word is connected to الْأَمْرَ (the matter/affair) which it manages.
Present tense verb meaning 'to manage/arrange' - the subject is Allah
Added vowel marks to connect letters and change from verb to noun meaning
Added ال to make it definite (the matter) and َ at the end to show it's receiving the action of يُدَبِّرُ (He arranges/manages)
This is a negative particle that negates the word شَفِيعٍ (intercessor) in this ayah, meaning 'there is no intercessor'

steps.m: from (connecting شَفِيعٍ with what comes after it)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. Here it connects the word شَفِيعٍ (intercessor) with what follows it.

steps.m: to intercede or mediate between two parties

steps.n: Added short vowels (fat-ha) to make it mean 'he interceded' in the past
Changed the pattern to show someone who does the interceding

steps.m: any intercessor (after مَا مِن - there is not)
steps.n: Added the two dots below (tanween) to show 'any intercessor' - this happens after 'there is not' (ما من) to make it more general
Basic particle used for exception. In this ayah, it shows that there are no intercessors except after Allah's permission.

steps.m: from/of (shows where something starts or what it's made of)
steps.n: Same as previous مِن but with a small mark below

The small mark (called a 'small noon') is added because this word comes before another word starting with ب. It helps make the pronunciation smoother, like how in English we say 'an apple' instead of 'a apple'.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'after'
The kasra shows this word is possessed by the word إِذْنِهِۦ (His permission), forming the phrase 'after His permission'

steps.m: permission (the act or state of permitting)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (to permit) into a noun meaning permission

steps.m: His permission (possessed by Him, coming after بَعْدِ)
steps.n: Added 'his' to show ownership. The ِ before هِ shows this word belongs to what comes before it (بَعْدِ meaning 'after').
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
Adding alif above changes from pointing to something near to something far
Adding ل with kasra (لِ) is a connector that helps join the demonstrative to what comes after it
Adding كُمُ shows this is being pointed out to multiple people (you all)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite and the shadda emphasizes the uniqueness. The ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.

steps.m: one who nurtures completely (subject of إِنَّ)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes this a noun for someone who does the action perfectly and completely

steps.m: your (all of you) Lord and Nurturer (subject of إِنَّ)
steps.n: The suffix كم shows this refers to all the people being spoken to. This word is the subject (doer) that إِنَّ is talking about.
Changes root 'worship' into a command by adding sukun (ْ) and damma (ُ) marks
Added 'alif' at start to make command stronger/more emphatic
Changes from commanding one person to commanding a group by adding 'و' at the end
Adds 'so/therefore' meaning
Adds 'Him' (هُ) at the end to show who to worship, and changes the beginning slightly
Question word that expects the answer 'yes' and shows surprise or criticism
Adds فَ to show this question follows from what was just mentioned about Allah being your Lord

steps.m: then will you not? (connected to تَذَكَّرُونَ - remember)
steps.n: Adds لا to make this a negative question that encourages remembrance. This combination is asking 'After knowing Allah is your Lord, shouldn't you remember/take heed?'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make it a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows doing the action more strongly or repeatedly
Adding تَ at the beginning changes 'reminding others' to 'reminding yourself/taking a reminder'
Adding ُ shows this is happening now (present tense)
Adding ون changes it from 'he' to 'you all' and shows multiple people are doing the remembering/taking heed
This is a basic preposition indicating direction or destination. Even though it's a particle without a clear root, we start with its basic letters.
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.
The هِ at the end shows it's talking about him (Allah in this context). This word connects to مَرْجِعُكُمْ (your return) showing that the return will be to Him.
This is the basic concept of returning or going back
Adding مَ at the start and the vowel marks changes this from the action of returning to the place or time where returning happens
Adding ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: your (plural) place/time of return (subject of إِلَيْهِ)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this is your (plural) place of return. This word is connected to إِلَيْهِ (to Him) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from a verb (gathered) to a noun meaning 'all together' by changing the middle letter to ي

steps.m: all together (describing how إِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ happens)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this describes how the return happens - everyone returns together
Changed from an action (to promise) into a thing (a promise)
Added a fatha at the end to show this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes right after it, making it mean 'Allah's promise'
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and greatness of Allah
Added kasra at the end to show that this word is possessed by وَعْدَ (promise) in the phrase 'promise of Allah'
Adding fatha (َ) shows this is a past tense verb

steps.m: truly/certainly (describing how Allah's promise is real)
steps.n: Adding ًا at the end changes it from just 'truth' to 'truly' - like changing 'quick' to 'quickly' in English
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it more certain, like adding 'definitely' in English

steps.m: indeed he/it (referring to Allah's promise mentioned before)
steps.n: Added هُ which means 'he/it' to show what is being emphasized. The small mark ۥ after هُ shows the sound is stretched slightly when reading.

steps.m: to begin or start something new

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Added يَ at start to show present/future tense, changed vowels to show ongoing action
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding وا at the end (written as ؤُا)
Root خ-ل-ق (create) becomes خَلْقَ (creation) - a noun showing what is being created. The fatha at the end shows it's connected to the verb 'begins'
Added 'the' at the beginning to make it specific
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects 'He begins creation' with 'then He repeats it.'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'returned' to present tense 'returns' and made it mean 'making something return' instead of just 'returning'

steps.m: he returns/brings it back (referring to الْخَلْقَ/the creation)
steps.n: The هُ shows what is being returned - referring back to الْخَلْقَ (the creation) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: he gives/will give what is deserved
steps.n: Adding يَ at the start and changing the vowels shows this happens in the present/future

steps.m: so that he may give what is deserved (purpose of يُعِيدُهُ - He repeats it)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose and َ at the end because it's affected by another word. This explains why Allah repeats creation.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to make a relative pronoun
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people or things
Adding ال makes it mean 'those who' instead of just 'who'
Adding the proper pronunciation marks and connecting it to the next word ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed/had faith). This word is describing the people who believed.
Adding alif makes it mean 'to give trust/security to someone else'
Added وا with a small alif (۟) on top to show that a group of people did this action
Connected the root letters with vowels to create the basic past tense verb
The suffix وا shows this is a group of people (they) doing the action
Added conjunction 'and' to connect this word with ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed) in the ayah

steps.m: to make something right or good

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a word describing someone who has this quality
Makes it plural feminine form, used for non-human things like deeds or actions
Adds 'the' (ال) to the word, making it specific instead of general
Changes the ending to show this word is being acted upon by the verb عَمِلُوا (they did/performed)
Root letters were given vowels to make a noun meaning 'justice' instead of the action 'being just'
Added ال to make it specific and ِ at the end because it follows the word لِيَجْزِىَ (to reward) in the ayah

steps.m: with justice (connected to لِيَجْزِىَ - to reward)
steps.n: Added بِ to show how the reward is given - with justice. This connects to the word لِيَجْزِىَ (to reward) in the ayah, showing that Allah rewards with justice.
Added ال to make it specific and shadda to strengthen the meaning
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one)

steps.m: and those who (connected to ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous group mentioned (those who believed)

steps.m: to reject, deny, or cover up truth

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This is the basic past tense verb form.
Added وا at the end and changed the last vowel to ُ to show multiple people did this action. This word is connected to لَهُمْ (for them) in the next ayah which refers to these disbelievers.
This is a preposition showing who the drink is for. It connects to the word شَرَابٌ (drink) in this ayah.

steps.m: for them (referring to الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ - those who disbelieved)
steps.n: هم means 'them' (masculine plural) - here referring back to those who disbelieved (الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to change from action to the thing itself

steps.m: a drink (subject of لَهُمْ - they have)
steps.n: Added double damma to show this is the subject of لَهُمْ (they have) in this ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or what it's made of

Added emphasis mark (shadda) because it comes after another word ending in ن. This is just for pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning. It connects to the word شَرَابٌ (drink) to show what the drink is made from.
Adds ي between second and third root letters to show something has a very strong quality of heat
Shows this is what the drink is made from, connected to مِّنْ (from) in the ayah
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the painful result of punishment. This word connects to لَهُمْ earlier in the ayah to show what the disbelievers will get.
The tanween (ٌ) shows this is any punishment, not a specific one. It's in the nominative case because it's describing what the disbelievers will get.

steps.m: and a punishment (connected to شَرَابٌ - 'a drink')
steps.n: The وَ (and) connects this punishment to the شَرَابٌ (drink) mentioned before it, showing the disbelievers will get both things.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added ي between second and third letters to show this describes something that causes pain
Added double dammah to make the word indefinite (like saying 'a painful thing' instead of 'the painful thing')

steps.m: because of what, due to what
steps.n: This is a combination of two particles: بِ (meaning 'with/by') and مَا (meaning 'what')
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the past tense
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' and shows this happened in the past
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The يَ at the start shows this is happening now
Changes from 'he rejects' to 'they reject' by adding ون at the end

steps.m: he (the one who made the sun and moon)
steps.n: This is a standalone pronoun that refers back to Allah in this context, as He is the one being described as creating the sun and moon
This is the basic form of the word that connects a description to what's being described
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it specific and show it's describing something particular (in this case, هُوَ - He)
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word describes what 'He (Allah) who' did in this ayah.
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it a noun. This word is what Allah جَعَلَ (made/placed) in this ayah.
Adding 'ال' makes it specific - 'the sun' instead of just any sun
The sun gets a small 'a' sound (fatha) at the end because it's being acted upon in the sentence
Changed و to ي and added vowels to make it a noun meaning 'light' instead of the action of giving light
Added tanween (two damma marks) to show this is indefinite
The word already had tanween - this is just a spelling variation showing the same word 'light' as the object (what Allah made the sun into)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Adding ال makes it definite. The َ at the end shows it's the object of جَعَلَ (made/placed)
Adding و connects this to ٱلشَّمْسَ (the sun) which was mentioned before it
Root meaning 'light/shine' becomes the noun 'light' with damma and sukun
Added tanwin alif to show this is what Allah made the moon to be. This word describes what the moon was made into, coming after the verb جَعَلَ (He made/appointed).
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Doubling the middle letter makes the meaning more precise and intensive, like saying 'determined very precisely' instead of just 'determined'

steps.m: and he determined precisely (connected to جَعَلَ - made)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) to connect this action to جَعَلَ (made) earlier in the verse, showing these are related actions

steps.m: and He determined it precisely (referring to the moon's phases)
steps.n: Added هُ (it) to show what is being determined - refers back to the moon (القمر) mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to show where something happens
The singular word becomes plural by adding an alif after the first letter and changing the middle of the word - this is a common plural pattern

steps.m: phases (of the moon that Allah set)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by قَدَّرَ (He determined/set) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ
Changed from singular 'you' to plural 'you all' by adding وا

steps.m: so that you all may know
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show purpose - this explains why Allah made the sun and moon as mentioned in the ayah
Forms a noun meaning 'number' that is the object of 'so that you may know' in this ayah
Added vowel marks and changed و to ة to make it a noun meaning one year
Changed vowels and added ين to make it plural. This word follows عَدَدَ (number) in the ayah, showing how many years.
Added ٱل to specify these particular years and doubled the س with shadda
Added final vowel mark to show its grammatical role after عَدَدَ (number)
Adding vowel marks to specify the noun pattern
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the vowels and added alif to make it a noun meaning the process or result of calculating
Added ال to make it specific
Added و to connect this word with the previous words in the verse
Added fatha at the end because this word is the object of the verb لِتَعْلَمُوا (so that you may know)

This is a negative particle that comes before the verb خَلَقَ (created) to negate it, making the meaning 'did not create'. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase 'Allah did not create that except in truth.'

steps.m: to create or make something from nothing

Added ال to specify this is THE one true deity, and the hamza was dropped because this is a special word. The ل is given emphasis with a shadda to show the uniqueness and importance of Allah.

steps.m: Allah (the subject of خَلَقَ - He created)
steps.n: Added ُ to show Allah is the one doing the action (creating) in this verse. The alif at the start is written with a special symbol (ٱ) because it connects with the word before it.
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby

steps.n: The كَ at the end makes it specifically point to something mentioned before in the ayah - in this case, pointing back to how Allah created the sun and moon and their measurements

Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this word is connected to something else
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific

steps.m: with/by the truth (describing how Allah created this)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show this describes how Allah created the sun and moon system - He did it with/by truth and wisdom

steps.m: to separate or divide into distinct parts

Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding damma at end. This word refers to how Allah explains His signs in detail.

steps.m: and the earth (in relation to Allah's signs)

steps.m: to show, make clear, be evident

Root letters turn into a present tense verb by adding vowels - like adding 'he' to 'protect' in English

steps.m: our meeting (object of يَرْجُونَ - they hope for)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show this is our meeting. In this ayah, this word is what they don't hope for - it's the object of يَرْجُونَ (they hope for).
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that the people are deep within their state of transgression.

steps.m: to cross boundaries or rebel against limits

steps.n: Changed the verb 'to rebel' into the noun 'rebellion' by adding marks (damma and sukun)
Added ٰن at the end to show this is intense rebellion
Added kasra at the end to show this word is affected by a preposition
Added هِمْ at the end to show this belongs to them

steps.m: to be confused and wander without direction

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show 'he' is doing the action now, changed vowels to show he is still wandering (not finished)
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is a time word that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects the time when harm touches someone with their response of calling upon Allah.

steps.m: and when (introduces the condition of مَسَّ/touches)
steps.n: The و connects this condition with what came before it in the Quran, showing this is part of a series of examples or situations being described
Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
The doubled س shows the touch/effect was stronger or repeated

steps.m: to be close and friendly with others

steps.n: Root meaning 'friendly/social' becomes a noun meaning all humans as a social species
Adds ان to create the singular noun meaning 'a human'
Makes the word specific by adding 'the'
Shows this word is being acted upon by the verb مَسَّ (touches/affects)
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show the harm was intense or happened multiple times
Changed the vowel mark to show it's the thing being affected
Added 'the' at the beginning to show it's talking about specific harm mentioned in the ayah that affects mankind

steps.m: to call out to, to pray to, to invite

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas. The و at the end changes to ا because it comes after a fatha - this is a common pattern in Arabic verbs ending in و

steps.m: he called upon us (when affliction touched him)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show who is being called upon (us). In this ayah, it refers to the person calling upon Allah when hardship affects them.
Root letters got vowels (َ and ْ) to make the noun meaning 'side'
Added لِ at beginning to show direction or purpose
Added kasra at end because it follows the preposition لِ
Added هِۦٓ at end to show it's 'his side'. This describes how the person was positioned while calling (دَعَانَا)
This is a basic connecting word that shows alternatives or choices between sitting and standing. Not derived from a root.
Added fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
This form shows the current state of sitting. The word describes how someone is calling upon Allah in this ayah.
The ًا ending shows this word is describing the state of the person calling upon Allah (from دَعَانَا earlier in the ayah)
This is a conjunction particle that gives a choice between options. In this ayah, it connects different positions someone might take when making dua: lying down (لِجَنۢبِهِ) or sitting (قَاعِدًا).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb into a word describing someone/something in this state. The و changes to ا and ئ due to Arabic spelling rules.
Added tanween alif to show this describes how someone is doing something (in this case, how they are praying). The ا becomes آ for proper Arabic spelling.
This is a word that means 'when' and connects two events in time. In this ayah, it connects 'when' to 'we removed' (كَشَفْنَا).

steps.m: then when (connects to كَشَفْنَا - we removed)
steps.n: The فَ at the beginning shows this happens as a result of something before it. Here it shows what happened after the person called upon Allah.
Added vowel marks (fatha) to make it 'he removed' in the past tense

steps.m: we removed/uncovered (from him ضُرَّهُ his hardship)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This connects to ضُرَّهُ (his hardship) which comes after this word in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows movement away from something or someone
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added هُ to show this is happening to 'him'. In this ayah, it refers back to الضُّرُّ (the harm) that was removed from the person.
Added vowel marks (fatha) and doubled the middle letter to make it mean 'he harmed'
Changed the first vowel to damma (ُ) to show something was done to the subject rather than them doing it
Added pronoun هُ to show the harm belongs to him. This word is what was removed in the phrase 'we removed his harm'
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters

steps.m: he passed by (as part of كَأَن لَّمْ يَدْعُنَآ)
steps.n: Combined the doubled ر into one letter with shadda. This word is part of a phrase meaning 'as if he had not called upon Us'
Basic comparison particle that shows similarity
Adding أَن changes the meaning from simple comparison to a hypothetical comparison, making it mean 'as if' instead of just 'like'
Basic negative particle that makes verbs refer to the past and negates them, like saying 'did not' in English
The ل gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after a word ending in a vowel, making the word stronger and more emphatic when saying 'not'
The root letters got vowels (fathas) to make it past tense 'he called'. The last letter و turned into ا because it's at the end.
Added يَ at start to show present tense. Changed middle vowel to sukun and last vowel to damma. The و stays because it's a weak letter.
Added نَآ at the end to show 'us' as the object being called
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. It's one of the most common Arabic prepositions.

Added an alif khanjariyah (small alif) above the ى. This is just a writing style and doesn't change the meaning. In this ayah, it connects to the word ضُرٍّ (harm/hardship) to show what the person is going towards.
This root carries the basic meaning of causing harm or difficulty
Changed from verb 'to harm' into a noun meaning 'harm' by adding damma and combining the two ر letters with shadda

steps.m: any harm/hardship (being affected by مَسَّ - touched)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is any general harm, and it's being affected by the word 'touched' in the verse
The middle letter is doubled (has shadda) because two identical letters come together
Connected the root letters and doubled the middle letter with shadda because two identical letters come together. Added fatha to show past tense.
Added هُ at the end to show 'him' as the object being touched
Added a shadda (doubling mark) on the س to make the touch more emphatic/intense
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'this'. It doesn't come from a root since it's a demonstrative word.
Adding ٰلِ changes the word from 'this' (near) to 'that' (far)
Adding كَ makes it mean 'that specific thing' instead of just 'that'
Adding كَ at the beginning changes the meaning to 'like that' or 'similarly'
This root carries the basic meaning of making something beautiful or adorning something
Connected the letters and added shadda (double letter) on ي to show strong action
Changed the vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it. The ُ (damma) at the start and ِ (kasra) in the middle show this is passive
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does this action habitually
Added plural ending to show multiple people
Added 'the' (ال) to make it specific

steps.m: for the ones who exceed limits
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose or direction

steps.m: what (referring to يَعْمَلُونَ - they used to do)
steps.n: This is a relative pronoun that connects to the verb يَعْمَلُونَ (they used to do) to show what they were doing. In this context, it's used to refer back to their actions.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
The و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action in the past
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what comes before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing the vowels makes the verb causative - instead of something perishing by itself, someone is causing the destruction
Adding نَا at the end changes the subject to 'we' (first person plural past tense)
Changed the vowels to make it plural, showing many generations
Added ٱلْ to make it specific generations and َ to show it's being acted upon by the verb 'We destroyed' (أَهْلَكْنَا)
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. Here it connects with قَبْلِكُمْ (before you) to mean 'from before you'
This root carries the basic meaning of facing or coming before something
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to create the noun meaning 'before'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to what comes after it
Added كُمْ to show this means 'before you (plural)'
This is the base particle that shows negation in the past
When we add the extra letters to لَمْ (not), it becomes لَمَّا (when) - which shows one action happened before another. Here it connects to 'they wronged' in the ayah.

steps.m: to do wrong, to act unjustly

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense. This word is connected to لَمَّا (when) in the ayah.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. The small و above (۟) helps show pronunciation.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle letter ي changes to ا because it's a weak letter. This makes the basic past tense form.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Adding ت at the end shows this is about a female subject
Adding هم shows who received the action - 'them' (a group of males)
Adding ت shows the subject is feminine - 'she'
The pattern فَعُول (with و) turns a verb into a noun meaning 'someone who does this action a lot' - so رَسُول means someone who delivers many messages
Changes to a special plural form that's common for important roles or titles
Added a mark to show this word is doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: their messengers (who came to them with جَآءَتْهُم)
steps.n: Added 'their' to show these were the messengers sent to them, connecting to the verb 'came to them' (جَآءَتْهُم) in the verse
Added shadda to make the meaning stronger
The feminine plural ending shows multiple clear proofs or evidences
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Adding preposition بِ shows these proofs were used as tools or means
The word has بِ (with/by) attached at the start and means 'with clear proofs' - the ending shows it's being acted upon
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation

steps.m: and not (they were not going to believe)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before. This word connects to كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟ (they were going to believe) to say they were not going to believe.

steps.m: to be or exist (in a state or condition)

steps.n: Added fatha marks to show it happened in the past. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle.
Adding وا at the end changes 'he' to 'they' (masculine plural). This word connects to لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟ (so that they would believe) in the ayah.
Changes from basic root to past tense 'he' form of verb
Adding long alif at start makes it mean 'to have faith' instead of just 'to trust'
Changes past tense to present tense
Adds 'for/in order to' meaning to the verb
Adds plural masculine ending to show multiple people
This is the base demonstrative word meaning 'that' which points to something far away
Added كَ at the end to show the thing being pointed to is far away

steps.m: like that (referring to how We destroyed previous nations)
steps.n: Added كَ at beginning to mean 'like/similar to'. This word connects to نَجْزِى (We recompense) in the next part, showing that the punishment will be similar to what happened before.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action. Changed vowels to present tense pattern. The word relates to الْمُجْرِمِينَ (the criminals) who are receiving the reward/punishment.

steps.m: people who stand together (acted upon by نَجْزِى - we recompense)
steps.n: Changed from an action to a group of people who do that action together. This word is receiving the action of نَجْزِى (we recompense).

steps.m: the people (acted upon by نَجْزِى - we recompense)
steps.n: Added 'ال' (the) at start and fatha at end to show this is 'the people' receiving the action
Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who does the action
Added ين to show it's talking about many people. This word describes القوم (the people) in this ayah.
Added definite article
This is a basic connecting word that shows sequence of events. It is a primitive particle that has no root.

steps.m: then/next (connects to جَعَلْنٰكُمْ - We made you)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda with fatha (َّ) to complete the word. In this ayah, it connects two actions, showing that 'We made you' happened after something mentioned before.

steps.m: to make something into something else

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to connect the root letters together to make a past tense verb
Added نٰ at the end to show that 'we' are doing the action. In the Quran, when Allah uses 'we', it shows His majesty and power, not multiple gods.

steps.m: we made you all (into خَلٰٓئِفَ - successors)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show who is being made into something. This connects to the next word خَلٰٓئِفَ which tells what they are made into - successors/representatives on earth.

steps.m: to come after or replace something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to show a person who does the action, and added ة to make it a specific instance

steps.m: successors (plural) (object of جَعَلْنٰكُمْ - we made you)
steps.n: Changed to broken plural form to show many successors. This word is what 'we made you' into according to جَعَلْنٰكُمْ in the ayah.
This is a basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it shows the location where Allah made the people successors - in the earth.
The ي changes to ى (alif maqsurah) because it comes at the end of a word pause in recitation
Root letters got short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to make a proper word meaning 'earth'
Added 'the' at the beginning and 'i' sound at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in)
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it connects to the word بَعْدِهِمْ (after them) to show the starting point in time.
Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end because it's followed by ب. This helps make the pronunciation smoother when connecting words.

steps.m: to be after, follow, come later

steps.n: Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to the root letters to make the word mean 'after'

steps.m: after (ready to be owned by something)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show this word is going to be owned by something
Added هِمْ at the end to show who owns or possesses this 'after'. This whole phrase مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ means 'from after them' in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense with 'we' as the subject by adding نَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: so that we may look (purpose for جَعَلْنٰكُمْ - We made you)
steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose and changed final vowel to fatha to show this is a dependent verb explaining why Allah made them successors
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding vowel marks to show how to pronounce the word correctly
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ون makes it plural for a group

steps.m: when/whenever (introduces the condition of تُتْلَىٰ - being recited)
steps.n: This is a time word that shows when something happens. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when verses are recited.

steps.m: and when (introduces the condition of تُتْلَىٰ - being recited)
steps.n: The و at the beginning connects this to what was said before. It's like saying 'and' in English to connect two ideas.
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels and turning و into ا at the end
Changed to passive present tense, showing something is being recited rather than someone reciting

steps.m: it (our clear signs) is being recited (to them)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ى) at the end to show the word stretches out when read, but the meaning stays the same - it's still about reciting
This is a preposition showing something happening on or upon something else. The root letters combine to form the basic preposition.
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns at the end

steps.m: upon them (referring to those who don't hope for meeting Us)
steps.n: Added هِمْ at the end which means 'them' when referring to a group of men. In this ayah, it refers to those who don't hope for meeting Allah.
Forms the singular noun meaning 'sign' or 'verse' by adding long alif after the first root letter
Makes the word plural by adding ات at the end
Adds 'our' and shows this is the subject being recited (تُتْلَىٰ)
Added shadda to middle letter to show intensity of clarity
Makes the word feminine, changes from adjective to noun meaning 'a clear proof'
Makes the word plural - multiple clear proofs
Changed ending to show it describes 'signs/proofs' (آيَاتُنَا) - means they are very clear and obvious
Added short vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he said'

steps.m: he said (responding to those who don't believe in meeting Allah)
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This is a common change in Arabic for words with و in the middle.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to describe someone or something
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific - now it means 'those specific people who' instead of just any 'who'
Added final vowel mark and changed spelling slightly (ا to ٱ and added ّ) to match Quranic writing style. This word is the subject (doer) of قَالَ (said) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَرْجُونَ (they hope) that comes after it, making it mean 'they do not hope'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to damma. The final و returns because it's in the middle of the word now.

steps.m: they hope/expect (referring to the disbelievers who don't hope for meeting Allah)
steps.n: Added نَ at the end to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers mentioned earlier who don't hope to meet Allah.
Adding vowels turns the root into a proper past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'meeting'. The ي changes to ء and we add ا after ق
Added نَا at the end to show it belongs to us
The َ (fatha) at the end shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Connected the root letters together
Added hamza between first two letters to show doing action to oneself
Changed from basic verb 'to come' into a command 'Come!' by dropping the last letter and adding special marks. Like telling someone 'Come here!'
The root 'read/recite' becomes a noun meaning 'that which is recited' (Quran). The two dots under show it follows a preposition.

steps.m: with a Quran (object of ٱئْتِ - bring)
steps.n: Added بِ meaning 'with'. This word is what they're asking to be brought in the phrase 'bring a different Quran'

steps.m: to be different from, to change

steps.n: Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by قُرْءَانٍ (Quran) in the phrase 'a Quran other than this'
This is a demonstrative pronoun that points to something nearby. It doesn't come from a root.
Added the small alif (ٰ) above ه and fatha on ذ to help with pronunciation
Added the long alif (آ) at the end to emphasize we're pointing to something specific being discussed

steps.m: or (connecting 'bring' with 'change' in بِقُرْءَانٍ غَيْرِ هٰذَآ أَوْ بَدِّلْهُ)
steps.n: This is a basic conjunction particle that gives choices between things. Here it connects two requests from the disbelievers - either bring a different Quran OR change this one.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
The shadda (double letter) makes it a command and makes the meaning stronger - it's a forceful command to change something completely

steps.m: change it! (as a strong command to change the Quran)
steps.n: Adding هُ shows what should be changed. In this ayah, the disbelievers are demanding to change the Quran itself.
Changed from root to command form by adding damma (ُ) - like telling someone 'Say!'
The و (weak letter) drops out and is replaced with a sukoon (ْ) to make the word easier to say. This word is a direct command from Allah to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ.
In this ayah, مَا is being used as an interrogative particle meaning 'what'. It's asking about what is possible/appropriate, connecting to the verb يَكُونُ (it is/becomes) that follows it.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense form
The يَ at the beginning shows present/future tense. The ُ (damma) vowels are used because this is a hollow verb (has و in middle)
This is a basic preposition that shows direction, purpose, or possession
Added ى at the end to show this relates to "me". The ي is written as ى when at the end of a word.
Added the elongation mark (ٓ) which shows the vowel is pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: that (connects to أُبَدِّلَهُۥ - I change it)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that joins two parts of a sentence. Here it connects to the next word أُبَدِّلَهُۥ (I change it) to show what is being talked about.

steps.m: to exchange or replace something with something else
to change or replace something completely
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows doing something thoroughly or completely
The أُ at the start shows something is being done to the subject rather than the subject doing it

steps.m: I change/replace it (referring to the Quran mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Adding هُ at the end shows what is being changed - in this case referring back to the Quran mentioned before

steps.m: from (showing that changing the Quran would come from the Prophet's own self)
steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from or originates. In this ayah, it connects with تِلْقَآئِ (own accord) to show the disbelievers are asking for changes that would come from Muhammad's personal choice rather than divine revelation.

steps.m: to meet or come face to face with something

steps.n: Adding vowel marks changes this into a complete past tense verb meaning 'he met'
Added تِ at beginning and changed vowels to create noun showing direction or manner

steps.m: of one's own accord (in relation to نَفْسِى)
steps.n: Changed ending to show possession/relation. This word connects to نَفْسِى (myself) in the ayah to mean 'of my own accord'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by something

steps.m: my self/soul (in تِلْقَاءِ نَفْسِىٓ - of my own accord)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to show this belongs to 'me'. The long vowel mark (ٓ) shows this is at the end of a phrase.
This is a conditional word that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'if I disobey' to the consequence 'I fear punishment'
Basic meaning of following or coming after something
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make basic past tense verb
Added shadda to emphasize the action
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'to cause someone to follow'
Changed ending to 'u' sound to show 'I' am doing the action now (present tense)

steps.m: except (introduces what is excluded from يُوحَىٰٓ)
steps.n: This is a particle that excludes what comes after it from what was mentioned before. In this ayah, it shows that the Prophet follows only what is revealed to him, excluding everything else. The shadda (ّ) and long alif (ا) are essential parts of the word's structure.
This مَا is being used as a negative particle that negates the verb يَكُونُ (to be) that comes after it. Together they mean 'it is not' in this ayah.
Basic meaning of divine revelation or inspiration - specifically used for divine communication
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive present tense by adding يُ at start. The final ي becomes ى due to being a weak letter at the end.
Added small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's a long vowel sound
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination
Added the pronoun 'me' (ي) which combines with the final ى and gets a shadda. The fatha is added to show the sound clearly.
This is the basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to statements
Added ي at the end to show this refers to 'I'. The ن gets a shadda because it merges with the following ي
The ي is written as ى with an alif (ٓ) above it when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle letter و changes to ا which is common in Arabic for this type of word.

steps.m: I fear (in response to قُلْ - say)
steps.n: Changed to present tense first person (I). This word is what the speaker is commanded to say in response to قُلْ (say).
This is a helper word that shows the speaker fears something that depends on a condition - in this case, fearing punishment if he disobeys. It connects أَخَافُ (I fear) with عَصَيْتُ (I disobey).

steps.m: to disobey or go against authority

steps.n: Adding the vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb
Changed from 'he disobeyed' to 'I disobeyed' by adding تُ at the end and changing the last letter ى to يْ

steps.m: to care for, nurture, raise up
one who nurtures and cares for others completely
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter and adding fatha shows this describes someone who does this action perfectly

steps.m: one who nurtures and cares (connected to something)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word will be connected to something that comes after it

steps.m: my Lord (subject of عَصَيْتُ - if I disobey)
steps.n: Adding ى at the end shows this is my Lord. In this ayah, it's connected to the verb عَصَيْتُ (if I disobey).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past-tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to make it into a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is what is being feared (object of أَخَافُ - I fear) in this ayah.

steps.m: to spend or pass a day

steps.n: Root letters y-w-m form the simple noun 'day' with fatha on ya and sukoon on waw. Like saying 'book' from 'to write'
Added kasra tanween to show this word is being described by عَظِيمٍ (great). Together they mean 'a great day'.
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing something becoming great
Changed the verb into an adjective by adding ي between second and third letters and changing the vowels

steps.m: great, mighty (describing يَوْمٍ - day, showing it follows another word)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra at the end to show this adjective follows the word it describes (يَوْمٍ - day)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense to command form by dropping last vowel and changing middle letter to 'u' sound
The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter. This is common in command forms of hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle).
Basic conditional particle used for hypothetical situations that are unlikely or impossible
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning and sukun at the end to show word ending. In this ayah, it introduces a hypothetical situation: 'if Allah had willed'
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he wanted' in the past tense
Changed to a hollow verb pattern by adding long alif between first and last root letters
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added 'the' to make it specific/definite
Added damma to show this is the subject
This is a basic word that shows what something is or what happened. Here it points to the Quran that was recited.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the final weak letter و with a sukun for pronunciation before adding suffixes
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action

steps.m: I recited it (referring to the Quran)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show what was recited. The ۥ mark shows this is pronounced with a long vowel.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'. It's one of the most common prepositions in Arabic.
The ى changes to يْ when adding pronouns at the end. This is just a spelling change to help connect the parts.
The كُمْ at the end shows this is addressing multiple people (you all)
Starting with the basic negation particle
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Added an alif with madda (آ) at the end to show emphasis in pronunciation, but this doesn't change the meaning
Basic meaning of knowing or being aware of something
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and changed vowels to make it mean 'cause to know'
Added alif maksura (ى with small alif above) at the end to show this is from a root ending in ي
Added كُم at the end to show the action was done to 'you all'
Basic preposition showing how something is done
Added pronoun هِ to show who/what the preposition refers to
Added small alif (ۦ) after هِ as a pronunciation guide - this doesn't change the meaning
Basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before it. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about Allah's will.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: I remained/stayed (describing how long the Prophet ﷺ lived among his people)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' form to 'I' form by adding تُ at the end. In this ayah, this is the Prophet ﷺ speaking about how long he lived among his people before receiving revelation.
Basic preposition showing location or presence within something
Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This connects to the earlier part where Allah is telling the Prophet to speak to the people about how long he lived among them.
Added damma vowels to create the noun form meaning 'lifetime'
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being acted upon. This word shows how long the staying was in the phrase 'I remained among you a lifetime'.
This is a primary preposition that has no root or derivational forms. It is one of the basic particles in Arabic.
Added a shadda (doubled م) because it comes after the word فَقَدْ which ends with a silent letter. This makes it easier to pronounce but doesn't change the meaning.
This form makes it mean a time or place before something
Added vowels to make it mean 'before' as a time word
Added kasra to show this word is owned by something
Added هِۦٓ to show this means 'before it', referring to the Quran mentioned earlier in the conversation
Starting with the question particle أَ that shows we're asking about something
Adding فَ shows this question follows from what was just said
Adding لا makes this a negative question. The full phrase أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ means 'then will you not use reason?'
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
The تَ at the start shows this is happening now and the person being spoken to is doing the action

steps.m: you (all) use intellect (addressing the disbelievers who should reflect on the Prophet's life)
steps.n: The ون at the end shows we're talking to multiple people. In this ayah, Allah is addressing those who doubt the Prophet ﷺ, telling them to use their reason to reflect on how he lived among them.
This is a question word that asks about people. The sukun (ْ) shows it's complete on its own.

steps.m: so who/then whoever (subject of أَظْلَمُ - more unjust)
steps.n: The فَ connects this to what came before and shows a logical sequence. This word is connected to أَظْلَمُ (more unjust) in the question 'Then who is more unjust?'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes it mean 'more' or 'most' of the base meaning
Basic interrogative word meaning 'who'
Added the preposition مِن before مَن to show source or comparison
When مِن combines with مَن, the two م letters merge with a shadda. This word asks about who did the action افْتَرَىٰ (invented/fabricated).
Added kasra at the end to connect this word with what follows it

steps.m: to separate or create something new

steps.n: Added fathas to make it a past tense verb
Added اِفْتَ pattern to show doing something in a negative way. This word describes what the 'most unjust person' (أَظْلَمُ) does.
Changed spelling of alif to match Uthmani script and added alif maksura
This is the basic form meaning 'high' or 'elevated'
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition meaning 'upon' or 'against'. Here it connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to show what is being acted against.
Changed to the proper name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the word عَلَى (against/upon) in the phrase 'made up lies against Allah'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed the verb 'he lied' into the noun 'a lie'. This word is what was 'invented' (افْتَرَى) against Allah in this ayah.
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options. Here it connects two bad actions: making up lies about Allah OR denying His signs.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: he strongly denied/rejected (the signs/verses of اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes the denial stronger and more emphatic. This word relates to بِـَٔايٰتِهِۦٓ (His signs/verses) in the ayah.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: The root letters were shaped into a noun with a long 'aa' sound (آ) to mean 'something that shows or makes clear' - like a sign
Changed from singular to plural using the feminine plural ending ات
Added بِ at the start to show these signs are being used or acted with
Added kasra to show this word will be connected to something that possesses it

steps.m: with/by His signs or verses (object of كَذَّبَ)
steps.n: Added هِ to show these are Allah's signs. This word is being denied/rejected by the subject of كَذَّبَ (he denied/rejected)
This is an emphasis particle that makes what comes after it stronger, like saying 'truly' or 'certainly' in English
Added the pronoun ه with damma (ُ) to show 'he/it' as the subject being emphasized
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُفْلِحُ (succeed/prosper) negative, changing it from 'succeed' to 'not succeed'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added يُ at start to show present tense, changed vowels to show active voice, and added ُ at end to show it's the subject. This verb is negated by لا before it in the ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters together
Adding مُ at the start and changing the vowels shows who does the action
Adding ُونَ makes it refer to multiple criminals
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific. These criminals are the subject of the verb لَا يُفْلِحُ (will not succeed) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
The ي at the start shows it's happening now (present tense) and 'he' is doing it
Adding ون shows multiple people are doing the action
The و connects this to what comes before, while مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ after it shows they worship other than Allah
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects with دُونِ to mean 'from besides/other than'.

steps.m: to be lower or beneath something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes it to mean 'beneath/lower than'
Added -i sound at the end because it's connected to the next word (Allah) - this shows it belongs together
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
The word 'Allah' got a kasra (i) sound at the end because it comes after عِندَ (meaning 'with/near'). The first letter changed to make it easier to connect with the word before it.
This word refers to things (not people) that are being worshipped. In this ayah, it connects to يَعْبُدُونَ (they worship) and refers to false deities.
This is a negative particle that makes the following verb يَضُرُّهُمْ (harms them) into 'does not harm them'. It is a basic word that doesn't come from a root.
Added the fatha mark (َ) to show how to pronounce the word correctly as 'laa'
Connected the root letters and added shadda to show emphasis on middle letter
Adding يَ at start and changing vowels shows this is happening in present/future
Adding هُمْ shows who is being harmed (them)
Adding fatha to show clear pronunciation of the negation particle
Added the conjunction 'and' to connect this negation to the previous phrase. This connects to the verb يَضُرُّهُمْ (harms them) to say 'and does not harm them'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added هُمْ to show who receives the help/benefit
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Changes from past tense (he said) to present tense (he says) by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Adding ون shows that multiple people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action

steps.m: and they say (about their false gods)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement about people worshipping false gods
The basic pointing word starts with هٰ with a long alif sound to draw attention
Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show emphasis when pointing
Added ؤُ to show we're pointing to multiple things
Added لَآ to complete the plural demonstrative form
Added ءِ at the end to show this word is being used to point to something specific in the sentence

steps.m: to help someone by speaking for them
he helped by speaking for someone
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: someone who helps by speaking for others
steps.n: Added ي between second and third letters to show someone who does this action as their role

steps.m: people who help by speaking for others
steps.n: Changed the word to show many people instead of one person

steps.m: our helpers who speak for us (describes هؤلاء/these)
steps.n: Added نا at the end to show these are our helpers. This word describes هؤلاء (these) in the sentence.
This root carries the basic meaning of being present or near something
Adding the vowels changes this into a preposition. In this ayah, it shows where their claimed intercessors (شُفَعٰٓؤُنَا) are located - with/by Allah.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of دُونِ (besides/other than). Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: say! (a command from Allah to Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by adding damma at start and sukun at end. The middle weak letter و is dropped in this form.
Added shadda to intensify meaning
Changes from past to present tense, adds 'you' as subject
Changed from 'you' (singular) to 'you all' (plural) by adding ون at the end

steps.m: Do you (all) inform (Allah about something He doesn't know)
steps.n: Added questioning alif to create a rhetorical question expressing disapproval
This is a unique word that became the proper name for The One True God in Islam. The ال here is part of the name itself, not just meaning 'the'.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being informed about)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this is the object being informed about in the phrase 'Are you informing Allah?'
Basic preposition meaning 'with'
Added kasra vowel mark for pronunciation
Added ما which means 'what' to create the meaning 'with what'. This word asks about something that will be mentioned in the rest of the sentence.
This is a basic word that means 'not'. In this ayah, it comes before يَعْلَمُ (knows) to say 'does not know'.
Added the fatha mark (َ) to show how to pronounce the first letter correctly.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Adding a 'u' sound at the end shows this is the doer of the action
Basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it's used to talk about what exists (or doesn't exist) in the heavens.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show how to pronounce it as 'fi'
Changed the ي to ى which is a common spelling variation at the end of words

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb 'to rise' into a noun 'sky' by adding a long aa sound and hamza at the end
Added feminine plural ending ات and brought back the و that was changed to ء
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س because it comes after ال
Changed 'al' to a smoother 'al', shortened the long 'aa' sounds to small marks above, and added a small 'i' sound at the end to show connection to another word
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يَنفَعُهُمْ (benefits them) in this ayah
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous one, showing both things are not true
This is a basic preposition that shows location. Here it connects what Allah knows with 'the heavens', showing where the knowledge exists.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make it a noun
Added 'the' at the beginning and 'i' sound at the end because it comes after the word فِي (in)
The root evolved to mean praising or glorifying, as if floating above worldly things
Changes from verb 'to glorify' into noun meaning 'glory/perfection'
Adding ان makes it stronger - like saying 'completely perfect' or 'most glorified'
Adding the fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is being used as a direct object
Added هُ (his) with prolongation sign ۥ to show possession
The root relates to height and elevation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning and changed ending to show doing the action to oneself

steps.m: and He is exalted (above what they associate with Him)
steps.n: Added و (and) at beginning to connect to previous phrase. The capital 'He' refers to Allah, showing respect.
Combined two separate words: عَن (from) and مَا (what)
When عَن comes before مَا, the ن merges into the م, shown by the shadda mark (ّ). This word connects to يُشْرِكُونَ (they associate), showing what they are associating away from.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding يُ at the start and changing the vowels shows this is happening now or in the future

steps.m: they join/associate (others with Allah in worship)
steps.n: Adding ون changes 'he' to 'they' and shows this is about multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to those who associate others with Allah in worship.
This is a special word that negates the verb كَانَ (was) that comes after it in this ayah
The و connects this word to what came before it in the Quran
Added basic past tense vowel pattern. This word tells about the state of النَّاسُ (the people) in the past.
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
people (those who are social by nature)
steps.n: The أ is dropped and ا is added after ن to make a word for people who have this social quality
Adding ٱل makes it specific - we're talking about these particular people. The ن gets a shadda to show emphasis.
The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject (doer) of the verb كَانَ (was) in this ayah
Starting with the basic negative particle لا with its vowel mark
Adding إِ at start and shadda on ل changes this from a negative word to one showing exception. In this ayah, it's telling us about an exception regarding the people (ٱلنَّاسُ).
The alif is written with a special mark (آ) to show it's pronounced longer. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be a source or reference point that others follow

steps.n: Root أمم becomes plural أُمم (nations) by adding the middle damma vowel

steps.m: community, nation (a group united by common source/reference)
steps.n: Changed from plural (nations) to singular (one nation) by removing extra meem and adding taa marboota at end
Added tanween fatha to show this is the object being described in the phrase 'mankind was but one community'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and changed middle vowel to kasra to make it into an adjective
Added ة at the end to make it feminine

steps.m: one (feminine, as a description of أُمَّةً/nation)
steps.n: Added tanween fatha to show this word describes أُمَّةً (nation) in the ayah

steps.m: to differ from or come after something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show people actively disagreeing with each other (form VIII verb pattern)
Added وا to show multiple people did the action

steps.m: so they disagreed (after being one united community)
steps.n: Added فَ at start to show this happened as a result of what came before. The hamza on the alif changes to a waṣla mark because it comes after فَ.

steps.m: if not for (introduces a hypothetical scenario)
steps.n: This is a special word that introduces what would happen if something didn't exist. It's like saying 'If X didn't exist, then Y would happen'

steps.m: and if not for (connects to previous statement)
steps.n: The و at the beginning connects this condition to the previous statement, showing these ideas are related
Adding vowels turns the root into a basic past tense verb meaning 'to speak'
Added ة to show one specific instance of speaking - a word
Added tanween damma to show this is the subject doing the action in سَبَقَتْ (preceded)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Added feminine marker ت with sukun to show this refers to كَلِمَةٌ (word) which is feminine
This is a small word that connects two things by showing where something comes from. Here it shows that the 'word' (كَلِمَةٌ) came 'from your Lord' (رَّبِّكَ)
The kasra (ِ) under the م shows this word is connecting to what comes after it

steps.m: to nurture, care for, and guide with authority

steps.n: Connected the letters and doubled the middle letter to show complete authority and mastery
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something
Added كَ to show this belongs to the person being spoken to. This word follows مِن (from) in the ayah.
The ر gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after the word مِن
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Changed vowels to show that something received the action rather than did it

steps.m: surely it would have been judged (in response to كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to show emphasis and connection to the previous phrase 'if not for a word that preceded'
Adding vowels changes this from a root into a common preposition

steps.m: between them (referring to الناس/the people who differed)
