steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them' (the people mentioned earlier who differed)
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it connects to the verb يَخْتَلِفُونَ (they differ) to show what they differ in.
Added ما which makes this mean 'in what' or 'regarding what'. This combination shows what the people are differing about in the verb يَخْتَلِفُونَ (they differ).
This is a small word that shows something is inside or within something else

steps.m: in it (referring to what they disagree about)
steps.n: Adding هِ shows we're talking about something specific that was mentioned before. Here it refers back to what the people are disagreeing about.

steps.m: to differ from or come after something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added اِ at start and ت after first letter to show mutual action - people disagreeing with each other
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle و stays because it's part of the root.
Adding ون makes it plural for a group of men

steps.m: and they say (about the lack of a sign from Allah)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to the previous statement. This word starts a question from the disbelievers about why Muhammad ﷺ hasn't been given a miraculous sign.
This is the first part of the compound word, meaning 'if' by itself
Adding لا (not) to لو (if) creates a compound particle expressing a wish or desire for something that isn't happening

steps.m: why not (questioning أُنزِلَ - was sent down)
steps.n: The extended alif (آ) adds emphasis to the questioning. Here it's questioning why a sign wasn't sent down.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: was sent down (something that وَيَقُولُونَ 'they say' about)
steps.n: Changed to passive voice by adding أُ at start and changing middle vowel to kasra. This shows the action happened to something rather than something doing the action. In this ayah, this is what 'they say' about.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation of the preposition
Changed the final ى to ي with sukun to prepare for the pronoun attachment
Added the pronoun هِ (him) to show who is being referred to. This word connects to أُنزِلَ (was sent down) to show what was sent down upon him.
The hamza and alif combine to make a long vowel sound, and the weak letter ي helps form the base word

steps.m: a sign/miracle (something that shows truth clearly)
steps.n: Adding ة makes this into a specific instance of something that shows or makes clear
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it 'a' sign rather than 'the' sign
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'. No derivational changes since it's a particle.
Added shadda to show this word connects to رَبِّهِ (his Lord) in this ayah. The shadda appears because the word comes after a pause in speech.

steps.m: to care for and develop something to its full potential

steps.n: Added fatha to first letter and doubled the middle letter to show intensity of meaning
Added kasra at the end to show this is being possessed by someone/something (like 'my lord')
Added هِ at the end to show possession
Added extended alif after هِ to help with pronunciation
Changed to a short command meaning 'say!' - like when a teacher tells you to 'speak!'

steps.m: then say! (responding to وَيَقُولُونَ - and they say)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a response to what was said before (they say... then say...)
This is an emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'indeed' or 'certainly' in English

steps.m: only/just (restricts what يَقُولُونَ 'they say' to one thing)
steps.n: Adding ما changes the meaning from emphasis to restriction. It limits what comes after to just one thing, like saying 'The only thing is...' in English. Here it restricts what 'they say' to one specific thing.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and form a noun meaning 'something unseen'
Added ال to make it specific, referring to the unseen knowledge they are demanding
Added ُ to show this is the subject of 'belongs' in the phrase 'the unseen belongs to Allah'
This is the base word Allah, with shadda on ل to show emphasis
Added لِ at the beginning to show direction/purpose and ِ at the end to connect it to what comes after
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Form VIII (with ا and ت) shows doing the action for oneself - here watching becomes self-directed waiting
Adding وا makes it a command for a group
Adding فَ connects this command to what was said before - 'If they ask for a sign, then tell them to wait'
This is an emphasis word that makes statements stronger, like adding 'truly' or 'indeed' in English
The extra ي (yaa) at the end shows 'I' am saying 'indeed' - like saying 'Indeed I...'
Adding ى at the end makes this about 'me' or 'I'
This is a preposition that indicates being together with something or someone

steps.m: with you all (being addressed in إِنِّى)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. This connects to إِنِّى (indeed I am) in the ayah, where Allah is speaking to multiple people.
Basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'
Added emphasis by doubling the م sound
Added fatha at the end to connect it to the next word ٱلْمُنتَظِرِينَ (those who wait)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
مُ at start shows this is someone/something doing the action
Changed from singular (one person waiting) to plural (many people waiting) by adding ين at the end

steps.m: the ones who are waiting (among مَعَكُم - with you)
steps.n: The word becomes 'the ones who are waiting' by adding ال (the) at the start and changing the ending to match its role in the sentence
This is a conditional particle that shows something happens after something else, like saying 'when this happens, that happens'
The و connects this condition to what came before it
The alif is lengthened at the end to add emphasis and show a pause in reading
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action causative - making someone else do the action

steps.m: we made (someone) taste/experience (النَّاسَ رَحْمَةً - made people taste mercy)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. The و drops out when نَا is added. This word connects to 'people' (النَّاسَ) and 'mercy' (رَحْمَةً) in the ayah.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: The root meaning 'to be social' becomes a word for 'people' by dropping the first letter and changing the vowels. Like how humans are social beings!
Added ال (the) at the start to mean 'the people' instead of just 'people'

steps.m: the people (being given mercy by Allah)
steps.n: Added fatha (small diagonal line) at the end because 'the people' is the object receiving mercy in this ayah
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun instead of an action
Added ة at the end to show we're talking about one specific instance of mercy
Added tanween (ً) to show this mercy is what's being given to people in the verse
Basic preposition that shows where something starts or what it's made of
The م gets a shadda because it's followed by another ن sound in the next word (بَعْدِ). This makes it easier to pronounce. The small ن at the end (ۢ) shows this connection.

steps.m: to be after or follow something

steps.n: Changes from a root meaning 'follow' into a simple word meaning 'after' - like changing 'following' to 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by ضَرَّآءَ (hardship), forming the phrase 'after hardship'
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong harm
Added آء at the end to make it a noun meaning intense hardship

steps.m: severe hardship (object of أَذَقْنَا - we gave them to taste)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb أَذَقْنَا (we gave them to taste)
The middle letter has shadda (ّ) which makes the meaning stronger, showing this is a significant touching/feeling
Changed to feminine because رَحْمَة (mercy) is feminine in Arabic
Added هُمْ to show who was affected by the mercy (referring to ٱلنَّاسَ - the people - mentioned earlier)
This is a time particle that introduces a condition or temporal sequence. In this ayah, it connects to مَسَّتْهُمْ (touched them) to show when something happened.
This is the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose
Added the pronoun هُم which means 'them' (referring to 'the people' mentioned earlier). This word connects to مَكْرٌ (plotting) in the ayah, showing who is doing the plotting.

steps.m: to plan secretly or trick others

steps.n: Adding a shadda on the first letter makes the word stronger, showing this is a serious or important plan
The shadda (double letter) was removed and tanween added, changing it from a connected word to a standalone word meaning 'a plot/plan'
Basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it shows the location where the plotting/planning happens - within Allah's signs.
The alif at the end is lengthened for emphasis when stopping at this word in recitation
Changed the root into a noun for a single sign or verse
Changed from singular to plural form
Added 'our' to show possession and ِ because it comes after the preposition في (in/concerning)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed vowels and removed weak letter و to make command form
Added kasra at end to help connect with next word
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of أَسْرَعُ - is swifter)
steps.n: Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال, doubling the ل with shadda, and adding ُ to show it's doing the action in this sentence. This word is the subject (doer) of أَسْرَعُ (is swifter) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a basic past-tense verb

Added أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end to show this is comparing Allah's speed to others. In this ayah, it's talking about how Allah is quicker in responding with punishment than the disbelievers are in their plotting.
The root letters are connected with vowel marks to form a noun meaning the act itself
The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of being faster (أَسْرَعُ) in the ayah
Adding shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional 'if' to an emphatic 'indeed'. Here it emphasizes that 'Our messengers' (رُسُلَنَا) are definitely recording.
Adding َ_ُو_ to the root makes it mean 'someone who sends messages as their job/role'
The word changed from one messenger to many messengers by changing the middle vowel and removing the long vowel
Added نَا to show the messengers belong to us. These messengers are the subject of يَكْتُبُونَ (they write) in the next ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows it's happening now and he's doing it
Changed from 'he writes' to 'they write' by adding ون at the end - shows multiple people doing the action
This is a relative pronoun that refers to the plotting/planning mentioned in the previous part of the verse. It is the object of the verb يَكْتُبُونَ (they write/record).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
This is a standalone pronoun that refers to Allah in this ayah. It connects to the next word يُسَيِّرُكُمْ (makes you travel) by showing who is doing that action.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to describe or refer back to something
Added ال with shadda to make it definite. This word connects back to هُوَ (He) and introduces a description of Him.
Added a shadda (ّ) to make it mean 'causing something to move' instead of just moving
Added يُ at the beginning to show this is happening in the present/future
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is happening to 'you all'

steps.m: in/within (shows location inside الْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ - the land and sea)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows people traveling within/through the land and sea.
Adding the fatha vowel mark helps show how to pronounce the word
Combining two ر letters into one with shadda (ّ) makes the sound stronger
Adding ال makes it specific, and the kasra shows it follows في (in/on) in this ayah

steps.m: to split or cut through (especially of water)

steps.n: Root letters ب-ح-ر were combined and given vowels to form the noun 'sea' - something that splits/cuts through water
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added و at the beginning to connect it with the previous word (البر - the land)
Added kasra at the end because it comes after في (in/through). The alif in ال becomes ٱ when connected to و.
This is a basic particle that shows a limit or endpoint. It has no root because it's a helper word.
Adding the shadda makes the meaning stronger and clearer
The ى with alif above (ىٰ) and the small alif (ٓ) show this word connects to what comes after it (إِذَا meaning 'when'). Together they mean 'until when'.
Basic time particle that points to a specific moment

steps.m: when (introduces the condition of being in ships)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes it from a simple time marker to a conditional word that introduces a scenario. Here it introduces the condition of 'when you are in ships'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
The middle weak letter و drops out and the vowels change to make it easier to say
Adding تُمْ changes 'he was' to 'you all were' - like adding 'you all' in English
Basic preposition showing location. In this ayah, it connects 'makes you travel' (يُسَيِّرُكُمْ) with 'the land and sea' (ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ), showing where the traveling happens.

steps.m: to move in a circular path
ship (because it moves in circular voyages)
steps.n: The damma and sukun pattern creates a noun for something related to circular movement - in this case, a ship that travels in circular routes
The word 'ship' gets 'the' (ال) added to it and ends with a kasra sound because it comes after 'in'
Added fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb form
Added نَ to show multiple feminine subjects (referring to الْفُلْكِ - the ships)
Added وَ meaning 'and' to connect this action to the previous events. The ships are sailing with the people (بِهِم) mentioned right after this word.
This is a basic preposition that shows how something was done or what was used

steps.m: with/by them (referring to the people on the ships)
steps.n: Adding هِم shows this applies to a group of people. In this ayah, it refers to the people on the ships who thought they were surrounded.

steps.m: wind (subject of جَآءَتْهَا - came to them)
steps.n: The و changes to ي and gets a kasra to make it a noun meaning the thing that does the flowing/moving - wind
Adding tanween (ٍ) makes it indefinite (any wind, not a specific one) and shows it's being acted upon by the preposition بِ

steps.m: with/by a wind (describing how they traveled)
steps.n: Adding بِ shows the wind was the means by which they traveled in the ships
Adding shadda makes the middle letter stronger, which makes the meaning stronger too
Makes the word feminine to match with رِيحٍ (wind) which is feminine in Arabic

steps.m: with a very pleasant/good (describing رِيحٍ - wind)
steps.n: The ٍ shows this word describes رِيحٍ (wind) in the phrase بِرِيحٍ طَيِّبَةٍ (with a pleasant wind)
Root letters got vowels added to make past tense verb 'he became happy'
Adding وا changes the subject from 'he' to 'they' (for a group of men or mixed group). The و gets a damma (ُ) before وا.
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it in the ayah, showing this happiness happened after they were on the ships with good wind
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or used
Added ها which refers back to the feminine noun رِيحٍ (wind) mentioned in the previous part of the verse
The root letters were given vowels and the middle letter ي became ا to make the past tense 'he came'
The alif is made longer to emphasize the action of coming
Added تْ to show the subject is feminine (the wind/ريح)

steps.m: it came to it (wind came to the ship)
steps.n: Added هَا to show what was affected by the coming (referring to the ship mentioned earlier)

steps.m: wind (the thing that moves through air)
steps.n: The و changes to ي and gets a kasra (ِ) to make it into a noun meaning the thing that does the action

steps.m: a wind (subject of جَآءَتْهَا - it came to them)
steps.n: Adding tanween dammah (ٌ) shows this is any wind, not a specific one. This wind is doing the action of coming in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed pattern to show who/what does the action. This describes the wind (رِيحٌ) in the ayah.
Added tanween damma to show this is describing an undefined thing (the wind)
The middle letter ي changes to ا with a special mark (آ) to show lengthening
Added third person masculine plural object pronoun suffix

steps.m: and came to them (referring to the waves in الْمَوْجُ)
steps.n: Added conjunction prefix وَ meaning 'and'. This word connects to الْمَوْجُ (the waves) which is the subject.

steps.m: to move in waves, surge up and down

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning 'wave'. This word is what 'came' to them in the story.
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific ('the' wave), and ُ shows it's doing the action

steps.m: from (showing where the waves came from)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it's showing that the waves are coming from every direction.
The doubled ل (with shadda) makes the meaning stronger, showing it means completely all or every
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to مَكَانٍ (place) that comes after it, making it mean 'of all places'

steps.m: to be somewhere or in some state

steps.n: Root 'to be' gets م added at start to make a noun meaning 'place where something is'

steps.m: to think or believe something without being completely sure

steps.n: Added fathas (َ) after each letter to make the basic past tense form
Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to show intensity
Added وا at the end to show many people did the action, and changed the vowel before it to match
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it in the verse
This is a particle that introduces a new clause and emphasizes certainty. In this ayah, it connects to ظَنُّوٓا۟ (they thought) to show what they thought.

steps.m: that they (object of ظَنُّوٓا۟ - they thought)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (they/them). This connects to the previous verb ظَنُّوٓا۟ (they thought) to complete the meaning: 'they thought that they...'
Connected the root letters together with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice to show the action happened to someone rather than they did it. In this ayah, it refers to the people on the ship feeling surrounded by danger.
Basic preposition showing how the ships moved - they sailed by means of something

steps.m: with/by them (object of جَرَيْنَ - they sailed)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show who was being carried by the ships. This connects back to the people on the ships mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to call out to someone or pray to them for help

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from one person to many people doing the action in the past
The alif at the end shows this is a group of men in the past tense
Changed from 'a deity' to 'The Deity' by adding ال and merging letters. The word Allah is unique and refers only to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the one being called upon)
steps.n: Added shadda to emphasize the ل and fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being called upon in their prayer. The hamza at the start changed to a special connecting hamza (ٱ).
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding مُ at the start changes 'purified' to 'one who purifies' - like changing 'ran' to 'runner'

steps.m: those who are making (something) pure sincerely (calling upon Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: The ين ending shows this describes many people. In this ayah, it describes how the people on the ship are calling upon Allah with sincere worship when in danger.
Added fatha to show the basic vowel of this preposition

steps.m: for him/it (connected to دَعَوُا۟, they called)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (him/it) to show who the action is for. This word connects to دَعَوُا۟ (they called) to show they called to Him (Allah).
Connected the root letters and added kasra to create the noun form
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon
Added 'the' (ال) at the beginning, which caused the middle letter to be doubled
This is the basic conditional word meaning 'if'

steps.m: if indeed (connecting to دَعَوُا۟ they called)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing this is a strong or important condition. This connects to how they called upon Allah in the previous part of the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action affect someone else - changing from 'being safe' to 'making someone safe'
Added تَ to show 'you' did the action in the past

steps.m: you saved us (referring to the supplication to Allah in the ayah)
steps.n: Added نَا to show who was saved (us). This is part of a prayer to Allah mentioned in the ayah.

steps.m: from (connects كُلِّ مَكَانٍ to the waves)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it shows the waves came from every direction.
Basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for pointing to something near
Added هِ at the end to make it feminine, with ِ (kasra) before it to connect the sounds
Added small ي (ۦ) as a pronunciation helper to show the kasra sound clearly
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added لَ at the start to mean 'surely/definitely' - makes a stronger promise about what will happen

steps.m: surely we will definitely be (responding to اللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added emphatic نَّ suffix to add strong emphasis to the promise being made to Allah in this context
This is a basic preposition showing something comes from or belongs to a group. Here it connects to الشَّاكِرِينَ (the grateful ones) to show who they will be from among.

Added fatha (َ) at the end to show this connects to the definite word الشَّاكِرِينَ (the grateful ones) that follows it. This fatha helps connect the words smoothly when speaking and shows we're talking about a specific group.
This is the basic meaning of being thankful or showing gratitude
Changes the word to mean someone who does the thanking
Adding ال at the beginning makes it specific (like 'the' in English). The first letter gets doubled (شّ) because it comes after ال

steps.m: those who are thankful (connected to مِنَ meaning 'among/from')
steps.n: Adding ين at the end makes it plural (more than one). This word comes after مِنَ in the ayah, showing these are among the thankful people.
Added fatha and shadda to create the basic word meaning 'when'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else
Changed the final ا to آ, which is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: to rescue or deliver from danger
steps.n: Basic meaning of saving or rescuing someone
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing the action. The ا changes to ى at the end.
Added the alif symbol (ٰ) to show the long vowel sound
Added هُمْ at the end to show who was rescued
This is the basic form of the word that refers to a specific time in the past
Adding ا at the end changes it from referring to a specific past time to introducing a condition or future possibility. In this ayah, it's used to describe what happens when Allah saves people.
Basic pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them'. In this ayah, it's the object (receiver) of the verb أَنجَىٰ (He saved/rescued), so it means 'them'.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form past tense. The final ى replaces ي because it's at the end of the word.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, with sukun and kasra vowels for present tense pattern. The final ي is the original root letter returning.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. The final ي drops because it can't combine with ون. In this ayah, it refers to people transgressing against the truth (بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ).
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it shows being in/on the earth.
The ي at the end changes to ى when it's at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make them pronounceable
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to make it specific

steps.m: of/in/on the earth (connected to فِى before it)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/on) in the ayah
Basic noun form showing something that is different or other than something else
Added بِ at the beginning to show 'by means of' or 'with'. When used with غَيْر it means 'without' or 'by other than'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to الحَقِّ (the truth) that comes after it
Root letters combined into a noun with a shadda (doubled middle letter) to mean 'truth'
Added definite article to specify we're talking about 'the truth' rather than just any truth
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the word بِغَيْرِ (without) that comes before it

steps.m: to point to, indicate, or draw attention to

steps.n: Root letters أ-ي-ي combined with shadda to make the question word 'which/what'
Added the vocative particle يٰ to show this is calling out to someone
Added damma (ُ) to show this word is in the nominative case
Added ها to make this a stronger attention-calling word
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he was social' verb to 'people' by dropping first letter and changing middle vowel, like how 'kataba' (wrote) becomes 'kaatib' (writer)
Adding definite article makes the noun definite

steps.m: the people (as the one being addressed)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) shows this word is being directly addressed in the phrase يٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ (O people!)
Adding shadda changes this from 'if' to 'indeed'. This emphasizes what comes after it.
Adding ما limits what comes after to mean 'only'. Together إِنَّمَا introduces a strong statement that restricts or specifies something.

steps.m: to wrongfully seek more than one's right

steps.n: Changed the word into a noun that names the action itself

steps.m: your (all of you) transgression (against أَنفُسِكُم - yourselves)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this belongs to multiple people being addressed. This transgression is described as being against 'yourselves' (أَنفُسِكُم) in the ayah.
Basic preposition showing something is on top of or against something else
Added elongation mark (ٰ) to show the long 'aa' sound is maintained
Added pause mark (ٓ) to show a slight pause in recitation
Added vowel marks (fatha and sukun) to make the root into a word meaning 'soul' or 'self'
Changed to broken plural form by adding hamza at start and damma on last root letter
Added kasra at end to show it's being possessed by something
Added كُم at end to show it belongs to multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding shadda on first letter changes it from a verb to a noun meaning the enjoyment itself
The alif is written as a long vowel mark (ٰ) above the letter, but pronounced the same
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'life' by adding و and ة
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'the life' instead of just 'life'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to الدُّنْيَا (worldly) which comes right after it in the ayah, forming the phrase 'the worldly life'
Root meaning 'near' changed to make the feminine form meaning 'nearest/lower (life)', like how we say 'the worldly life'
Added ال to specify we're talking about this worldly life specifically
Basic particle form that shows sequence of events

steps.m: then (connecting what happened after Allah saved them)
steps.n: Added damma and shadda to complete the word. This particle shows that one thing happens after another, connecting it to the previous ayah about Allah saving them.
Basic preposition meaning 'to' or 'towards'
Added sukun before adding pronoun
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'to us'. This word connects to مَرْجِعُ (return) in this ayah, showing where the return will be.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
Adding مَ at the start and changing the vowels makes it mean the place or time when something returns
Added ُ to show this is the main subject being talked about
Adding كُمْ shows this belongs to all of you. This word connects to إِلَيْنَا (to Us) in the ayah, showing where the return is to.
Doubling the middle letter ب makes the action stronger or more emphatic
نُ at start means 'we', and the word changes to show we are the ones telling the news

steps.m: and then we will tell important news
steps.n: Added فَ meaning 'and then' to show this happens after their return to Allah mentioned in previous ayah

steps.m: and then we will tell you all important news
steps.n: Added object pronoun suffix كُم meaning 'you all' to specify who is being informed
The preposition بِ shows how something is done or what it's about

steps.m: with/about what (connects to تَعْمَلُونَ - you used to do)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to ask about or refer to the things that follow. Here it connects to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ (you used to do) to mean 'with/about what you used to do'
Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he was'
Changed from past tense 'he was' to command form 'be!' by making the word shorter and stronger
Added تُ at the end to show 'I' did the action in the past
Added plural masculine marker to show multiple people being addressed
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he did' to present tense 'you do' by adding تَ at start and changing vowels
The ون at the end makes it plural - more than one person doing the action
Added shadda to strengthen the meaning from conditional 'if' to emphatic 'indeed'
Added ما at the end which changes the emphasis from just 'indeed' to a restrictive meaning of 'only/just'
Adding the vowels َ (fatha) after each letter turns the basic idea into a noun meaning 'example'
The ُ (damma) at the end shows this word is the subject being talked about after إِنَّمَا (indeed/verily)
Changed root letters into a special noun pattern with و to mean 'intense/complete life'
Added ة to make it a proper feminine noun
Added ال (the) at start + changed spelling of middle part slightly + ِ at end to connect it with الدُّنْيَا (worldly/this world)
Changed to describe the life that is closer/nearer to us (compared to the afterlife)
The word الحياة (life) appears before this word in the ayah, showing this describes the worldly life specifically
The shadda emphasizes how this life is very close/immediate compared to the afterlife. This word describes الْحَيَوٰةِ (life) in this ayah.
Basic root meaning related to water and fluidity
The root letters combine to make the noun 'water'. The last root letter ه changes to hamza (ء) in this form.
Adding the fatha vowel mark and extending the alif for pronunciation
Adding tanween kasra to show this word is being acted upon by something
Adding كَ at the beginning to show comparison, meaning 'like' or 'similar to' water
Basic root meaning of downward movement
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added أ at beginning to show causing something to happen. Changed first vowel mark to sukun.
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added هُ at the end meaning 'it' (referring to the rain/water mentioned before). The alif in نا becomes longer (ٰ) when connected to هُ.
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly to the next word السَّمَآءِ (the sky). This combination means 'from the sky'
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb meaning 'to rise' into a noun meaning 'sky' by adding ء at the end
Makes the word definite by adding ال, like adding 'the' in English
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from)
The root letters show the basic idea of mixing things
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added ا and ت to show something mixed by itself naturally (like plants mixing with rain), rather than someone mixing it

steps.m: and so it became mixed (with نَبَاتُ)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this mixing happened as a result of the water being sent down. This word connects to نَبَاتُ (plants) showing how the plants mixed with the water.

steps.m: him/it (referring to the water mentioned before)
steps.n: Added kasra to make it a connected pronoun that can attach to other words

steps.m: with it (referring to the water that mixed with plants)
steps.n: Added the preposition ب which shows how something is done. Here it shows the water is what mixed with the plants.

steps.m: with it (referring to the water that mixed with plants)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after the pronoun to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to grow' to noun 'vegetation'. This word is in a possession relationship with الأرض (the earth), where vegetation belongs to the earth.
Adds ُ to show this word is the subject doing the mixing with water in فَٱخْتَلَطَ (then it mixed)
Added vowel marks to connect the letters: fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it a noun
Added ٱلْ (al) at the beginning to make it specific - we're talking about THE earth
Added kasra at the end because this word comes after نَبَاتُ (plants) in the phrase نَبَاتُ ٱلْأَرْضِ (plants of the earth)
Combined the two words مِن (from) and ما (what/that which) together
The ن merges into the م, shown by the shadda (ّ). This is a common pronunciation rule when ن is followed by م.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on first root letter, and damma at the end

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
people (those who are social with each other)
steps.n: The root means 'to be social' and was changed to mean 'people' - those who are social with each other. The أ was dropped to make it easier to say.
Adding ال makes it specific - we're talking about specific people, not just any people
The ُ shows this word is doing the action in the sentence

steps.m: to graze, feed, or benefit from comfort

steps.n: Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Changed from a verb meaning 'to graze' into a plural noun meaning 'livestock' by adding أَ at the start and changing the middle vowel to a long ا
Added ال (the) at the start and ُ (damma) at the end to show this is 'the livestock' and is doing the action
Added وَ (and) at the beginning of the word
Basic form of this preposition that shows when something ends. It's not derived from a root.
Adding shadda makes the meaning stronger
Adding ىٰٓ at the end shows this connects to what comes next in the ayah (أَخَذَتِ - took). It's like saying 'until [this happened]'

steps.m: when (introduces the time when أَخَذَت happens)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition. It connects to the verb أَخَذَت (took/reached) in the ayah, showing when something happened.

steps.m: when (introduces the time when أَخَذَت happens)
steps.n: The alif is elongated to show emphasis and to help with proper Quranic recitation. This doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to grasp or take hold of something

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added ت with kasra at the end to show this is about the earth (الأرض) which is feminine in Arabic. This word connects to زُخْرُفَهَا (its adornment) in the ayah.
Adding vowel marks to the root letters to make the basic word for 'earth'
Adding definite article ال to specify we're talking about THE earth (subject of أَخَذَتِ - took/reached)
Adding damma to show this is the subject doing the action in أَخَذَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ (the earth took/reached)
Added special vowel pattern to change from action to thing
Added fatha to show this is what's being acted upon by the verb أَخَذَتِ (took)

steps.m: its beauty/decoration (being taken by the earth)
steps.n: Added ها to show this belongs to the earth (which is feminine in Arabic). This whole word is what's being taken (object of أَخَذَتِ)
Added shadda (double letter) on ي to show doing something strongly or thoroughly
Adds feminine marker to indicate the subject is feminine singular
Added 'i' and doubled 'z' at start changes meaning from 'she decorated something' to 'she decorated herself' - like adding 'self' in English

steps.m: and she made herself beautiful or adorned herself
steps.n: The prefix 'وَ' means 'and', connecting this sentence to a previous one.

steps.m: to think/believe/assume something without being completely sure

steps.n: Adding vowel marks makes it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he thought'
Added و at beginning to connect this to the previous description of how the earth took its ornament and was beautified, showing what happened next.

steps.m: to live in a place, settle, populate

steps.n: Basic noun pattern with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter. This word is in a possession relationship where it is owned by/belongs to الأرض (the earth), forming the phrase 'people/inhabitants of the earth'.
Added damma to show this word is doing the action of thinking/assuming
Added the feminine possessive pronoun هَآ meaning 'its' to show these are the earth's people
This is a particle that emphasizes and connects what comes after it, like saying 'that' at the start of 'I know that you are here'
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being talked about (they/them). This connects to the word قٰدِرُونَ (capable) that comes after it, showing who is capable.

steps.m: to be able, to have power over something
one who is able/capable (subject of أَنَّهُمْ)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun showing who does the action. This word is part of what the people thought (after أَنَّهُمْ)
Added ون to show multiple people (the inhabitants) are being described as capable
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of or over something else
Added ي with sukun (ْ) to connect with the coming pronoun

steps.m: upon it/her (referring to الْأَرْضُ - the earth)
steps.n: Added هَآ which means 'it/her'. Here it refers to الْأَرْضُ (the earth) mentioned earlier in the verse
Changed from basic root to past tense 'he' form by adding fathas and changing ي to ى at the end
Added alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى for proper pronunciation

steps.m: he came to her/it (subject of أمرنا)
steps.n: Added هَآ (meaning 'her/it') to show who/what he came to. The هَآ refers back to something feminine mentioned before (like the earth or command)
Root letters made into a noun by adding marks - now means 'a command' instead of 'to command'
Added damma to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb أتى (came) in this ayah
Added نا to show the command belongs to us. This word is the subject that does the action in أتى (came/reached)
Added short 'a' and 'sukoon' vowels to the root letters to make the word for 'night'
Added ًا to show this word tells when something happened. In this ayah, it tells when Allah's command (أَمْرُنَا) came (أَتَىٰهَآ) to the earth.
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two options: our command coming at night (لَيْلًا) or day (نَهَارًا). It's one of the basic Arabic particles that isn't built from a root.
Added the vowel mark fatha (َ) on أ and the sukun (ْ) on و to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'aw'

steps.m: to flow or pass (like time or water)

steps.n: Root letters n-h-r got short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make a word meaning 'flowing'
Adding alif makes it mean 'time of continuous flowing (of light)' - daytime

steps.m: during the daytime (describes when أَمْرُنَا/our command came)
steps.n: The ًا ending makes this word describe when something happens. Here it tells when Allah's command came in the previous ayah (أَتَىٰهَآ أَمْرُنَا/our command came).

steps.m: to make something into something else
and so he made (something into something)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this action follows from what happened before in the verse

steps.m: and so we made (something into something)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action instead of 'he'. Also changed the last fatha to sukun

steps.m: and so we made it (referring to الْأَرْضُ - the earth)
steps.n: Added هَا to show what was affected by the action (the earth, which is feminine in Arabic). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show it's long
Changed the word to show something that was cut down or harvested, like crops that have been collected

steps.m: completely cut down/harvested (describing how فَجَعَلْنٰهَا made it)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this describes how Allah made something (referring back to فَجَعَلْنٰهَا - 'We made it')
This word combines two parts: كَ (like) and أَنْ (that) to create the meaning 'as if'. It connects to the next phrase لَّمْ تَغْنَ (had not existed) to show the land was as if it had never existed.
The final ن loses its sukun because it connects to the next word
This is a negative particle that changes present tense verbs to past tense meaning and negates them. It's like saying 'did not' in English.
The ل gets doubled (shadda) because it comes after a word ending in ن or similar sounds. This is a common pronunciation rule in Arabic.
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ت at beginning to show 'she' and changed to present tense form, with different vowels to show ongoing action
Sukun on second letter marks this as present tense indicative form
Adding ٱلْ (al) makes it specific, like saying 'the yesterday' instead of just any yesterday
Adding بِ shows when something happened - in this case, it means the events happened during yesterday
This is the base demonstrative word meaning 'this'
The alif is replaced with alif waslah to change 'this' to 'that'
Adding كَ at the beginning shows comparison or similarity
Adding لِ is a common addition to this demonstrative word
Adding كَ at the end makes it address 'you'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter makes the action more thorough and complete
Adding نُ at the beginning shows 'we' are doing the action in the present/future
The ُ ending shows this is a normal present tense statement (not a command or wish)
Changed أ-ي to آ and added ة to make it a noun referring to one clear proof or sign
Added ات to make it plural, showing multiple signs or proofs
Added ال to make it specific and added hamza for proper pronunciation

steps.m: the signs, the proofs (being explained in detail by نُفَصِّلُ)
steps.n: The word gets a kasra (i) sound at the end because it's receiving the action of explaining from نُفَصِّلُ (we explain)

steps.m: to stand together, to rise up as one

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the noun. The meaning of standing together evolved to refer to a group of people.
Added the preposition لِ to show this word connects to يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (they reflect/think) in the next ayah

steps.m: for/to a people (who will think يَتَفَكَّرُونَ)
steps.n: Adds tanween kasra to make the word indefinite genitive. The preposition لِ (for/to) puts this word in the genitive case.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter intensifies the meaning

steps.m: to think deeply about something by oneself
steps.n: Adding ت makes it reflexive - doing the action to oneself
ي at start shows 'he' is doing the action now (present tense), while تَفَكَّرَ shows it happened in the past

steps.m: they think deeply (about the signs of Allah mentioned in this ayah)
steps.n: Changes verb from singular to plural masculine form. In this ayah, it refers to people who deeply reflect on Allah's signs in nature and life that were just described.
This is a unique word that comes from the root meaning of divinity but has become the proper name for God in Arabic. The double ل with shadda is a special feature of this name.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled, and fatha on it for pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence - Allah is the one who invites/calls

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَدْعُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. Also added the connecting hamza (ٱ) that comes after و.
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it past tense
Added يَ at beginning and sukun on middle letter to show present tense
Changed the final vowel to damma and the weak letter و combines with it

steps.m: He calls/invites (to دَارِ ٱلسَّلٰمِ - the Home of Peace)
steps.n: Added alif and small circle for emphasis. This verb connects to 'Home of Peace' with 'to' in the next word.
This is a basic preposition that shows movement or direction towards something. Here it connects to دَارِ (home/abode) to show movement towards it.
Added an alif khanjariyah (ىٰ) at the end which is a writing style variation that doesn't change the meaning
The و changes to ا after fatha, making a noun for a place where people stay
The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is connected to the word السَّلٰمِ (peace) that comes after it

steps.m: to be safe, secure, at peace

steps.n: Added alif after second root letter to form a noun meaning the state or result of being safe/peaceful
Added ٱل (al) at beginning and doubled the س with shadda to show this is THE peace, not just any peace

steps.m: of the peace (possessed by دارِ - home/abode)
steps.n: Added kasra at end to show this peace belongs to something (دارِ/home). Together they form دارِ ٱلسَّلٰمِ (home of peace). Also changed alif to elongated fatha (ٰ) in final form.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changed the root letters into a past tense verb by adding the vowel marks 'fatha' (َ) to mean 'he guided'
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to kasra
Added وَ to connect this word to the previous verb يَدْعُوٓا۟ (he invites/calls), showing both actions are done by Allah
This is a standalone word used to refer to people in a general way. In this ayah, it refers to whoever Allah guides, as they are the ones who 'wish/want' (يَشَآءُ).

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Added fatha vowels and the middle ي became ا because it's between two fathas
Changed from past tense (شَاءَ) to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing the ending vowel to ُ
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects the verb يَدْعُوٓا۟ (He invites/calls) with دَارِ (home/abode), showing what He is calling to.
Added the small alif (ىٰ) at the end which is just a writing style variation - it doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
The pattern فِعَال is used for nouns meaning paths or ways
The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) - this is just a writing variation with no meaning change
The tanween (double kasra) shows this is indefinite (like 'a path') and comes after the word إِلَىٰ (to) in the ayah
Added vowels to the root to make a word meaning 'straight/correct' - like when you stand up straight
Added مُسْتَ at the beginning to show something that actively seeks or maintains the quality of being straight. This describes the path (صِرٰطٍ) mentioned in the ayah.
Added emphasis to the م with a shadda
Added tanwin kasra to show this describes the path (صِرٰطٍ) mentioned in the ayah
This is a relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence, like saying 'the one who' in English
Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding ل at the beginning means 'for' or 'to'. This word connects to أَحْسَنُوا (they did good) in the ayah
When two ل letters come together, they merge with a shadda, keeping the same meaning
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Adding 'أ' at the start changes from 'being excellent' to 'making something excellent' or 'doing something in an excellent way'

steps.m: they did excellently (subject of لِلَّذِينَ - for those who)
steps.n: Added plural marker وا to show multiple people doing the action. This word describes what the people mentioned in لِلَّذِينَ (for those who) did.
Added damma and sukun to make it a noun meaning the quality itself
Added ى at the end to make it a feminine superlative, meaning 'the very best' or 'most beautiful'
Added ال to make it definite. This word refers back to the reward for those who did good (أَحْسَنُوا۟) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to increase or add to something

steps.n: Changed from basic root meaning 'increase' into a name 'Ziyad' by adding vowels (ِ َ َ) and an alif. This is a common Arabic name.
Added ة to show one specific instance of increase
Added tanween dammah to make it indefinite (any increase)
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
This is a basic negative word used to say 'no' or make something negative. In this ayah, it's negating ذِلَّةٌ (humiliation).

The و connects this negation to what came before, joining two related ideas. Here it connects two things that won't affect the people of Paradise: darkness won't cover their faces, and humiliation won't touch them.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ prefix, sukun on middle letter, and ُ ending. This verb acts on وُجُوهَهُمْ (their faces) in the ayah.
Adds a 'u' sound at the end to show this is the main verb in the sentence
Added vowel marks to form the singular noun for 'face'
Changed from one face (wajh) to many faces (wujūh) by changing the vowels inside the word
Added a small line (fatha) above the last letter to connect it to another word
Added the plural possessive pronoun هُمْ to show ownership
Adding the short vowels 'fatha' (َ) turns the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'to become dusty'
Changed from a verb (to become dusty) into a noun (dust) by adding the double damma mark. This makes it a subject that does the action together with 'humiliation' in the sentence.
This is the basic negation word that means 'not' or 'no'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before. This negates the verb يَرْهَقُ (covers/overwhelms) in this ayah.
Combined the root letters and doubled the middle letter (shown by shadda) to make it mean 'he became low'
Changed the first vowel to kasra (ِ) and added ة to make it mean the state of being low/humble
Added ٌ to make the word indefinite (a humiliation)
This is the base form of the demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those'
The alif with overbar (ٰ) emphasizes this is for plural
Adding ئِ shows we're pointing to something far away

steps.m: those (pointing to something far) (subject of أَصْحٰبُ)
steps.n: The كَ at the end completes the demonstrative pronoun. This word points to the people mentioned before (those who did good) and connects them to what comes after (companions of Paradise).
Root letters got vowels to make the simple word for 'companion'
This form makes the word plural. It's connected to the word الجَنَّةِ (Paradise) that comes after it, showing these are the companions of Paradise
The ُ ending shows this word is doing something in the sentence. Here, these companions are the ones who will be in Paradise forever (as mentioned in هُمْ فِيهَا خٰلِدُونَ)
Adding shadda makes the action stronger
Adding ة (taa marboota) turns the verb 'to cover' into a noun meaning 'garden' - since gardens are covered places with trees
The word got 'ال' (al) at the start to make it specific - 'the garden' instead of just any garden

steps.m: they/them (referring to أَصْحٰبُ 'the companions' in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a basic pronoun that refers to a group of people (usually male or mixed gender). In this ayah, it refers back to أَصْحٰبُ (the companions) mentioned just before.
This is the basic preposition في which shows location or being inside something
Added the feminine pronoun ها to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to Paradise (الْجَنَّةِ) which is mentioned in the previous part of the verse.
Changes from the basic word 'stay forever' to mean 'someone who stays forever' by adding an alif after first letter and kasra after second letter. Like changing 'write' to 'writer'.
The ون ending makes the word plural and shows these are the doers of the action. This describes هم (they) in the ayah.
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it point to specific people
Added و at the beginning to connect this with what came before. This word is connected to كَسَبُوا (they earned) as its subject.

steps.m: to get or earn something through effort

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters together, making it a past tense verb meaning 'he earned/got through effort'
Changed وا at the end shows many people (they) did the action. This word connects to جَزَآءُ (reward/recompense) in the ayah, showing what they earned will have a consequence.

steps.m: to harm or cause bad effects

steps.n: Root meaning 'bad' becomes a noun 'badness' by adding the vowel 'a' and 'silent w'
Changed from basic 'bad' to a stronger form by adding ي and doubling ئ to mean 'very bad'
Added ات to make it plural, referring to multiple evil deeds
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it's being acted upon by the verb كَسَبُوا (they earned)

steps.m: to repay or give what is due

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the word to show the act of repaying itself rather than someone doing it
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence, connected to the word سَيِّئَةٍ (evil deed). The long alif (آ) is just a writing style variation.
The و changes to ي and gets doubled to show something is very bad
Adding ة makes it refer to one specific bad action instead of the general quality of being bad
The ٍ (tanween kasra) shows this word is indefinite, meaning it's talking about any bad deed, not a specific one

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: This form creates a noun meaning 'likeness' or 'similarity'. In this ayah, it refers to the likeness of an evil deed (سَيِّئَةٍ) mentioned before.
ها refers back to the evil deed (سَيِّئَةٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: with its like (meaning: with a similar evil deed)
steps.n: The بِ means 'with' here, indicating that the recompense matches the evil deed. This completes the meaning in the ayah that those who do evil deeds will be repaid with similar evil deeds.
Adds past tense vowel pattern to make 'he' form
The ت shows this word is feminine because it's describing ذِلَّةٌ (humiliation) which is feminine
هُمْ refers back to الَّذِينَ كَسَبُوا (those who earned evil) mentioned earlier in the ayah
و means 'and'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed first vowel from fatha to kasra to turn verb into noun
Adding ة makes it describe the quality or state of being humiliated
Adding ٌ makes it 'a' state of humiliation rather than 'the' state
This is the basic form of the word that can mean 'what' or 'whatever' depending on context
The shadda (doubled م) adds emphasis. In this ayah, it's used with مِنْ before it to negate عَاصِمٍ (protector), meaning 'there is no protector'
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation
Added هُم at the end to show who the action is for. This word connects to ذِلَّةٌ (humiliation) in the ayah, showing who will experience the humiliation.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
The shadda (ّ) makes the 'n' sound stronger because it comes after another 'n' sound in speech
The fatha (َ) at the end helps connect this word smoothly to the next word اللَّهِ (Allah)
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one supreme deity

steps.m: of Allah/God's (possessed by or connected to Allah)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
Basic preposition showing that something comes from or belongs to something else. In this ayah, it connects the word عَاصِمٍ (protector) to ٱللَّهِ (Allah), showing there is no protector from Allah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changes from 'he protected' to 'protector' by using the active participle pattern (faa'il). Like changing 'write' to 'writer'
The tanween (double kasra) shows this is indefinite (like 'a/an' in English) and is possessed by something. In this ayah, it shows there is no protector from Allah.
Starting with the basic comparison particle
Adding أَنَّ creates emphasis in the comparison

steps.m: as if, just as (describing how their faces were covered)
steps.n: Adding مَآ makes the comparison more general and complete. This word connects to أُغْشِيَتْ (were covered) to describe how the faces were covered.
Basic past tense verb form showing something covered or overwhelmed something else
Added أ at beginning to show causing something to happen to someone else
Changed vowels to show action was done to someone rather than them doing it

steps.m: it was covered (describes وُجُوهُهُمْ - their faces)
steps.n: Added تْ to show this refers to a feminine subject (faces/وُجُوهُهُمْ)
Basic root meaning showing the concept of facing or directing towards something
Root letters joined together with vowels to make the noun meaning 'face'
Changes from singular 'face' to plural 'faces' by adding damma marks inside the word
Adds damma to prepare for attaching the pronoun
Adds the possessive pronoun 'their'

steps.m: to cut or separate into parts

steps.n: Changed the word from an action (cutting) to the result of that action (pieces) by adding specific vowel patterns

steps.m: pieces (describing how something was covered in أُغْشِيَتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ - their faces were covered)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word describes how the covering happened - like pieces of darkness covering their faces
This is a preposition meaning 'from' - it doesn't come from a root. It shows where something starts or its source.
The م is doubled (with ّ) to make the meaning stronger, like saying 'directly from' or 'truly from'
The َ (fatha) at the end shows this connects to the next word (اللَّهِ), making it mean 'from Allah'
Connected the root letters together with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun
Added ال to make the word definite, with the ل merging into the first letter

steps.m: of the night (part of قِطَعًا مِّنَ - pieces of)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word follows مِنَ (from/of) in the phrase 'pieces of the night'
This root carries the basic meaning of darkness or oppression
Added مُ at the start to make it mean 'something that causes' darkness, like how darkness makes things dark
Added ًا at the end to show this word is describing the pieces (قِطَعًا) of night mentioned in the verse
This is the basic form of the demonstrative pronoun that points to things far away
The alif with madda (ٰٓ) shows that we're pointing to something far away
Adding ئِكَ completes the demonstrative pronoun. This word is the subject of أَصْحٰبُ (companions) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to make it mean 'one who does the action'
Changed the word pattern to make it plural. This is called a 'broken plural' because it changes the internal structure of the word rather than just adding an ending.
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject. The alif is written as alif maqsura (ٰ) in this position. This word is connected to النَّارِ (the Fire) to mean 'companions of the Fire'.
Connected the root letters and changed the middle و to ا because it's a weak letter. This word comes from the concept of light/fire.
Added tanween (double damma) to make it indefinite, meaning 'a fire' instead of just 'fire'
Adding ال (the) makes it specific - 'the fire' instead of just 'any fire'. The shadda (double letter) makes it more intense.

steps.m: of the intense fire (possessed by أَصْحٰبُ/companions)
steps.n: The kasra at the end shows this word belongs to or is possessed by أَصْحٰبُ (companions), forming the phrase 'companions of the fire'
This is a pronoun that means 'they' for a group of males or mixed group. In this ayah, it refers to the people who will be in the fire, as they are the subject of خٰلِدُونَ (abiding forever).
This is a basic preposition meaning 'in' or 'inside'. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.

steps.m: in it (referring to النار - the Fire)
steps.n: Added ها to show this refers to النار (the Fire) which was mentioned earlier in the ayah. The Fire is feminine in Arabic, so we use the feminine 'it/her' form.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to describe someone who does this action
Added plural ending to show multiple people who remain
Connected the root letters together with vowel marks to make it a noun
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb 'We will gather them' (نَحْشُرُهُمْ)
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'we' form by adding نَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: we gather them (referring to the disbelievers mentioned later as الَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا)
steps.n: Added 'them' at the end to show who is being gathered. In this ayah, 'them' refers to the disbelievers who will be mentioned right after this word.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'gathered' verb to 'all together' noun by adding ي in middle - makes it describe a complete group

steps.m: all together (describing how something is done)
steps.n: Adds -an ending to show how something is done together as a group
Basic form of the sequencing particle that shows one thing happens after another

steps.m: then, after that (connecting وَيَوْمَ with نَقُولُ)
steps.n: Added damma (ُ) and shadda (ّ) to complete the word. This particle connects 'And the Day' with what happens next - 'We will say'
Changed vowels to make present tense base. The middle و combines with the vowels to make a longer sound.
Added نَ at the beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added ال to make it specific. This word connects descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the one who writes' instead of just 'who writes'
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people instead of just one person
Added لِ at the beginning to show these people are receiving or experiencing something. In this ayah, it's referring to those who committed shirk (associated partners with Allah)

steps.m: to join or partner with others

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the verb causative - showing someone made something happen
Adding وا at the end shows that a group of people did this action
Added مَ at the beginning to show where something happens, turning the idea of existing into a place where things exist
Added fatha at the end because this word is being acted upon by the verb نَقُولُ (we say) in this ayah

steps.m: your (all of you) place! (what we say to them)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show we're talking to multiple people. This is what Allah says to the polytheists on the Day of Judgment, telling them to stay in their place.
This is the basic form of the second person pronoun meaning 'you'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added م with sukun at the end to make the pronoun plural masculine. This shows we're talking to multiple people (all male or mixed group).
Changed vowel pattern and added long alif and hamza to create plural noun for those who are partners
Changed spelling to accommodate upcoming suffix - alif becomes آ and hamza sits on و with damma
Added suffix كُمْ to show possession by multiple people
Added conjunction وَ at beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added vowels and doubled middle letter to show intensive separation
Added نا to show 'we' did the action, and sukun before it

steps.m: and so we separated completely (between them)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happened as a result of what came before. This connects to بَيْنَهُمْ (between them) in the ayah.
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to make it into a preposition

steps.m: between them (referring to the false gods and their worshippers)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this means 'between them'. In this ayah, it refers to separating the false gods from those who used to worship them.
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Added fatha vowels to make it past tense
Changed from قَوَلَ to قَالَ because of weak middle letter و
Added و at beginning to connect this to the previous action of separating (فَزَيَّلْنَا) in the ayah
Root letters got vowels and long 'ee' sound added to make a word meaning 'one who partners'
Changed from single partner (شَرِيك) to many partners by changing vowels and adding اء at the end
Changed alif to alif madd for pronunciation
Added damma to show this word is doing the action
Added هُم at the end to show these are 'their' partners. This word is the subject of قَالَ (said) in this ayah.
This is a negative word that makes the meaning opposite, like adding 'not' in English. Here it's saying 'you never worshipped us' by negating كُنتُمْ تَعْبُدُونَ (you used to worship).
Added a shadda (doubled letter mark) on the م to show it's connected strongly to the word before it. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.
The weak letter و drops out and the vowels change according to regular rules for hollow verbs (verbs with و in the middle)

steps.m: you all were (addressing the polytheists مَكَانَكُمْ أَنتُمْ وَشُرَكَآؤُكُمْ)
steps.n: Added تُمْ to show this is talking about 'you all' in the past. This word is addressing the polytheists and their partners mentioned in the phrase مَكَانَكُمْ أَنتُمْ وَشُرَكَآؤُكُمْ (you and your partners stay in your places)
This is the base form that shows something is receiving an action. It's like saying 'to' or 'at' in English when showing who receives an action.
Added نا at the end to show this is happening to 'us'. This word is receiving the action of the verb تَعْبُدُونَ (you worship), so the complete meaning is 'worship us'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' or 'she' and changed vowels to show present tense action

steps.m: you (all) worship (addressing the polytheists in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are being addressed. This word is directed at the polytheists mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added fathas to make it past tense. The final ى (alif maqsurah) appears because this is a weak-ending verb.

steps.m: so/then he was sufficient (connected to previous statement)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before
Added superscript alif (ٰ) as a pronunciation guide for the alif maqsurah
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added shadda on ل to create the unique name Allah, referring specifically to the One True God
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

steps.m: by Allah (as witness between فَكَفَىٰ and شَهِيدًۢا)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show Allah is being used as a means or instrument. In this ayah, Allah is being called upon as a witness, connecting the words 'sufficient' (فَكَفَىٰ) and 'witness' (شَهِيدًۢا)
Basic past tense verb form meaning 'he witnessed'
Changed from action 'witnessed' to person 'witness' by adding ي - like changing 'saw' to 'seer'
Added tanween alif to show this is the object being acted upon by the verb كَفَىٰ (sufficient)
Added vowel marks to connect letters and show it's a preposition. The fatha shows movement and the sukun stops the middle letter.
Added نَا at the end to show this means 'us'. This word works with فَكَفَىٰ (then sufficient) to say 'then sufficient between us'.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show it's being used as a preposition meaning 'between'
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. In this ayah, it's specifically addressing the disbelievers Allah is witnessing between.

steps.m: and between you all (connecting to بَيْنَنَا - between us)
steps.n: Added وَ meaning 'and' to connect this with the word بَيْنَنَا (between us) that came before it in the ayah

This is a special word that adds emphasis to what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that 'we were unaware of your worship.' It's like adding 'truly' or 'definitely' at the start of a sentence in English.
Added fathas to make past tense. The middle و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Changed from past tense 'he was' to command form 'be!'
Makes the command stronger and more emphatic
Added نا at the end to show 'we' did the action
This is a preposition that connects words, like 'about' in English. Adding vowel marks to show correct pronunciation.
Added vowel marks to show pronunciation. In this ayah, عَنْ connects to عِبَادَتِكُمْ (your worship) to show being unaware/heedless of their worship.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb
Changes from singular verb 'he worshipped' to plural noun 'worshippers'
Changes from 'people who worship' to 'the act of worship' by adding ة at the end
Added 'i' sound at the end to show it connects to the next word (possession)
Added suffix كُمْ to show this belongs to multiple people being addressed
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed the word to describe someone who has this quality
Made the word plural to show multiple people
Added لَ (meaning 'surely/definitely') at the start to make it stronger. This word connects to كُنَّا (we were) in the sentence.
This is the basic demonstrative word meaning 'here' that points to a nearby place or situation
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'here' to 'there', making it point to something farther away
Adding كَ makes it point to a specific place or situation being discussed, like saying 'there in that exact place'
Changes from general 'to test' to specifically 'you test'
Adding alif at end makes it refer to 'they' instead of 'you'
The small waaw above alif shows this is 'they' (plural masculine)
Added damma to show it's a noun and doubled the last letter to show completeness
Added final damma to show this word is describing what comes after it (نَفْسٍ meaning soul/self)
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun pattern. This changes the concept of breathing/being precious into the noun for soul/self.
Added the tanween kasra (ٍ) at the end to show this is indefinite (like saying 'a soul' instead of 'the soul'). This word is doing the action in تَبْلُوا (tests/experiences).
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that refers to things rather than people
The shadda adds emphasis to show importance of what's being referred to

steps.m: whatever, that which (with emphasis and lengthened pronunciation)
steps.n: The alif with madda (آ) shows a longer pronunciation of the final vowel
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create basic past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning to show causing something to happen. The س gets a sukun (ْ) to help pronunciation.

steps.m: she sent ahead/forwarded (referring to نَفْسٍ - soul)
steps.n: Added تْ at the end to show the subject is feminine (نَفْسٍ/soul). This word appears in an ayah talking about what each soul sent ahead for itself.

steps.m: to send back or return something to where it came from

steps.n: Added vowel marks and doubled the middle letter to make it mean 'he sent back'
Changed the first vowel to damma to show this happened to someone/something rather than someone doing it
Added وا at the end to show this happened to a group of people

steps.m: and they were sent back (to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase. This word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) in the ayah to show who they were sent back to.
This is a preposition that shows movement or direction toward something. In this ayah, it connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) to show movement/direction toward Allah.
Changed the word to the special form used only for Allah, with ال merged into the word

steps.m: of Allah (possessor of مَوْلَىٰهُمُ - their true protector)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession, and shadda on lam. This word shows who owns or possesses مَوْلَىٰهُمُ (their true protector) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be close to and take care of something

steps.n: Added مَ at the beginning and changed the ending to ى to turn the basic idea of 'protecting' into 'one who protects'
Added the small alif (ٰ) above the ى to show it's pronounced as a long 'aa' sound
Added هُمُ at the end to show this is their protector. In this ayah, it refers to Allah being the protector of those mentioned.
The doubled letter makes the meaning stronger and more definite
Adding ال makes it refer to absolute truth rather than just any truth

steps.m: of/to the truth (being returned to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: The kasra shows this connects to being returned to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: to lose the way, to wander off the right path

steps.n: Adding the short 'a' vowels (َ) turns the root letters into 'he went astray' in the past tense
Adding و connects this word to what came before it
This is a preposition that shows separation or moving away from something

steps.m: from them, away from them (refers to their false gods mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هُم (them) to show who is being separated from. In this ayah, it refers to the false gods that were mentioned before.
This is a connecting word (relative pronoun) that refers to things rather than people. In this ayah, it connects to the verb أَسْلَفَتْ (sent ahead), meaning 'what they sent ahead'.
Added shadda for emphasis. This is a common change when ما follows certain words.
Added fatha marks to make the past tense form. The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did this action. This word connects to يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent) in the next part of the verse.

steps.m: to make something up or create falsely

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb

steps.m: he made up (something false) on purpose
steps.n: Added اِفْتَ to show someone doing something deliberately and with effort - like making up lies on purpose
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing final vowel to ِي
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ before مَن يَرْزُقُكُم)
steps.n: Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to ask the question that follows.
This is a question word used to ask 'who?' or refer to 'whoever'. In this ayah, it's asking 'who provides for you?' as part of a series of questions about Allah's power.
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation. This word is asking who does the providing (يَرْزُقُ) in this ayah.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: he provides you all (object of يَرْزُقُ)
steps.n: Added كُم to show who receives the provision - in this ayah, Allah is asking who provides for all of you
This is the basic preposition that shows something comes from or is part of something else
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلسَّمَآءِ (the sky/heaven)
Added shadda because the previous word ends with a similar sound that merges with this word's first letter
Root meaning 'rise up' becomes 'sky' by adding اء, since the sky is high above us
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س letter because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of). Also elongated the alif to show proper pronunciation.
Connected the root letters and added basic vowel marks
The kasra ending shows this word follows مِنَ (from) in the phrase 'from the heaven and the earth'
Starting with the two separate words that will combine: أم (or) and من (who)

steps.m: or who? (asking about who does the actions in يَمْلِكُ and يُخْرِجُ)
steps.n: The two words combine with a shadda, making it one word that asks a question. This word is asking about who controls hearing and sight (يَمْلِكُ) and who brings forth life (يُخْرِجُ) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create past tense verb

steps.m: he possesses/controls (the hearing in this ayah)
steps.n: Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra (ِ), and ending with damma (ُ). This verb shows Allah's control over hearing, as mentioned in the ayah.
Root meaning 'hear' becomes the noun 'hearing' - like changing 'hear' to 'hearing' in English
Added definite article ٱل and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ٱل
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb يَمْلِكُ (possesses/controls) in this ayah

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to see' into noun meaning 'sight'
Changed to broken plural form - a special Arabic plural that changes the word's pattern by adding أ at start and ا after ص
Added ٱلْ to make it specific
Added وَ to connect it to the previous word and َ at the end to show it's the object being possessed in يَمْلِكُ (controls/possesses)
This is a question word asking about who manages/arranges things. It's connected to the verb يُدَبِّرُ (arranges/manages) in this ayah.
Adding وَ (and) connects this question to the previous questions in the ayah, making it part of a series of questions about Allah's powers
Changed the vowel pattern to show causing something to happen rather than doing it directly
Added يُ at the beginning to show this is happening in the present/future and who is doing it
