Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to create an active participle (someone/something that does the action)

steps.m: the Living One (object of يُخْرِجُ - brings out)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite. The ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word. This word is being acted upon by يُخْرِجُ (brings out) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or its source
Added َ (fatha) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلْحَىِّ (the living). This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to make a basic noun
Changed middle letter to ya with shadda to show something that has died
Adding the definite article ال to make the noun definite

steps.m: of the dead (thing/person) (object of مِنَ - from)
steps.n: The word gets a kasra (i) ending because it comes after مِنَ (from)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he exited' to 'he makes exit' by adding yu- prefix and changing middle vowels. Makes someone cause the action instead of doing it themselves.

steps.m: and he makes (something) exit/brings out (connected to يَرْزُقُكُم - provides for you)
steps.n: This word is connected to the earlier verb يَرْزُقُكُم (provides for you) in the ayah, listing another of Allah's actions
The و changes to ي and gets doubled with shadda because it's between two vowels. This makes it into a description of something affected by death.
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific dead thing rather than just any dead thing

steps.m: the dead (object of يُخْرِجُ - brings out)
steps.n: Adding fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of 'bringing out' in the ayah
This is a basic preposition showing something moving away from or coming from somewhere else. In this ayah, it shows the dead coming from the living.
When مِنْ comes before a word starting with ال, the ْ changes to َ to make it easier to pronounce. Here it comes before ٱلْمَيِّتِ (the dead).
The doubled ي becomes a shadda, making it an adjective meaning 'alive'
Adding the fatha vowel mark makes it readable as an active adjective
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific, like saying 'the living one' instead of just 'living'

steps.m: of the living one (object of مِنَ)
steps.n: Adding kasra (ِ) shows this word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah. The ي is written as ى when it has a shadda.
This is a combination of و (and) with من (who), used to ask about or refer to people
This root carries the basic meaning of managing or arranging something in an orderly way
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show this is done thoroughly and with great care
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and ُ at end. The middle vowel changes to ِ. In this ayah, Allah is managing all affairs mentioned in the previous verses.
This pattern turns the verb into a noun meaning the action itself or result of the action
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'command' by adding the vowels َ (a) and ْ (sukoon)
Adding ٱلْ makes it specific, like adding 'the' in English
Adding fatha at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the verb يُدَبِّرُ (manages/regulates)
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle weak letter و changes to ُ in present tense
Added يَ at the start to show present tense for 'he'
Added ونَ to show multiple people doing the action
Added سَ to show future tense

steps.m: then they will say (in response to قُلْ)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this as a response to the previous command قُلْ (say)
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: Allah (the one true God who will say this in response)
steps.n: Added ٱل (the) at the start, doubled the ل with shadda, and added ُ at the end to show this is the subject who will be speaking. This special word evolved from إلَه (deity) to become the proper name of the One True God.
The middle weak letter و is dropped in the imperative form. This is a command to one person to speak
Added damma (ُ) on first letter and dropped the weak letter و to make it a command. This tells one person to do something.
Added فَ at the beginning to connect this command with what came before it. In this ayah, it follows after they will say 'Allah', so it means 'then say!'
This letter turns a statement into a yes/no question, like adding a question mark in English
The فَ shows this question follows from what was just said, like saying 'so' or 'then'

steps.m: So will you not...? (following from previous statement)
steps.n: The لا adds 'not' to make this ask why something isn't happening. In this ayah, it's asking 'So will you not fear (Allah)?' based on the evidence just mentioned.

steps.m: to protect or guard against harm
steps.n: Basic meaning showing the concept of protection
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create past tense verb
Added ت at beginning and doubled middle letter to show doing action to oneself
Added second ت to show 'you' and merged letters due to similar sounds
Added ون ending to show multiple people doing the action
Basic pointing word used to indicate something nearby, like saying 'this' while pointing at something
Adding لِ and كَ changes it from pointing to something near to pointing to something far away
Changed كَ to كُمُ to show it's addressing multiple people instead of one person

steps.m: so that for all of you (pointing to اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Added فَ to connect this with what came before it, pointing to Allah mentioned right after
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added the U mark at the end to show it's the subject
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
The root verb 'to nurture' becomes 'Lord' by doubling the middle letter, showing someone who nurtures and cares for everything
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: your Lord (referring to Allah as the true Lord in this ayah)
steps.n: Added كُمُ to show possession by multiple people being addressed
Added basic vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
Added damma at end to make it a noun in nominative case
Added ال to make it 'the truth' instead of just 'truth'
This is the basic question word used to ask about things or seek information
Adding ذَا makes the question more specific by asking about a particular thing

steps.m: so what thing (comes after الْحَقُّ - the truth)
steps.n: The فَ connects this question to what came before, showing a logical sequence. It's asking what comes after the truth that was just mentioned.
Basic concept of distance or separation in space or time
Added vowel marks to create a preposition meaning 'after'. This word comes before 'the truth' (ٱلْحَقِّ) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changed from a past tense verb (was true) to a noun meaning truth, like changing 'it was true' to just 'truth'
ال makes it specific - not just any truth, but THE truth
Kasra ending indicates genitive case. This word is in the genitive case because it comes after بَعْدَ (meaning 'after'), which requires the following word to be in genitive case.
Starting with the basic particle form. This word is used to exclude what comes after it from what came before it, like saying 'everything EXCEPT this'.
The shadda (double letter mark) on ل makes the word stronger, showing a clear separation between two things. Here it strongly separates truth from misguidance.

steps.m: to wander off the right path

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from a verb to a noun meaning the act or state of going astray
Added the definite article ال to specify this particular state of being lost
Added damma at the end because it comes after إِلَّا (except). The alif is written as a long alif (ٰ) in this word.
This is a question word that can ask about manner, place, or time. The shadda (double letter mark) and alif maksura at the end give it its interrogative meaning.

steps.m: so how (connects to تُصْرَفُونَ - you are turned away)
steps.n: The فَ shows this question follows from the previous statement. It's asking about how people could turn away after the truth has been made clear.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he turned' to 'being turned' by adding تُ at start and changing vowels to show something is receiving the action

steps.m: you (all) are being turned away (from the truth)
steps.n: Adds plural ending to show it's happening to multiple people. In this ayah, it refers to how people are being turned away from the truth after it has been made clear.
Starting with the comparison particle ك that shows similarity
Added ذا which is a demonstrative word meaning 'this'
Added ل with kasra which extends the pointing to something further away, changing 'this' to 'that'
Added ك at the end to show this is being addressed to someone (in this case, the Prophet ﷺ)

steps.m: became true/certain (verb in كَذٰلِكَ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَتُ)
steps.n: The middle letter is doubled (has shadda) to show intensity. This word is connected to كَلِمَتُ (word) which comes after it.

steps.m: she/it became true (referring to كَلِمَتُ - the word)
steps.n: Adding feminine marker ت shows this refers to كَلِمَتُ (word) which is feminine
Added short vowels (fatha) to make the past tense verb 'he spoke'
Changed middle vowel from 'a' to 'i' to change meaning from speaking to being hurt
Added ة to make it a noun meaning 'word' - while they look similar, this is actually a different root than the verb 'to wound'
Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in حَقَّتْ (became true/established)
The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who nurtures completely and perfectly
The kasra at the end shows this means 'my Lord' or 'O my Lord' - it's like adding 'my' to 'Lord'
Adding كَ shows this Lord belongs to the person being spoken to, while still being possessed by كَلِمَتُ (word/decree)

This is a preposition that shows something is on top of, over, or affecting something else. In this ayah, it connects what came before (كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ - the word of your Lord) to what comes after (ٱلَّذِينَ - those who).
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun used to refer to someone or something
Adding ال makes it more specific, like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Adding ين makes it plural, changing from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This word is connected to فَسَقُوٓا۟ (they defiantly disobeyed) in the ayah.
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: they broke away from obedience (subject of لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show a group of people did this action. This word describes the same people who 'do not believe' (لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ) in the ayah.
This is a particle that introduces a new clause, showing that what comes after it is connected to what came before
Added هُمْ at the end to show who the word is talking about (they/them)
This is a negative particle that makes something 'not' happen. In this ayah, it negates the verb يُؤْمِنُونَ (they believe) to mean 'they do not believe'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to cause to feel safe/to believe
steps.n: Adding long alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
The يُ at the start shows it's happening now instead of in the past. The ُ at the end shows he is doing the action.
The ونَ ending shows this is about a group of people (either all male or mixed gender) doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma (ُ) on first letter, and 3) adding sukun (ْ) on last letter. This word is telling one person to speak.
This is a question word that comes at the start of a sentence to ask yes/no questions. In this ayah, it's asking if there are any partners (gods) who can create and recreate.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it's asking about partners/associates, showing which ones from among them can start creation.

steps.m: to share or join with others

steps.n: Added vowels and ي to make the word mean someone who does the sharing
Changed the first vowel from 'a' to 'u', middle vowel from 'i' to 'a', and added اء at the end to make it plural
Added كُم at the end to show the partners belong to multiple people being addressed
Basic question word that asks about people
Added shadda to connect smoothly with previous word. This word is asking about who can begin creation, referring to the verb يَبْدَؤُا۟ (begins/starts) that comes after it.

steps.m: to begin or start something new

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fathas to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show present/future tense
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding a small waaw at the end - similar to how we change 'begins' to 'begin' for plural in English

steps.m: to create or form something from nothing

steps.n: The root letters خ-ل-ق turned into the noun 'creation' by adding the vowel marks fatHa and sukoon. This is what is being created/started.
Added ٱلْ at the beginning to mean 'the' and َ at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb 'begins'
This word shows that Allah first starts creation (يَبْدَؤُا۟) and then brings it back (يُعِيدُهُ). It's like saying 'first this happens, then that happens' in English.
Changed the middle weak letter و to ي and adjusted vowels for present tense form
Added يُ (yu) at start to show 'he' is doing the action in present tense
Added هُۥ at the end to show what is being brought back. This refers to الْخَلْقَ (the creation) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
These are the three basic letters that give us the idea of saying something
Changed the root into a command by adding damma (u) and sukun (silent mark) - tells someone to say something
The weak letter و is dropped to make the command shorter and easier to say
Added kasra at the end to connect smoothly to the next word هَلْ in the ayah
Added ال to make it specific and changed the first letter, making it a proper noun for The One True God

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَبْدَؤُا۟ - He begins)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added damma to show this is the subject doing the action in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word is paired with يُعِيدُ (he returns) in the ayah.
Changed to plural 'they' by adding وا. The hamza (ء) changes to ؤ when followed by و.

steps.m: to create, form, or shape something from nothing

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he created' (a verb) to 'creation' (a noun) by changing middle vowel to sukun. This is the thing that Allah begins.
Added ٱلْ to show we're talking about a specific creation
This word connects two actions in sequence - first Allah starts the creation (يَبْدَؤُا۟), then He repeats it (يُعِيدُهُۥ). The shaddah makes the meaning stronger.
Added vowels to make it mean 'he returned' - the middle letter و changed to ا which is common in hollow verbs
Added يُ at start to make it present tense (he is doing it now), and the middle letter changed to make it easier to say

steps.m: he returns/brings it back (object of يَبْدَؤُا)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what is being returned. This connects to the verb يَبْدَؤُا (to begin) in the ayah, showing what is being returned is the same thing that was begun.
This is a basic question word asking about manner or method
Added vowel marks and emphasis on middle letter to show correct pronunciation
Added alif maksura at the end to show proper spelling

steps.m: so how/in what way (connects to تُؤْفَكُونَ - you are deluded)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to connect this question to the previous statement, showing consequence or result. This word questions how they could be so deluded after the clear evidence mentioned before.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice - something is happening to the subject rather than subject doing the action
Added ون to show this applies to a group of people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The middle weak letter و is dropped and vowels change to make a command. This is telling one person to speak. This word starts a new sentence in the ayah.

steps.m: is there? / do you have? (asking قُلْ - say)
steps.n: This is a simple word that asks a yes/no question. In this ayah, it's connected to قُلْ (say), asking the listener if they have something.
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'your partners' (شُرَكَآئِكُم) with what follows, asking which of them can guide to the truth.

steps.m: to share or join with others

steps.n: Added vowels and ي to make the word mean someone who does the sharing
Changed to plural by adding اء at the end and changing middle vowels - like going from 'partner' to 'partners'
Changed the ending to prepare for showing possession
Added كُم at the end to show these are your partners
Basic interrogative/relative pronoun meaning 'who' or 'whoever'
Added shadda for emphasis and to connect smoothly with the previous word. This word is asking about 'who guides' (يَهْدِى) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to kasra to match present tense pattern
Added ى at the end to complete the present tense verb form
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects the verb يَهْدِى (guides) with ٱلْحَقِّ (the truth), showing what someone is being guided towards.
Root letters joined together with a shadda (double letter) to make the noun 'truth'
Added ال to point to a specific truth rather than truth in general
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word إِلَى (to/towards) in the ayah
Changed to basic command form by adding damma on first letter and sukun on middle letter
The middle letter و is dropped because it's between a damma and sukun, which is a common rule for hollow verbs (verbs with و or ي in the middle)
Added kasra at the end because this word connects to the next word in the ayah
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for The One True God in Islam. The ال at the beginning and the emphasis (shadda) on the ل show this is THE one and only deity worthy of worship.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, changed middle vowel to sukun, and last vowel to kasra. This verb connects to الْحَقِّ (the truth) using إِلَى (to) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters and added shadda to make it stronger
Added ال to point to a specific truth rather than truth in general

steps.m: to/for the truth (object of يَهْدِى - guides)
steps.n: Added لِ to show this is what someone is being guided to. This word connects to يَهْدِى (guides) in the ayah, showing what people are being guided towards.
Added a small diagonal line (kasra) under the last letter because it's receiving the action of 'guides' in the sentence
This letter turns the sentence into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Added فَ to show this follows from what came before
Added مَن which asks about who does something. In this ayah, it's asking about who guides to the truth.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The ي at the end becomes ى because it's a weak letter at the end.
The ي at start shows it's happening now, not in the past
The ى makes it clear this is present tense
This is a primary preposition that shows direction or destination. It has no root or derivational steps as it is a particle.
Added the proper vowel marks to show how it's pronounced. In this ayah, it's used to show direction towards 'the truth' (الْحَقِّ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Doubled the middle letter to add emphasis to the meaning
Adding ال (the) changes this from a verb meaning 'was true' to a noun meaning 'the truth'
The kasra (ِ) is added because this word comes after إِلَى (meaning 'to')
Connected letters with fatha and added shadda to show doubled letter

steps.m: more/most true/right (than those who don't guide)
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning to show comparison. In this ayah, it's comparing those who guide to truth versus those who don't.
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of أَن يُتَّبَعَ (to be followed)
This particle connects parts of a sentence and introduces what should/could happen. Here it connects to يُتَّبَعَ (to be followed), showing what is more worthy of being followed.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from active (he followed) to passive (he is followed) by adding يُ at start and making the middle letter silent
The ت is doubled (has shadda) to show emphasis and following closely
Starting with the first part of the compound word that means 'or'
Combined with مَن (who), where the ن of من merges into the م of أم creating a shadda. This word is asking about who guides to the truth.
This is a basic negative particle that shows something is not true
Adding shadda makes the negation stronger. This word is negating the verb يَهِدِّىٓ (guides) in this ayah, emphasizing that guidance cannot happen except under certain conditions.

steps.m: to guide or show the right way

steps.n: The root letters were connected with fatha marks to make 'he guided'. The last letter ي becomes ى (alif maqsura) at the end of words.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra, and final ى back to ي for present tense
Added a double letter (shadda) in the middle to show doing lots of guiding
Added a long vowel at the end to extend the sound
Basic particle form meaning 'except'. Used to exclude something from what was mentioned before.
Added kasra under the hamza to show correct pronunciation
Added shadda and fatha on lam to show emphasis and pronunciation
Added elongated alif at the end to show lengthened pronunciation. In this ayah, it excludes those who need guidance from those who can guide others.

steps.m: that/to (connects to يُتَّبَعَ - to be followed)
steps.n: This particle introduces the verb يُتَّبَعَ (to be followed) and connects it to what came before. In this ayah, it's asking whether someone who guides to truth is more worthy that they should be followed.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he guided' to 'he is guided' by adding يُ at the start and changing the vowels to show someone is receiving the guidance instead of doing the guiding
Added superscript alif (ٰ) after the alif maqsurah (ى) to show it's a long vowel sound
Combines 'so' (fa) with 'what' (ma) to ask a question about a result or consequence
Added fatha to show this is connected to what follows
Added كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being addressed
This is a question word used to ask about the way or manner something happens. In this ayah, it's asking about how they make judgments (تَحْكُمُونَ).

steps.m: to make decisions or determine what is right/wrong

steps.n: Connected the letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense form
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense

steps.m: you (all) judge/decide (in this ayah, asking how you make your judgment)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action. This word appears in a question (كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ - how do you judge?)
Basic particle that can mean either 'what' or 'not' - in this ayah it's used as 'not' to negate the verb يَتَّبِعُ (they follow)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end

Added emphasis by doubling the ت with shadda and changing the vowel to kasra, showing this is a more intense or persistent form of following. This word describes what 'most of them' (أَكْثَرُهُمْ) are doing.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Adding أ at the start makes it mean 'more' or 'most'
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added هُمْ to show this refers to 'them' (the disbelievers mentioned earlier in the surah)

This is a fundamental particle used for exception/exclusion. In this ayah, it shows that 'most of them' follow nothing except assumption/conjecture. It connects what comes before it (what they follow) with what comes after it (ظَنًّا/assumption).

steps.m: to assume or make guesses without certainty

steps.n: Added fathas to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past
The second ن is doubled with a shadda mark to strengthen the meaning
Added ًا at the end to show this is what is being followed (object of يَتَّبِعُ meaning 'they follow')
This is the basic form of the particle that connects ideas together
Adding the shadda makes the word stronger, changing it from a simple connector to a word that emphasizes what comes after it. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah knows what they do.

steps.m: to guess or make assumptions without certainty

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make the word pronounceable as a past tense verb
Changed from 'he guessed' (verb) to 'the assumption' (noun) by adding ال and putting a shaddah on ظ and ن. This is what they follow in the ayah.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُغْنِى (it enriches/benefits) negative, changing its meaning to 'it does not enrich/benefit'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to be sufficient or free from need

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a past tense verb

Changed from past tense 'was rich/sufficient' to present tense 'makes rich/sufficient' by adding يُ at start. The word now shows someone is causing/making something sufficient rather than just being sufficient
Adding ى at the end shows this is a normal present tense verb (not emphasized or commanded)
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added َ (fatha) at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word الْحَقِّ (the truth). This is like adding a connecting sound between words.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make it a past tense verb
The kasra at the end shows this word is the object of a preposition
Adding the definite article makes it 'the truth' rather than just 'truth'
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'thing'
Added tanween and alif to show this word is receiving the action of يُغْنِى (avail/benefit). The word means 'anything' in this context because it comes after لا (not).
The word 'thing' gets a special mark (ً) at the end to show something is happening to it
This is a special word called a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It's like saying 'I really mean it!' before making a statement.
Added the vowel marks and shadda to show how it's pronounced. This word emphasizes الظَّنَّ (assumption) in this ayah to show that assumptions really cannot replace truth.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال and joining letters together
Added fatha at the end to show Allah is the object being discussed after إِنَّ

steps.m: to know deeply or understand completely

steps.n: Added vowels and ي to show this describes someone who knows everything completely. This word describes Allah (اللَّهَ) in this ayah.
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is describing Allah's quality of being All-Knowing
This is a preposition that shows how something is done or what it's done with
Added مَا which means 'what/whatever'. Together with بِ it connects the preposition to the verb يَفْعَلُونَ (they do) in this ayah, showing 'with whatever they do'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The يَ at the start shows this is happening now or in the future

steps.m: they do/are doing (referring to the majority mentioned in أَكْثَرُهُمْ)
steps.n: Changes from singular 'he does' to plural 'they do' by adding ون at the end, matching with 'most of them' mentioned earlier
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation. In this ayah, ما is used with كَانَ to say 'was not'.

steps.m: and what/not (connects to previous statement and negates كَانَ)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this idea with what came before it
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas. This word is negated by مَا before it, making the meaning 'was not'
This is the basic pointing word that shows something is near. The alif on top (ٰ) is a special mark that shows it's stretched out when speaking.
Adding ذَ makes the pointing more specific, like using your finger to point at something exact
The ا at the end shows we're pointing to something masculine and singular. In this ayah, it's pointing to القُرْءَانُ (the Quran).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The pattern فُعْلان makes the word mean 'something full of or characterized by reading/recitation'
Added ال to make it specific and ُ to show it's the subject of the sentence. This refers specifically to the holy book of Islam.
This is a particle that connects two ideas. Here it introduces what couldn't happen - that the Quran could be invented/forged. It works with the word يُفْتَرَىٰ to show this meaning.

steps.m: to make up or invent something false

steps.n: Changes from active (someone invents) to passive (something is invented)
Adds 'ت' to show something is done to itself or receives the action

steps.m: it could be fabricated (subject of كان)
steps.n: Adds final elongation mark to complete the passive verb form. This word is the subject of كان (was) in كان...أن showing what was not possible.
This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of. Here it connects the word دُونِ (besides/other than) with ٱللَّهِ (Allah).

steps.m: to be lower or below something

steps.n: Adding damma (ُ) changes the word into a common preposition meaning 'below' or 'without'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it
Changed the root into the basic noun form meaning 'a deity'
Changed to the unique name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters with shadda
Added kasra at the end to show possession by رَّبِّ (Lord) in this ayah. Also changed alif to wasl alif since it comes after another word.
This is a particle that shows contrast with what came before. It introduces an exception or different perspective from the previous statement.

steps.m: and yet/but still/however (connecting to previous statement about Quran not being fabricated)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this contrasting idea with the previous statement about the Quran not being fabricated
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to show who does the action
Added ت at start to make it mean 'the act of confirming'
This is the base form of the relative pronoun used to connect clauses. It points to something mentioned before.
Adding ٱلَّ makes it more specific and definite. In this ayah, it refers back to the Quran (القُرْءَانُ) mentioned before.

steps.m: to be separate/clear from something else

steps.n: Adding vowels changes it from a root into a preposition meaning 'between'
The root letters combine to form the basic noun meaning 'hand' - a common body part word in Arabic
Added ي with fatha before it to show it's exactly two hands
Added هِ to show possession. The word follows بَيْنَ (between) in the ayah, which requires the genitive case.

steps.m: to separate or explain in detail

steps.n: The shadda (doubled middle letter) shows someone did the action of explaining things very clearly and in detail
Changes the verb into a noun meaning 'the act of explaining in detail'

steps.m: and a detailed explanation (object of كَانَ)
steps.n: Added وَ meaning 'and' at the start, and َ at the end to show this is the object of كَانَ (was) in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters together to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowel pattern to make a noun representing the result of writing. This word is in the genitive case because it comes after تَفْصِيلَ (detailing) in a possession relationship.
ٱلْ makes it definite ('the'), and the ِ ending shows it's in the genitive case
This is a negative particle that doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it negates the word رَيْبَ (doubt), making the phrase mean 'there is no doubt'.
Added fatha mark on ل to show the correct pronunciation.
Changed the verb into a noun by adding َ on first letter and ْ on middle letter
The َ at the end shows this word is affected by لا (no/there is no) which comes before it. Together they mean 'there is no doubt'.
This is a preposition that doesn't come from a root
The word في (in) gets هِ (it) attached to show what something is in. Here it means 'in it' where 'it' points back to the Quran

steps.m: from (connecting دُونِ with what comes after)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. In this ayah, it connects دُونِ (besides/other than) with what comes after it, showing the Quran couldn't have come from any source other than Allah.

steps.m: to nurture, raise, sustain as a lord

steps.n: Doubling the ب shows intensity and makes it a noun meaning 'lord'
Adding kasra at the end makes it a call/address to the Lord

steps.m: to know deeply or mark something

steps.n: Root 'know' becomes 'world' using pattern faa'al - like how a world is something known/marked by its Creator
Adding ين makes it plural, showing multiple worlds/realms

steps.m: the worlds (object of رَبِّ - Lord of)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it definite ('the'). The َ at the end shows it belongs to رَبِّ (Lord of the worlds)

steps.m: the worlds (object of رَبِّ - Lord of)
steps.n: The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) but pronounced the same

steps.m: or (asking about an alternative in يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: This is a question word that means 'or?' when asking about choices. In this ayah, it's connecting to what comes after (يَقُولُونَ - 'they say') to ask if that's what they're claiming instead.
Added vowels to connect the letters. The middle و becomes ا in this form.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to original و.
Added ونَ to show multiple people (masculine plural) are doing the action. This word follows أَمْ (or) in the ayah.
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added ٱ at start and تْ after first letter to show he did this action himself

steps.m: he made it up himself (object of يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what was made up. This word is what 'they say' (يَقُولُونَ) in the verse.
Added damma to make it a command form
The و is dropped because it's between a damma and a sukun. This word is a command given in response to those who say (يَقُولُونَ) that the Prophet made up the Quran.
Root letters أ-ت-ي were joined together with vowel marks to make the present tense verb 'coming'
Changed from 'coming' to 'bring!' by making it a command - dropped the ي and added ُ (damma) at the end
Added وا to make it plural command
Added فَ to show this is a response or result (In this ayah, it's responding to those who say the Quran is made up, challenging them to bring a similar surah if they can)
Added damma to create a noun meaning the thing that encloses

steps.m: a single chapter (like one enclosed section)
steps.n: The ة (taa marbutah) makes it a noun referring to a single unit, derived from the concept of an enclosed/contained section

steps.m: with a chapter (object of فَأْتُوا - bring forth)
steps.n: The preposition بِ is added to indicate 'with/by'

steps.m: with a single chapter (object of command 'bring forth')
steps.n: The tanween kasra is added to mark the noun as indefinite and in the genitive case. The word is the object of the command 'bring forth' (فَأْتُوا) and is preceded by the preposition بِ which puts it in the genitive case.

steps.m: to be like or similar to

steps.n: Changed vowels to create a noun meaning 'similar' or 'like'
Added kasra at end to show this word is affected by a preposition
Added attached pronoun هِ to show possession (his/its)
Added small ي after هِ as a pronunciation helper - doesn't change meaning

steps.m: to call upon or invite someone

steps.n: Added sukun and damma to make basic command form. The و drops because it's a weak letter at the end.
Added helper alif at start to make pronunciation easier
Added وا at the end to make the command for multiple people
Added و with fatha at start to mean 'and'

This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. In this ayah, it's being used as the object of وَٱدْعُوا۟ (and call), meaning 'call whoever you can.' The kasra (ِ) at the end shows it's connected to what comes after it.

steps.m: to be able, to have capability

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to past tense verb by putting 'aa' between first and second letters
Added ٱسْتَ at the beginning to show trying or seeking to do something
Removing the long ا and adding ْ at the end makes it negative ('did not try' instead of 'tried')
Added تُم at the end to show that many people (you all) did this action in the past
Basic particle showing something comes from or belongs to something else
Added shadda because this word comes after a word ending in ن or tanween in speech, causing the sounds to merge. This is just a pronunciation change and doesn't affect meaning.

steps.m: to be below or less than something
steps.n: The root conveys the basic idea of being lower, beneath, or less than something else
Adding the damma (ُ) changes it from a root to a word meaning 'below'
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word اللَّهِ (Allah) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'besides Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Adding 'al' makes it refer to a specific deity rather than any deity

steps.m: of the deity, of the god
steps.n: Adding kasra at the end shows possession or belonging - like saying 'of the god' instead of just 'the god'

steps.m: if (introducing condition for كُنتُمْ صٰدِقِينَ - you are truthful)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces the condition 'if you are truthful'. It connects two parts: the condition (being truthful) and what should happen in that case (bringing a similar surah).
Changed the root letters into past tense 'he' form by adding vowels (u after k, long o, a after n)
The middle و is dropped because it's a hollow verb in past tense. Added sukun to show the letter connection.
Added تُمْ at the end to show that multiple people are being addressed. This word connects to صٰدِقِينَ (truthful ones) in the ayah.
Adds vowel marks to show this describes someone being truthful
Adding ين makes the word refer to many people. It has this ending because it's describing the word كُنتُمْ (you were).
A particle used to express transition or contradiction from previous statement
The doubled ذ shows this is done multiple times or with intensity
Changes from basic meaning 'to deny' into 'he denied' - shows one male person did the action in the past

steps.m: they strongly denied/rejected (something) as false (subject of بَلْ)
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'they' denied - the و and ا show multiple people did the action
Added kasra to show how the preposition is pronounced

steps.m: with what (they denied without having full knowledge of it)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' to specify what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers to what they denied without having full knowledge of it.

steps.m: not (negates يُحِيطُوا۟ 'they encompassed' to 'they did not encompass')
steps.n: A negative particle that changes present tense verbs to past negative. In this ayah, it negates يُحِيطُوا۟ (they encompassed) to mean 'they did not encompass'
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the past tense form
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels
The word changes from 'he surrounds' to 'they surround' and is used with لَمْ to say they cannot/do not surround something
Changed the root into a noun by adding the vowel marks ِ and ْ. This makes it mean the actual knowledge itself rather than the action of knowing.
Added بِ at the beginning to show this knowledge is being used as a tool or means ('with' or 'by')

steps.m: with His knowledge (which they did not fully grasp)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this is Allah's knowledge that the disbelievers failed to comprehend fully, as shown by يُحِيطُوا (they encompassed) in the ayah
Added small ي after هِ to help with pronunciation when continuing to next word

steps.m: and when not yet, and while not
steps.n: Compound word formed by joining conjunction وَ (and) with لَمَّا (not yet/when)
Added يَ at the beginning to show present tense, with ْ (sukun) on the أ and ِ (kasra) on the ت
Added هِمْ at the end to show the action is happening to 'them'. This word is negated by لَمْ in the ayah, making it mean 'has not come to them'

steps.m: to return to the original state or meaning

steps.n: Root letters أ-و-ل get short 'a' and silent marks to make the word 'awl' meaning 'to return/interpret'
Added تَ at start and ي after و to make a noun meaning 'interpretation' from the basic idea of 'returning'
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the verb يَأْتِ (comes) in this ayah
Added هُ with elongation mark ۥ to show possession
The basic demonstrative word meaning 'this/that'
Added ل with kasra to show distance - this changes 'this' to 'that'
The ك at the end is part of the complete word ذلك ('that'). It's not a separate piece - the word needs this ك to be complete and point to something far away.
Added vowels (a-a-a) to make it mean 'he denied' in the past
Adding shadda (doubling mark) on middle letter makes the meaning stronger. This word is connected to يُحِيطُوا (they encompass) in this ayah, showing what they strongly denied.
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال with shadda to make it definite and emphasize its connecting role
Added ن at the end to make it plural, referring to multiple people

steps.m: from (connects قَبْلِ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows where something comes from or starts. Here it connects قَبْلِ (before) to the previous part of the sentence, showing the timing relationship.

steps.m: to be in front of, to precede

steps.n: Root meaning 'front/before' becomes a simple word meaning 'before' (like 'earlier' or 'ago')
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by what comes after it

steps.m: before them (referring to those who denied in the past)
steps.n: Added هِمْ to show this refers to 'them' - specifically the previous deniers mentioned in the ayah
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he looked'
Changed from 'he looked' to 'look!' by adding U sound at start and making it a command to one person

steps.m: so look!/then observe! (command to Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: Added فَ at beginning to show this is a result/consequence of what came before. The alif gets a wasl mark because it comes after فَ.
This is a question word used to ask about the way something happens or the state of something
Adding these vowel marks helps show this is a question word asking about manner/way
Adding the fatha at the end shows this is being used as a question word
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels in past tense. This is a common change in Arabic words with و in the middle.

steps.m: to follow or come after something

steps.n: Changes from root to noun meaning 'what follows/results'
Changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) for Quranic spelling variation
Added ة to specify a particular instance or result
Added ُ to show this is the subject of كَانَ (was) in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters together and show this happened in the past

This pattern shows someone who does the action - like adding '-er' in English (teach → teacher). The word is in the genitive case because it comes after عاقبة (end/outcome) in a possession relationship, showing it is 'the end of the wrongdoers.'
Adding ين makes it plural - shows there are multiple wrongdoers, like adding 's' in English
Adding ال makes it specific - like adding 'the' in English. Also adds َ at the end to show its role in the sentence.
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from'. The sukun shows where to stop the sound.

steps.m: from them (referring to those who reject the truth)
steps.n: Added 'them' (هُم) to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers to those who reject the truth, since the next words (مَّن لَّا يُؤْمِنُ) talk about those who don't believe.
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect with previous statement
This is a word that refers to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who' in English. Here it's talking about who believes.
The م gets doubled (has shadda) because it comes right after وَمِنْهُم which ends in a vowel sound. This helps the words flow together smoothly when reading.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start. The middle vowel changed from a to u to show ongoing action. The ؤ (hamza) appears between vowels to help pronunciation.
Removes the dot under the middle letter (ؤ), but meaning stays the same - 'he believes'
This is a basic preposition

steps.m: in/with/by him/it (referring back to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added 'him/it' (masculine singular) to show who/what is being referred to. This pronoun refers back to something mentioned earlier in the conversation.

steps.m: in/with/by him/it (referring back to what was mentioned before)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
This is a basic preposition that connects to the verb يُؤْمِنُ (believes) in this ayah
The و adds 'and' to connect this phrase with what came before it. This preposition introduces who is doing the believing (يُؤْمِنُ)
هم is added to show it refers to 'them' (masculine plural). The sukun (ْ) is added before هم to help pronunciation. This whole phrase tells us who is doing the believing in يُؤْمِنُ.
This is a standalone relative pronoun used to refer to people. It is not derived from a root.

The shadda is added because this word comes after another word ending in ن (مِنْهُم). When ن meets م, the ن sound merges into the م sound, shown by the shadda. This word is the subject (doer) of يُؤْمِنُ (believes) in this ayah.
This is a negative particle that means 'no' or 'not'. It is a fixed form that does not derive from a root.
Adding the shadda (ّ) makes the 'no/not' stronger. Here it's emphasizing that some people definitely do not believe. This word is negating the verb يُؤْمِنُ (believes) in this ayah.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added an alif at the start to make it mean 'to believe/have faith' instead of just 'feel safe'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and ُ at end. The word بِهِ after it means 'in it/him'.
Basic preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'. In this ayah, it's used with يُؤْمِنُ (believes) to show believing in something.

steps.m: with/by it/him (referring to what يُؤْمِنُ believes in)
steps.n: Added pronoun ه meaning 'him/it' to show what is being believed in
Added small alif (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation guide to show the هِ is connected to what follows
Basic meaning of nurturing and lordship
The root letters combine to make a word meaning 'one who takes care of others', with the doubled middle letter making it stronger

steps.m: your lord (subject of أَعْلَمُ - most knowing)
steps.n: The كَ shows that this is your lord (speaking to one male person). رَبُّكَ is the subject (doer) of the verb أَعْلَمُ (knows/is more knowing).

steps.m: and your lord (subject of أَعْلَمُ - most knowing)
steps.n: Adding conjunction prefix وَ to connect this with previous phrase
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning and ُ at the end to show this is the highest degree of knowing. In this ayah, Allah is describing His complete knowledge of those who spread corruption.
Basic meaning of corruption

steps.m: someone who spoils or damages things
steps.n: Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to show who does the action

steps.m: people who spoil or damage things
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people. This word is being affected by the word أعلم (most knowing) in the sentence.

steps.m: the people who spoil or damage things
steps.n: Added ال at the start to point to specific people, like saying 'the' in English

steps.m: with the people who spoil or damage things (object of أَعْلَمُ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the start to show 'with'. This whole phrase tells us what Allah is 'most knowing' about.
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition

steps.m: and if (followed by كَذَّبُوكَ - they deny you)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter makes the meaning intensive - changing from general denial to strong rejection
The وا ending shows this is a group doing the action in the past
The كَ at the end shows you are the one being denied. This word connects to فَقُل (then say) in the ayah, showing what happened before the command to speak.
The middle letter و is dropped because it's a weak letter. Added damma (ُ) on ق and sukun (ْ) on ل to make it a command.

steps.m: then say! (responding to وَإِن كَذَّبُوكَ - and if they deny you)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a response to the previous statement 'and if they deny you.' The فَ here means 'then' or 'so' as a logical result.
Basic combination of the preposition ل (for/to) with the pronoun ي (me/my)

steps.m: for me (connected to عَمَلِى - my deeds)
steps.n: The ل gets a shadda because it comes after the word فَقُل in the ayah, which ends with a similar sound. This is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to work, to act, to do

steps.n: Adding vowels creates the basic past tense verb
Adds ى to show possession ('my'). This word pairs with عَمَلُكُمْ ('your deed') in the next part of the ayah to contrast 'my deed' vs 'your deed'
Added fatha to the basic preposition ل to make it readable
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: and for you all (connected to عَمَلُكُمْ - your deeds)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being addressed. This connects to عَمَلُكُمْ (your deeds) in the ayah.

steps.m: to work, to act, to do

steps.n: Adding tanween dammah makes it 'a deed' instead of 'the deed'
Lost the -un ending to connect with next word (shows 'deed of' instead of just 'a deed')

steps.m: your (plural) deed/action (in contrast to the Prophet's deed mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show plural possession. This word contrasts with عَمَلِى (my deed) mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation
Added م at the end to change from singular 'you' to plural 'you all'. This word is the subject of بَرِيٓـُٔونَ (innocent/free) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be free from, to have nothing to do with

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha and kasra) to make 'he was free from' in past tense

steps.m: one who is free from/innocent of
steps.n: Added ي to make it mean 'someone who is free/innocent' instead of 'he was free'

steps.m: ones who are free from/innocent of (referring to multiple people)
steps.n: Added ون to show this refers to multiple people, with َ at the end because it follows أَنتُم (you all) in the ayah
Combined the two basic words مِن (from) and مَا (what) together. The ن gets a sukun when joined.
When ن meets م, the ن sound blends into the م sound, shown by doubling the م with a shadda.
The final alif is lengthened to show it's at the end of a phrase. This word connects to بَرِىٓءٌ (free from) in this ayah.

steps.m: to work, to act, to do

steps.n: Added fathas to make basic past tense verb

steps.m: I do/work/act (in response to كَذَّبُوكَ - they denied you)
steps.n: Changed from 'he did' to 'I do' by adding أَ at start and changing the vowels. This is responding to 'they denied you'
This is the basic first person singular pronoun meaning 'I'
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it
Added a small alif (۠) above the final alif. This is just a pronunciation helper and doesn't change the meaning. This word is the subject of بَرِىٓءٌ (innocent) in this ayah.

steps.m: to be free from blame or responsibility

steps.n: Root letters connected with vowels to make a word meaning 'being free from something'
Added ي to make it stronger - like saying 'totally innocent' instead of just 'free from blame'
Added tanwin (double damma) to show this is describing a quality in general terms
Starting with the two separate words مِن (from) and مَا (what) that will combine together
When مِن comes before a word starting with م, its ن combines with the م to make a stronger م sound (shown by ّ)
Added extra emphasis to the first م to show it's strongly connected to the word before it (وَأَنَا۠ بَرِىٓءٌ)
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
The تَ at the start shows 'you' are doing it, and the word changed to show it's happening now (present) instead of before (past)
The ونَ ending shows multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: from (connects to يَسْتَمِعُونَ - those who listen)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' that introduces who is doing the listening mentioned later in the ayah
Added 'them' (masculine plural) to refer to a group of people being discussed. The sukun (ْ) is added before هُم to help pronunciation.

steps.m: and from them (those who listen to you)
steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning to connect with previous statement. This introduces a new group of people being described.
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to those who listen.
The م gets a shadda (doubling mark) because it comes after وَ in وَمِنْهُم. This is just for smooth pronunciation and doesn't change the meaning.
This is the basic meaning of hearing or listening
Adding the vowel marks changes it from a root to a complete past tense verb meaning 'he heard'
Added است at the beginning to show actively seeking to listen
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing final vowel to ُ

steps.m: they seek to listen (to إِلَيْكَ/to you)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This verb connects to إِلَيْكَ (to you) in the ayah.
This is a basic word that shows direction or movement towards something. It's like pointing an arrow to show where something is going.

steps.m: to you (object of يَسْتَمِعُونَ - they listen)
steps.n: Added ك at the end to show this is happening to 'you'. In this ayah, some people are listening to you - so you are receiving their listening action.
This is the basic pronoun for 'you' (masculine singular)
Added فَ to show this follows from the previous statement. This word is the subject of تُسْمِعُ (you make hear) in this ayah.
Added أَ at the beginning to make this a question. This questions whether 'you' can make the deaf hear.
Changed vowels to make it present tense form before adding prefix
Adding تُ changes it from 'hear' to 'you make hear'
This root relates to deafness or being unable to hear
Added a shadda (double letter) and fatha marks to make it mean 'he became deaf'
Changed from 'became deaf' (verb) to 'deaf ones' (plural noun) by changing the vowel mark

steps.m: the deaf ones (who are being addressed in أَفَأَنتَ تُسْمِعُ - can you make hear)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) to make it specific and a fatha mark at the end because it's receiving the action of 'making hear'
This is a conditional particle showing a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it's used to emphasize that even in an impossible situation (making the deaf hear), the outcome would still be the same.

steps.m: and even if (connecting to يَسْتَمِعُونَ - they listen)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement about listening. It shows that even if they could hear, they still wouldn't understand.
This shows the basic concept of being or existing
Added vowel marks to make it mean 'he was' - this is the past tense form of the basic word meaning 'to be'
Changed و to ا because it's a hollow verb (has weak middle letter)
Added وا at the end to show that 'they' (a group) did the action instead of just 'he'
This is the basic negative particle that negates the verb يَعْقِلُونَ (they understand) that follows it
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
This root relates to using one's intellect or reason
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
This is the base word meaning 'from' - it shows something coming from or originating from something else
Added هُم at the end to show who is being referred to (them)

steps.m: and from them (who يَنظُرُ look at you)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This word refers to some of the people who look at the Prophet, as shown by يَنظُرُ (look) in the rest of the ayah.
Basic relative pronoun that introduces a description of people
Added emphasis by doubling the م with shadda. This word introduces the people who are looking at the Prophet (يَنظُرُ إِلَيْكَ - looking at you).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ marks. In this ayah, this word connects to إِلَيْكَ showing who is being looked at.
This is a preposition showing direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix
Added ك at the end to show who the action is directed to. This word connects to يَنظُرُ (looks) to show who is being looked at.
Added vowel marks to the basic pronoun. This word is the subject (doer) of تَهْدِى (guide) in this ayah.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement
Added أَ at the beginning to make this a question
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end.

steps.m: you guide (addressing the Prophet ﷺ regarding الْعُمْىَ - the blind)
steps.n: Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' (present tense). Changed middle vowel to sukun and last vowel to kasra for present tense form. This is addressing Prophet Muhammad ﷺ about his ability to guide those who are spiritually blind.
Added vowels (fatha, kasra, fatha) to make it mean 'he became blind' in the past tense
Changed from 'became blind' (past tense verb) to 'blind ones' (plural noun) by changing vowels and making it plural
Added ٱلْ to make it definite and ى with fatha to show it's the object being acted upon. This word is receiving the action of تَهْدِى (you guide) in this ayah.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation - fatha on ل and sukun on و
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Added the three basic vowels (َ) to connect the letters together and make it mean 'he' in the past
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of word (called a hollow verb) where this always happens in the past tense
Added وا at the end to change 'he was' to 'they were'. This word is connected to لَا يُبْصِرُونَ (they do not see) in the ayah, describing the same group of people.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it (يُبْصِرُونَ - they see) into its opposite meaning (they do not see). It's like adding 'not' in English.
Added fatha mark (َ) on ل to show how it's pronounced (la).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he saw) to present tense (he sees) by adding يُ at the start - like changing 'saw' to 'sees' in English

steps.m: they see (referring to those who look at you)
steps.n: Changes from singular (he) to plural (they). This refers back to the people mentioned earlier in the ayah who look at you.
This is a special word that always comes at the start of a sentence to make what follows more emphatic
Adding the shadda makes this word stronger. It tells us to pay special attention to what comes next - in this case that Allah does not wrong people at all.
Added ال to make it specific and unique. When used for this word specifically, it becomes the proper name Allah, referring to The One True God.
The word 'Allah' got a stronger L sound (shadda), a special beginning A (wasl), and an -a ending because it comes after 'inna' (indeed)
This is a negative particle that comes before verbs to say something is not happening. In this ayah, it's saying Allah does not wrong people.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Added basic past tense form by connecting letters with fatha
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing vowels, and adding ُ at end. This word comes after لَا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'does not wrong'.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
steps.n: This changes from the idea of being social to mean 'people' in general
The root أنس became the word 'people' (ناس), then added emphasis with shadda on ن
Added ٱل to show we're talking about specific people
Added َ at the end to show the people are being wronged in the phrase 'لَا يَظْلِمُ ٱلنَّاسَ' (does not wrong the people)
This becomes a noun meaning 'a thing' or 'something'
Added vowels to the root letters to make the common noun meaning 'thing'

steps.m: anything (being acted upon by يَظْلِمُ - wronging)
steps.n: The word 'thing' gets an extra mark (tanween) at the end to show it's receiving the action of wronging - like saying 'a thing' became 'a thing being wronged'
This is the basic form of the word that shows contrast between ideas. The alif after ل is written as a long alif (لٰ) in this style.
Adding ّ (shadda) with َ (fatha) at the end makes the contrast stronger, like saying 'but indeed' instead of just 'but'

steps.m: and but indeed (connects contrasting statement to previous ayah)
steps.n: Adding وَ at the start connects this contrasting statement to what came before it. In this ayah, it connects 'Allah does not wrong people at all' with 'but indeed, the people wrong themselves'

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun for people by adding a long 'aa' sound. The first letter (hamza) drops because it's not needed anymore.
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ن letter to help pronunciation

steps.m: the people (being acted upon by يَظْلِمُ - wrongs/oppresses)
steps.n: Added a mark at the end to show these people are receiving the action of wronging/oppressing in the phrase 'لَا يَظْلِمُ ٱلنَّاسَ' (does not wrong the people)
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun meaning 'self'. This word is being acted upon in the phrase 'they wrong themselves'.
Changes the singular 'self' into a plural by adding hamza at start and changing middle vowel
Added هُمْ at the end to show these are 'their' selves, with َ before it because this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Added vowels to make it past tense 'he wronged' from the root meaning 'to wrong'
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word appears after لا (not) in the ayah, so it means 'they do not wrong'
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun
Added و with fatha to connect this word to what comes before it

steps.m: and (on) the day (when يَحْشُرُهُمْ/He will gather them)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is describing when something happens. This word connects to يَحْشُرُهُمْ (He will gather them) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being gathered. This pronoun refers to those who will be gathered on the Day of Judgment.
This is the lighter form of أَنَّ without the shadda, which changes the meaning slightly but keeps the basic idea of connecting thoughts
Adding كَ at the beginning changes the meaning from just connecting thoughts to making a comparison, like saying something is similar to something else
Basic negative particle that makes verbs past tense
Added emphasis with shadda. This word negates the verb يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ (they remained) making it mean 'they did not remain'
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people doing the action
This is a basic exception word that means 'except for' or 'only'. It's connecting to the word ساعةً (hour) to say 'except an hour'.
Added the vowel marks to show exactly how to pronounce it: 'il-laa'
The middle letter و changes to ا when we add vowels. This is a common change with weak letters.

steps.m: a period of time, an hour
steps.n: Adding ة makes this into a specific unit of time

steps.m: an hour (object of يَلْبَثُوا - they remained)
steps.n: The ً shows this is the object being acted upon in the phrase 'they remained not except an hour'
This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or that something is part of something else
Added emphasis by doubling the م letter with a shadda
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word النَّهَارِ (the day), making it mean 'from the day'
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Added alif to create noun for time period when light flows/shines
Added ال to make it specific and shadda on ن because it comes after ال

steps.m: of the daytime (possessed by/part of something)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this is possessed by or part of something (in this case, showing it's 'of the daytime')
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and ا after first syllable to show people doing the action to each other (like 'they got to know one another')
Present tense prefix 'ya' added to show ongoing action of recognizing each other

steps.m: they recognize each other (present tense, doer)
steps.n: Changes ending to -u sound to show this is the doer of the action

steps.m: they recognize one another (referring to the people being gathered on that Day)
steps.n: Adds plural marker to show multiple people recognizing each other. This refers back to the people mentioned in يَحْشُرُهُمْ (He gathers them) earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to be between or separate things

steps.n: Adding vowels changes this from a root into a usable word

steps.m: between them (referring to those who denied meeting Allah)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this means 'between them'. The 'them' refers back to those mentioned in the ayah who denied meeting Allah.
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle

steps.m: indeed/already (emphasizes يَحْشُرُهُمْ - He will gather them)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on ق and sukun (ْ) on د. In this ayah, it emphasizes the certainty of Allah gathering people on the Day of Judgment.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb. This word comes after قَدْ which emphasizes that the loss has definitely occurred.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun meaning 'who'
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال to make it specific, like saying 'those' instead of just 'who'
Added final vowel mark and shadda for proper pronunciation. This word is the subject of كَذَّبُوا۟ (they denied) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows doing something with intensity or repeatedly. This word is part of what those who lost (خَسِرَ) did.
Changes from 'he' to 'they' by adding وا at the end
Adding vowel marks to show this is a past tense verb meaning 'he met'
Changed the verb into a noun that means the action itself
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) which comes after it in the ayah
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is done 'with' or 'by' something
Changed to the specific name for the One True God by adding ال and merging letters
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the word بِلِقَآءِ (meeting with). Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasl form.
Basic negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
Adding the fatha (َ) makes it a past tense verb
Adding the second fatha (َ) completes the past tense verb pattern
Added وا at the end to show 'they' (multiple people) did the action in the past. This is the past tense verb that goes with يَتَعَارَفُونَ (they recognize each other) later in the sentence.

steps.m: to guide or show the way

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters into 'he guided' by adding the short 'a' sounds (َ) and ending with an alif
Added مُ at start and ت in middle to change from 'doing the guiding' to 'receiving guidance' - like changing from teacher to student
Added ين to show multiple people and to show this is affected by كانوا (were) in the ayah
The base word combines إِنْ (if) and مَا (what/ever) where the ن merges into م creating a shadda (double letter mark)
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed رَأَيَ to نُرِي by adding نُ at start to mean 'we', and changed middle vowels. The ي at end comes from original word meaning 'to see/show'
Added نَّ at the end to add emphasis and future meaning
Added كَ at the end to show who is being shown to
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to create the basic noun
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb نُرِيَنَّكَ (We show you)

steps.m: the one who/which (connects نَعِدُهُمْ with what comes before)
steps.n: Added ٱلَّ at the beginning to make it definite. This word connects what Allah promises them with the previous part of the sentence.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Changed from 'he' to 'we' by adding ن at start, and middle letter changed to match present tense pattern
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being promised to
This is a coordinating conjunction that connects two possible outcomes: either showing the Prophet some of what was promised, or causing him to pass away. It gives a choice between these two options.
Adding shadda makes the taking/collecting more complete and intensive
Added 'ta' at start shows doing the action to oneself or receiving the action thoroughly
Adds subject pronoun 'we' to the verb
The suffix 'نَّ' adds emphasis, making the statement more certain.

steps.m: we will surely take you completely
steps.n: The suffix ك means 'you' (when speaking to one male)
Starting with the basic preposition إِلَى which means movement or direction towards something
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else
Changed the final ى to يْ to prepare for adding the next part
Added نَا at the end to show this is happening to 'us'

steps.m: place or time of return (subject of شَهِيدٌ)
steps.n: Added مَ at start to mean 'place of' and changed vowels to make it 'the place of return' - this is what شَهِيدٌ (witness) is watching over
Added هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them. This whole word is what Allah is witness over in this ayah.
This is a basic connecting word (particle) that shows one thing happens after another, with some time in between. It's like saying 'after that' or 'later' in English.

steps.m: then (connecting وَإِمَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ with ٱللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ)
steps.n: Added vowel marks to show how to pronounce it correctly. In this ayah, it connects two ideas: 'Whether We show you some of what We promise them or We take your soul' with 'Allah is witness over what they do'.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it definite, the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the ُ shows this is the subject of the sentence.

steps.m: to witness or observe something directly

steps.n: Added vowels (َ ِ َ) to the root letters to make it mean 'he witnessed' in the past
Added ي between second and third root letters to make it mean 'one who does the action'

steps.m: a witness (of ما يَفْعَلُونَ - what they do)
steps.n: Added tanween damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the witnessing. In this ayah, Allah is witnessing what people do.
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى changes this from a concept into a preposition that shows one thing is above another

steps.m: upon, on, over (what يَفْعَلُونَ they do)
steps.n: The small alif (ٰ) is added to show this is the standard way of writing this word in the Quran. It connects to the word يَفْعَلُونَ (they do) that comes after it.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that connects to the verb يَفْعَلُونَ (they do) in this ayah. It asks about or refers to the things that are being done.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is past tense
Changes from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels inside the word

steps.m: they do/perform (referring to what they do in this ayah)
steps.n: The ونَ ending shows it's they (masculine plural) who are doing the action

steps.m: to be whole, complete, or include everything

steps.n: Doubling the middle letter makes it stronger - changes from 'complete' to 'all/every'

steps.m: for every/all (shows this applies to each individual thing)
steps.n: Added لِ at the beginning to show this is being given or applied to something

steps.m: and for every (connects to أُمَّةٍ - nation/community)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to what came before. This word connects to أُمَّةٍ (nation/community) that comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to lead or guide as a group

steps.n: Root أمم becomes plural noun أُمم (nations) by adding damma vowels

steps.m: a community or nation (as a unified group)
steps.n: Changed from plural 'nations' to singular 'nation' by removing extra meem and adding feminine ending
Added tanwin kasra to show this word belongs to كُلِّ (every/each) in the phrase وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ (and for every nation)
Adding ا and و to the root makes it mean 'someone who delivers messages as their job'
Adding tanween (double damma) makes it mean 'a messenger' instead of 'the messenger'
Cannot add shadda since it would change the word's meaning incorrectly
This is a word that means 'when' and introduces a future condition. In this ayah, it connects to the verb جَآءَ (came) to show when something happened.
The فَ prefix shows this is connected to what came before. It links the previous statement about every nation having a messenger to what happens when that messenger comes.
Added short 'a' sounds (fatha marks) to make it mean 'he came' in the past
Changed to hollow verb pattern with alif, a common change for verbs with weak middle letters
The alif is lengthened to show emphasis or intensity of the action of coming
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added هُمْ at the end to show the messenger belongs to them. This messenger is the subject of قُضِىَ (it was judged/decided)

steps.m: to decide or settle something definitively

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to something rather than something doing it
Changed the ي to ى because it comes at the end of the word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Forms a preposition meaning 'between'. This word shows where the judgment (قُضِىَ) happens.
Added هُم at the end to show who is being judged between

steps.m: to judge fairly, to be just

steps.n: Root meaning 'being fair' becomes a noun meaning 'fairness' - like changing 'to be fair' into 'justice'
Adding the definite article makes the noun definite
Added 'bi' (with/by) at start + changed ending to match
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' (for masculine plural). It's the subject of the verb لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (they are not wronged) in this ayah.
The وَ prefix connects this word to what came before it, adding the meaning 'and'
This is a negative particle that negates the verb that follows it (يُظْلَمُونَ - they are wronged), making it mean 'they are not wronged'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The passive form shows someone is being wronged
Changes from past tense (was wronged) to present tense (is being wronged) by adding يُ at the start. Both show someone receiving the action of wronging.
The ونَ suffix indicates masculine plural subject in indicative mood
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form. The middle و changes to ا which is common with this type of word.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا changes back to و.
Adding ونَ shows that a group of people (three or more) are doing the action

steps.m: and they say/are saying (followed by مَتَىٰ هٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ - when is this promise?)
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to what came before. This word introduces a question that follows.
This is a basic question word used to ask about time. It is not derived from a root.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. The word is asking about the timing of الْوَعْدُ (the promise) mentioned after it.
Added the small alif (ٰ) after ى which helps show proper pronunciation but doesn't change the meaning.
Base demonstrative word indicating something being pointed to
Added ها (attention marker) which is shortened to هٰ to specify something near
Added vowel marks to show proper pronunciation. This word is what 'they' are saying in يَقُولُونَ (they say)
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make it a noun. The fatha on و and sukun on ع show this is a noun meaning 'a promise'
Adding ٱلْ makes it talk about a specific promise that was mentioned or understood
