Adding ُ shows this word is the subject being asked about in the question 'When is this promise?'
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'if' with 'you are truthful' (كُنتُمْ صٰدِقِينَ) to form a complete conditional statement.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he existed'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a hollow verb (has a weak middle letter)
Changed vowels and added تُ to show 'I' did the action in the past
Changed from 'I' to 'you all' by changing تُ to تُمْ at the end to show we're talking to multiple people
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra on second letter to show someone who does the action
Changed the alif to an alif with overbar (صٰ) - this is just a writing variation that means the same thing

steps.m: truthful ones (describing 'you were' in إِن كُنتُمْ)
steps.n: Added ين with a kasra to show this describes multiple people. This word describes the 'you' in كُنتُمْ (you were).
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by adding damma on first letter. The middle weak letter و is dropped, which is common in commands. This is Allah telling Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to say something.
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added shadda to make the 'no' stronger and more emphatic
Changed the alif to an extended alif (آ) to show lengthened pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Changed from past tense (he possessed) to present tense (I possess) by changing middle vowel from 'a' to 'i'
Added أَ at beginning to show 'I' am doing the action
Added vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'self'
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is what is being controlled/possessed by the verb أَمْلِكُ (I possess/control)
Added ي with kasra to show this means 'my' or 'myself'. The whole phrase means 'I do not possess/control for myself' in the ayah.
Adding the short 'a' vowels (fatHa) turns the root letters into a past tense verb meaning 'he harmed'
The verb 'he harmed' becomes the noun 'harm' and gets tanween (double line ending) to show it's the object - the thing being controlled/possessed
This is a basic negation word that says 'no' or 'not' to something
Added و at the beginning to connect this negation with the previous phrase. This word negates the verb يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (they advance) in this ayah.
Changed the action into a noun by adding vowel marks. This makes it mean the actual benefit itself rather than the act of benefiting.
Added ًا at the end to show this is the thing being controlled in the phrase 'I do not control any benefit.' This connects to the verb أَمْلِكُ (I control/possess).
This is a basic particle used for making exceptions. It's not derived from a root but starts as a complete word.
Adding the shadda and alif makes the exception stronger and connects it to what follows. In this ayah, it's connecting to 'what Allah wills' (ما شاء الله).
This is a relative pronoun that connects with the verb شَآءَ (wills/wishes) to mean 'whatever Allah wills'

steps.m: to want or wish for something

steps.n: Added vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he wanted'
The weak letter changes cause the hamza to move to the end after alif
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name for The One True God by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda (ّ) shows the doubled letter.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of شَآءَ - willed/wished)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject (doer) of the verb شَآءَ (willed/wished) in this ayah. The ٱ is a special way of writing the alif at the start.
The doubled ل (with shadda) makes it mean 'every single one' without exception
Adds لِ meaning 'for' to show this word connects to أُمَّةٍ (nation/community) in the ayah
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is connected to the word after it (أُمَّةٍ meaning nation/community)

steps.m: to lead or guide (as a mother or leader does)

steps.n: The root أمم becomes plural أُمم (nations) by adding the damma marks - like how we make groups from single things

steps.m: a community or nation (like children of one mother)
steps.n: Changed from plural (nations) to singular (one nation) by removing extra م and adding ة at the end

steps.m: of a community/nation (possessed by لِكُلِّ - for every)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this word belongs to something else (in this case, it belongs to the phrase 'for every' - لِكُلِّ)
Added fatha marks to connect the letters and create a noun from the root
Added tanween dammah to show this is the subject of جَآءَ (came) in this ayah
This is a basic word that refers to a point in time
Adding ا makes it more general and conditional. In this ayah, it connects to the word جَآءَ (comes) to mean 'whenever [their term] comes'
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he came' in the past
Changed to hollow verb pattern by adding alif after first letter
Extended the alif to show emphasis/intensity of coming
Added vowels to make it a noun meaning the time or deadline itself
Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added هُمْ to show this deadline belongs to them. This word is the subject of جَآءَ (it came/arrived) in this ayah.
This is the basic negation word that says 'no' or 'not' to something

steps.m: then/so not (connects to يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ - they delay)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement. It connects to the next word يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ (they delay), showing the result or consequence.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added يَسْتَ at the beginning to show seeking or requesting the action

steps.m: they seek to delay (referring to people's inability to delay their appointed time)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word appears in a context talking about how people cannot delay their appointed time when it comes.
The middle letter و changes to ا, which is common in Arabic words
Adding ة makes it into a specific instance or unit of time
The ً (tanween) shows this is the object being acted upon in the sentence
Basic negation particle that says 'no' or 'not' to something. Here it's saying 'not' to having benefit/نَفْعًا.

steps.m: and not (connects to ضَرًّا/harm before it)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous phrase about harm (ضَرًّا). It's like saying 'neither harm NOR benefit'.
Added vowels to connect the root letters together
Added است at the beginning to show trying/seeking to do something (like asking permission)
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they try to go forward (in relation to سَاعَةً/an hour)
steps.n: Added ون to show many people doing the action. In this ayah, it's talking about people trying to move ahead of their appointed time (ساعة)

steps.m: to say, to tell, to speak
say! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers)
steps.n: The middle weak letter و is dropped in the command form. This is Allah commanding Prophet Muhammad ﷺ to speak to the disbelievers about what would happen if Allah's punishment came to them.
Connected the root letters ر-أ-ي and added vowels to make 'he saw' in the past tense. The ي at the end changes to ى
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question
Added تَ at the end to change it from 'he' to 'you'
Changed from 'did you see?' (asking one person) to 'did you all see?' (asking many people) by adding تُمْ at the end
This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical situation. Here it sets up the condition 'if His punishment comes to you' that the rest of the ayah builds upon.
Root letters got fatha vowels to make 'he came' in past tense
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end because this is a weak verb ending in ي
Added كُمْ at the end to show who was come to - you all
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from an action (punishing) to a thing (punishment). The middle letter gets longer by adding an alif (ا). This is a common pattern for turning verbs into nouns.
Added damma to show this word is doing the action in the sentence
Added هُ at the end to show the punishment belongs to him
Root letters form noun meaning 'house' - place where people spend nights
Changes from 'house' to 'spending the night' - shows action related to night

steps.m: at night (describing when عَذَابُهُۥ/His punishment comes)
steps.n: Added tanween to make it work as an adverb of time. This word describes when His punishment might come in the ayah.
This is a connecting word that gives a choice between two times: 'by night' (بَيٰتًا) or 'by day' (نَهَارًا). It helps ask about which time Allah's punishment might come.
The flowing brightness of the root becomes 'daylight' - like how daylight flows across the sky

steps.m: during daytime (when Allah's punishment might come)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word answers 'when?' about Allah's punishment possibly coming
This is the basic interrogative particle used to ask questions about things
Adding ذا makes the question more specific and emphatic, like asking 'what exactly?'

steps.m: what (more emphatic) (asking about what يَسْتَعْجِلُ - they seek to hasten)
steps.n: The shadda on م shows it connects smoothly with the previous word in speech. This word is asking about what the wrongdoers are trying to hasten.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or wanting to do the action

steps.m: he seeks to hasten (subject of يَسْتَعْجِلُ)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding ُ at end
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'

steps.m: from it/him (referring to عَذَابُهُ - His punishment)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show what/who is being referred to. Here it refers back to 'His punishment' (عَذَابُهُ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action
Added ون to show multiple wrongdoers
Added ٱلْ to make it specific - these particular wrongdoers
This is a basic conjunction particle that connects events in sequence, like saying 'and then' in English

steps.m: and then? (expressing disbelief about what comes after إِذَا)
steps.n: Adding أَ at the beginning turns this into a question expressing surprise or disbelief. In this ayah, it's questioning the timing of belief that comes after إِذَا (when)

steps.m: when (followed by مَا وَقَعَ - it happens)
steps.n: This is a time word that introduces something that will happen in the future. In this ayah, it connects to مَا وَقَعَ (it happens) to show when people believe.
This is an interrogative particle used here to ask about something that occurred. In this ayah, it's asking about believing after something happened.
This is the basic past tense form meaning 'it happened'
Added fathas to connect the root letters. This forms the basic past tense verb that comes after إِذَا (when) in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what comes before it, showing sequence of events
This form changes the meaning to 'believe' or 'trust in'
Adding alif after first letter makes it mean 'to believe/trust'
Added تُم at the end to show that many people (you all) did this action in the past
Returns vowel to original fatha sound
Basic preposition showing how something is done. Here it connects to the verb تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (you hasten) to show what they're hastening with.

steps.m: with/by it (referring to العَذَاب/punishment mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هِ which refers back to the punishment they were asking about

steps.m: with/by it (referring to العَذَاب/punishment mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added ۦٓ which is a pronunciation mark showing the هِ should be clear and elongated when reading
This word means 'the present moment'
The و changes to ا and combines with the أ to form آ, making a word for the present moment
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific present moment
Adding the questioning hamza at the beginning changes this into a surprised or disbelieving question, like saying 'Now?! (of all times)'
This is a particle that emphasizes that something definitely happened or has been happening
Adding وَ connects this emphasis to the previous statement, showing these things happened together
The middle letter و drops out because it's a weak letter. This is a common pattern in Arabic verbs with و in the middle.
Added ت to show past tense and second person (you)

steps.m: you (all) were (referring to the disbelievers being addressed in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ُم to show that it's addressing multiple people. This word is part of a question asking the disbelievers about their previous actions.
Added kasra (ِ) to show this connects something to what follows it
Added هِ to show who/what is being referred to. This connects to the word ءَامَنتُم (you believed), showing what was believed in.
Added a small mark (ۦ) after هِ to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to show seeking or wanting to do the action
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, changing middle vowel to kasra, and adding ُ at end
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the beginning
Added ونَ to show that multiple people are being addressed

steps.m: then (connecting what follows to what came before)
steps.n: This word connects events in sequence, showing that one thing happens after another. In this ayah, it connects to what was said before about punishment.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Adding ي to complete passive voice pattern
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ال and doubling the ذ makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'
Adding ين at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding لِ (li) at the start means 'for/to those who' - it connects to ظَلَمُوا۟ to show who is being referred to
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb for one person
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action. This word is connected to قِيلَ (it was said) in this ayah, showing who was being spoken to.
Basic command form telling one person to taste

steps.m: taste! (command to many people) (referring to عَذَابَ - punishment)
steps.n: Changes command from singular to plural, telling multiple people to taste. In this ayah, they are being told to taste the punishment (عَذَابَ).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding alif after the second root letter changes the verb into a noun meaning the result of the action. This word is what is being 'tasted' (metaphorically) in the phrase 'taste the punishment'
Changed the root into a word describing the quality of lasting forever
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it describes the word عَذَابَ (punishment) from earlier in the ayah
This is a question word that comes at the start of yes/no questions. In this ayah, it's asking if people are being repaid for anything except what they used to do.

steps.m: to repay or give what is due

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to passive voice by adding تُ at start and changing vowels - now means 'being repaid' instead of 'repaying'

steps.m: you all are being repaid (for ما كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ - what you used to earn)
steps.n: Added ون at the end to show this is happening to multiple people. This word relates to what these people used to do (كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ) in the ayah.
This is the basic negative particle that forms part of the word

Adding إِ at the beginning and a shadda (ّ) on the ل changes this from a negative word to one that means 'except'. In this ayah, it shows that people will only be rewarded based on what they earned - nothing else.
This word connects to تَكْسِبُونَ (you earn) to mean 'what you earn' or 'that which you earn'
Adding بِ shows we're talking about the reason or cause - in this context, it means they are being punished because of what they earned through their actions
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The middle weak letter و drops out in certain forms, with vowel changes to compensate

steps.m: you all were (referring to those who did wrong)
steps.n: Added تُمْ at the end to change 'be' into 'you all were' in the past tense - like adding 'ed' in English
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ prefix to show 'you' (present tense), changed middle vowel to kasra (i sound)

steps.m: you (all) earn/are earning (referring to what you used to do)
steps.n: Added ون suffix to show multiple people. This word relates to كُنتُمْ (you used to be) in the ayah, showing what they used to do in the past.
Added vowel marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he gave news'
Adds 'asking for' or 'seeking' to the meaning - like asking someone to give you news
Adding ي transforms the verb from past to present tense, indicating 3rd person masculine singular
Adding ون shows multiple people are doing the action
The ك (ka) at the end means 'you' - they are asking YOU for information. Without the ك it just means 'they ask'

steps.m: and they ask you for news/information (about أَحَقٌّ - is it true?)
steps.n: The و connects this to what came before. They are asking about whether something is true (أَحَقٌّ) as shown in the next part of the ayah.
Added vowels and doubled the last letter to make it mean 'it became true/real'

steps.m: more/most true (asking about the truth of هُوَ)
steps.n: Added أَ at the beginning to make it comparative. This word is asking about whether something (هُوَ - 'it') is true.
Added double dammah at the end to show this is indefinite and in the nominative case
This is a standalone pronoun that refers back to the word حَقٌّ (truth) mentioned in the question أَحَقٌّ
Added the vowel marks ُ (damma) on ه and َ (fatha) on و to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'huwa'
Changed from basic root 'to say' into a command by adding damma (u sound) - like telling someone 'say this!'
The middle letter و is removed to make it shorter when giving a command

steps.m: yes indeed (affirming أَحَقٌّ - is it true?)
steps.n: This is a word used for strong affirmation, answering the question being asked. It's connected to أَحَقٌّ (is it true?) in this ayah.
Root letters combined with a fatha and doubled middle letter to make 'lord/master'
Added ي (yaa) at the end to mean 'my', with kasra before it to connect the words smoothly
Added و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it
Word is already complete with prefix وَ and possessive ي suffix
This is an emphasis word that makes what comes after it stronger. It's like adding 'definitely' or 'certainly' in English.

steps.m: indeed it/he (referring to أَحَقٌّ - is it true)
steps.n: The هُ refers back to أَحَقٌّ (is it true) from earlier in the ayah. It's like saying 'indeed it' where 'it' refers back to the question about truth.

steps.m: indeed it/he (referring to أَحَقٌّ - is it true)
steps.n: The ۥ mark is just a writing helper that shows the هُ is pronounced clearly. It doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: surely truth (answer to أَحَقٌّ/is it true?)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to emphasize that something is definitely true. This word answers the question أَحَقٌّ (is it true?) in the ayah.
Added tanween dammah (double dammah) at the end to make it indefinite

steps.m: not (negating the ability to escape in أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ)
steps.n: This is a negative particle that makes something not true. Here it's saying people cannot escape.

steps.m: and not (negating the ability to escape in أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ)
steps.n: The و connects this negative statement to what came before it

steps.m: and not (negating the ability to escape in أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ)
steps.n: The alif is written with a long mark (آ) to show it's pronounced clearly at the end of the word
This is the basic singular masculine form of the pronoun 'you'
Adding م with damma at the end changes this from addressing one person to addressing many people (all male or mixed group)
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Creates a word for someone who causes the action to happen to others

steps.m: those who weaken/make unable (describes أنتم - you all)
steps.n: Makes the word plural to match 'you all' (أنتم) in the verse

steps.m: able to escape/overcome (lit: among those who can make [something] impossible)
steps.n: Adds 'bi' at start to mean 'among/with' and adds 'na' at end to make it negative - meaning you CANNOT escape/overcome
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios - things that could happen but haven't happened yet.
Adding وَ (and) at the beginning connects this condition to what came before it. When وَ combines with لَوْ, it adds emphasis, changing the meaning from just 'if' to 'even if'.
Basic particle that introduces new information or clauses
Adding shadda makes it stronger and more emphatic. In this ayah, it emphasizes the certainty that every soul that did wrong would want to ransom itself.
The doubled ل shows this means absolutely everything with no exceptions
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' every soul in the verse
Added kasra at the end because it's connected to the word نَفْسٍ (soul) that comes after it
Connected the root letters with fatha and sukun to make the basic noun meaning 'soul' or 'self'
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word belongs to كُلِّ (every)

steps.m: to do wrong or act unjustly to someone

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha marks to make a past tense verb. This word is part of the condition that leads to لَٱفْتَدَتْ (would ransom itself) in this ayah.
Adding ت at the end changes 'he' to 'she'. Here it refers to نَفْسٍ (soul) which is feminine in Arabic.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'whatever' in this context. It refers to all possessions or wealth that might be in the earth, which the wrongdoers would offer as ransom.
Basic preposition showing something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it shows that what's being discussed is 'in/on the earth'.
Adding short vowels (fat-ha and sukoon) to spell out the basic word for earth/land

steps.m: the earth (what is in مَا فِى - what is in)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning and kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Form VIII verb indicating doing action for oneself
لَ adds strong emphasis/confirmation to the verb

steps.m: surely she would ransom herself (referring to نَفْسٍ - soul)
steps.n: The ت shows this refers to the feminine subject نَفْسٍ (soul) mentioned earlier in the verse
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'

steps.m: with/by it (referring to مَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ - what is in the earth)
steps.n: Added pronoun ه (it/him) to show what is being referred to. Here it refers back to everything that is in the earth mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added small alif (ۦ) after ه as a pronunciation helper to show the هِ should be clearly pronounced

steps.m: to hide or keep something private

steps.n: Adding shadda makes the meaning more intense and direct

steps.m: to make someone happy, to cause happiness
steps.n: Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'

steps.m: and they kept (something) secret (acts on النَّدَامَةَ)

Added vowels to connect the root letters and create the basic past tense verb
Added feminine ending ة to create abstract noun indicating state/condition
Added ال to make the noun definite. The ن gets a shadda because it comes after ال
Adding a small line (fatha) at the end shows this word is being acted upon
Basic negative particle used for past tense negation
Adding shadda on the م changes this from a negative particle to a temporal one, showing when something happened
Adding ا at the end completes the temporal conjunction. This word connects to رَأَوُا۟ (they saw) in the ayah, showing when something happened.

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes or mind

steps.n: Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Added وا at the end to change from 'he saw' to 'they saw'

steps.m: punishment (what is being seen in رَأَوُا)

steps.m: the punishment (what is being seen in رَأَوُا)

Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to show something was done to it (passive) instead of it doing something (active)

Changed the action into a thing by adding special vowel marks
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added بِ at the start to show how something is done
Added kasra at the end because it follows the word بَيْنَهُم (between them) in the ayah
Combination of conjunction و (and) and pronoun هم (they)
Basic negative particle that negates the verb يُظْلَمُونَ (they are wronged) that comes after it. Together they mean 'they will not be wronged'
Converting active to passive voice
Changes from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start, but keeps the passive meaning of 'being treated unfairly'
Added ون at the end to show this happens to multiple people
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Added أَ at the beginning to change this from a negative word to an attention-getter

steps.m: Now pay attention! (used to draw attention to what follows)

This is the base word referring to God in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.

The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.

Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س because it comes after ال

Basic negative particle with fatha added for pronunciation

steps.m: Now surely! (used to draw attention to what follows)

This is the basic three-letter root that carries the meaning of knowing

Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final ي becomes ى because it's at the end of the word.

Added tanwin kasra to show this is the object of the word 'from' (مِن) in the ayah

steps.m: for you all (object of أَنزَلَ - sent down)
steps.n: Added the plural 'you' pronoun كم to show who the preposition is directed to. This word shows who received what Allah sent down.
This is a question particle that introduces the second part of a two-part question. Here it connects to ءَآللَّهُ (did Allah) to ask if they have Allah's permission or are making things up.
Basic preposition showing position or opposition. In this ayah, it shows that the invention/lying is being done against Allah.
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and absoluteness of Allah
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and the alif at the beginning becomes a wasl form (ٱ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and changed vowels to show 'you' are doing the action of making up/inventing something false
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Basic question word that asks about things or introduces relative clauses
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to think or assume without certainty

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense
Changed from a verb (he thought) to a noun (thought/assumption) by combining the doubled letters with shadda
Added damma to show this is the subject of وَمَا (and what is)
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ٱلَّ makes it point to a specific person or thing
Adding ينَ changes it from singular to plural. This word is connected to يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent) in this ayah, referring to those who invent lies.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra
Added ت after first root letter to show doing action to oneself
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action, dropped the final ي
This is a basic preposition that shows something is on top of, upon, or against something else. In this ayah, it connects to 'the people' (ٱلنَّاسِ) to show Allah's favor upon them.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced: 'ʿalā'. The first mark (َ) makes an 'a' sound, and the ى at the end makes a long 'aa' sound.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam
Added ِ at the end to show this is the object of the word عَلَى (against/upon) in the phrase 'يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ' (they invent lies against Allah)
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added ال to make it specific. This word is what is being invented/fabricated in the phrase 'those who invent lies against Allah'
Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make the basic noun form
Added fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the verb يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent) in this ayah
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of rising - the final resurrection
Added ال to specify this is THE resurrection, not just any rising
Added kasra to show this describes يَوْمَ (the Day) that comes before it in the ayah
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizing what follows: اللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ - Allah is full of bounty)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) on the ن to make it stronger. This shows strong emphasis, making the statement that follows more certain and definite. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah truly has bounty for people.
Changed إِلَه to اللَه, which is a unique name specifically referring to The One True God in Islam

steps.m: Allah (as the object being spoken about)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show Allah is being spoken about in this sentence
This is a special word that means 'owner of' or 'possessor of'. It's used to connect two nouns to show that one has or possesses the other.
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'. This word connects to فَضْلٍ (bounty) in the ayah to mean 'surely possessor of bounty'.
The root letters ف-ض-ل (meaning 'to give extra') become the simple noun فَضْل (meaning 'favor/bounty'). Like how 'to help' becomes 'help'.
Added the kasratan (ٍ) to show this word is being possessed by ذو (owner of) in the phrase ذو فضل (owner of favor/bounty)

This is a preposition that usually means 'on/upon', but when used with the verb يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent/fabricate), it means 'against' - showing who is being wronged by the fabrication. In this ayah, it shows people are fabricating lies against Allah.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'people' by adding a long 'aa' sound
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by something else in the sentence
Added 'the' (ال) at the beginning, which caused the ن to get a shadda mark
This is a compound particle made up of 'wa' (and) + 'lakin' (but) + 'na' (emphasis)
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb showing something increased in number
Added أَ at start and changed middle vowel to make it mean 'more/most'. This pattern is used to compare things or show something has more of a quality.

steps.m: most of them (refers to those who invent lies about Allah mentioned earlier in the verse)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them' - specifically referring back to those who invent lies about Allah mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَشْكُرُونَ (they thank/are grateful) negative, changing its meaning to 'they do not thank/are not grateful'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed from past tense to present tense by adding ya- at start and changing vowels
Added plural marker ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word comes after لا (not) in the ayah, so together they mean 'they do not thank'
Added fatha to show this is a complete word. In this ayah, it's used to negate the verb تَكُونُ (you are).
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English.
Changed first vowel to damma and final vowel to damma to make present tense base. The middle و becomes a long vowel.
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action. This word appears in a longer phrase describing what you (Muhammad ﷺ) are doing.
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. Here it shows being 'in' a matter/affair (شَأْنٍ).
Added the kasra vowel mark (ِ) to show how to pronounce the word, and changed the final ي to ى which is common at the end of words
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'matter' or 'affair'
Added tanween kasra at the end to show this is referring to any matter or affair. This word relates to تَكُونُ (you are) in the phrase 'you are in any matter'.
Basic particle that can mean either 'what' (question word) or 'not' (negation). Here it's used to negate the verb تَكُونُ (you are).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it

steps.m: to follow or recite something in order

steps.n: The ت at start shows it's happening now by a female
Adding alif at the end shows this verb is in subjunctive mood (like 'to recite')
This is a basic preposition meaning 'from' or 'of'. It shows where something starts or comes from.

steps.m: from it (referring to the Quran being recited)
steps.n: Added هُ to show what is being recited from. In this ayah, it refers back to the Quran (قُرْءَانٍ) that is mentioned right after this word.
Basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects what is being recited (قُرْءَانٍ) to the act of reciting (تَتْلُوا۟).
This is the basic root meaning of reading or reciting
Changed from basic root 'read' into noun meaning 'reading' by adding vowel marks (u sound and stop mark)
Adding ان makes it mean 'the complete/ultimate reading'
Added tanween kasra to show this is indefinite and being acted upon by مِن (from) in the ayah
This is the basic negation word that shows something doesn't happen or isn't true

steps.m: and not (connects to تَعْمَلُونَ - you do)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this negation to the previous phrase. This connects to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ (you do) to say 'and you do not'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he worked' to a noun meaning 'working' by adding sukun and damma marks
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are doing the action
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word قُرْءَانٍ (Quran) to the verb تَتْلُوا۟ (you recite), showing what is being recited.
Connected the root letters and added vowel marks to create a noun meaning the result of working/acting
The tanween kasra (ٍ) shows this is indefinite (any deed) and is affected by the word مِنْ (from/of) before it

steps.m: except (what comes after this word is excluded from what was mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a particle that doesn't come from a root. It's used to exclude something from what was said before.
This is the basic concept of existing or being
Added damma and sukun to make the basic command form
Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' did this action in the past
This is the basic form of the preposition, derived from the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns it into a preposition meaning 'upon' or 'over'
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching a pronoun

steps.m: upon you all (as witnesses over you)
steps.n: Adding كُمْ shows this applies to multiple people being addressed. In this ayah, it refers to Allah being witness over all of you.

steps.m: witnesses (as the object being witnessed by كُنَّا/We)
steps.n: Root 'witness' changed to plural noun 'witnesses' with -an ending (not -un) to show it's the object being witnessed. Like changing 'see' to 'the seen ones'

steps.m: when (referring to تُفِيضُونَ - you delve into it)
steps.n: This is a time particle that connects two events. Here it connects the main verb تُفِيضُونَ (you delve into it) with كُنَّا عَلَيْكُمْ شُهُودًا (We were witnesses over you), showing when Allah was witnessing their actions.

steps.m: to flow abundantly or pour forth
to engage in or delve into (something)
steps.n: Added 'أ' at start to mean 'make something flow/pour' instead of just flowing - like making people flow/pour forth from a place
Changed from past tense 'he' to present tense 'she' by adding ت at start and changing vowels
Added plural masculine ending to show multiple people are being addressed
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment
Added هِ which means 'it' (masculine) to show what is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to the word عَمَلٍ (deed) that was mentioned earlier.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' that introduces what is being hidden/escaping in this ayah

steps.m: and what (connects to يَعْزُبُ - escapes/is hidden)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, damma on second root letter, and damma at end. This word shows what is hidden from your Lord in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows separation or distance from something, or introduces what something is about. Here it connects to رَّبِّكَ (your Lord) to show what is being discussed.

steps.m: to care for, nurture, develop to completion

steps.n: The doubled middle letter (shadda) intensifies the meaning to show complete mastery and lordship
Adding kasra at the end makes it a call/address to the Lord, like saying 'O my Lord!'
The word 'Lord' changed from meaning 'my Lord' to 'your Lord' by replacing the hidden 'my' with the suffix ك (ka) meaning 'your'. The doubled ر (r) sound is because it comes after عَن.

This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts from or what it's made of. In this ayah, it connects the word عَمَلٍ (deed) to تَعْمَلُونَ (you do), showing what type of action is being done.
Root meaning 'heavy' becomes a noun meaning 'weight'
Added مِ at the start to mean 'the amount/size of' a weight
Adding shadda on م makes the meaning stronger/more emphatic
Added kasra at end to show possession - this word is followed by ذَرَّةٍ (atom/speck) in the ayah, showing 'weight of an atom'
Doubling the ر shows how extremely tiny the particles are

steps.m: a single tiny particle (object of مِثْقَالِ - weight of)
steps.n: Adding ة makes it refer to one specific tiny particle. This word is being used to describe the smallest possible weight of something.

steps.m: of a single tiny particle (object of مِثْقَالِ - weight of)
steps.n: Adding the tanwin kasra (ٍ) shows this word follows مِثْقَالِ (weight of) in the phrase 'weight of a tiny particle'
This is a basic preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. In this ayah, it connects the word شَأْنٍ (matter/affair) to where it takes place.
Added vowel marks to show how the word is pronounced
Adding 'ال' makes the word definite, like adding 'the' in English
The kasra ending shows this word comes after the word فِي (in) in the ayah
Added fatha to show clear pronunciation

steps.m: and not (connected to تَعْمَلُونَ - you do)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this negative phrase with what came before it. This connects to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ (you do) to say 'and you do not'
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. It connects with شَأْنٍ (matter/affair) to show 'in a matter'.
The ي changes to ى at the end of words in Arabic writing style. This doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the verb 'to rise' into a noun 'sky' by adding a long aa sound and hamza at the end
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda

steps.m: of the sky (in ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows فِى (in) in the phrase 'in the earth and in the sky'
Added fatha to help with pronunciation
Added alif with madda to show long vowel sound
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb

steps.m: smaller than (comparative to مِثْقَالِ ذَرَّةٍ - weight of an atom)
steps.n: Changes from 'became small' to 'smaller than' by adding أ at the start - makes it compare to something else
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comparing things

steps.m: than (comparing with مِثْقَالِ ذَرَّةٍ - weight of an atom)
steps.n: Added a small diagonal line (kasra) under م to show it means 'than' when comparing two things
Basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this' for something near
Adding the alif above (ٰ) changes it from indicating something near to something far
Adding ل with kasra (لِ) adds emphasis and helps connect to the next part
Adding ك with fatha (كَ) shows we're talking about one masculine thing
This is a basic negative word used to say 'no' or make something not happen
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Added the alif with madda (آ) to show a longer sound

steps.m: and not (connected to previous negation وَلَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ)
steps.n: Added و with fatha to connect this negation with the previous phrase 'and not in the heaven'
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb showing something became large

Added أ at the beginning and changed vowels to make a comparative form, showing something is larger than something else. In this ayah, it's saying 'nor larger than that' referring to the size of an atom.
Basic form of the exception particle
Adding shadda emphasizes the exclusion meaning
Adding alif at the end helps connect this word to what follows it. In this ayah, it connects to كُنَّا (we were) to mean 'except that we were'
This is a basic preposition showing something is located inside or within something else. In this ayah, it's used to talk about things being 'in' the earth.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changes from root meaning 'write' into noun 'book' by adding alif and vowels (kasra and fatha)
Adds ٍ at the end to show this word is connected to the word مُّبِينٍ (clear) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'clear book'

steps.m: one who makes things clear (describing كِتٰبٍ)
steps.n: Adding مُ at the start makes it describe something that actively makes things clear. In this ayah, it's describing كِتٰبٍ (a book/record)

steps.m: a clear one (indefinite with genitive case)
steps.n: The ٍ at the end shows this word is indefinite and follows a preposition or is describing another genitive noun
The shadda (ّ) on م adds extra emphasis to show something is really clear/obvious
Starting with the basic negative particle لا
Adding أَ at the beginning changes this from a negative word to one that gets attention, like saying 'Hey!' in English
The alif is elongated (آ) to add emphasis and draw even more attention to the important statement that follows about Allah's allies
This is the basic form of the word that shows something depends on something else
Adding the shadda makes this word emphasize what comes after it. Here it emphasizes that Allah's allies (أَوْلِيَآءَ ٱللَّهِ) have a special status.
Adding vowels makes it a past tense verb showing someone protected or was close to something
Changed from a verb (he protected) to a noun (protector) by adding shadda - like changing 'protect' to 'protector'
Changed to plural form. This word is connected to اللَّهِ (Allah) showing these are Allah's special protectors/allies.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic word for 'deity'
Added 'the' to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show possession
Basic negative particle that makes something not true. In this ayah, it negates the word خَوْفٌ (fear), stating there will not be fear.
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation
Root letters arranged with fatha and sukoon to make the noun meaning 'fear'
This word is the subject of لَا (no/not) in this ayah, showing what won't affect Allah's allies.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'over'
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them)
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates يَحْزَنُونَ (they grieve).
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it. It connects 'no fear upon them' with 'and they do not grieve'.
This is the basic third person masculine singular pronoun meaning 'he' or 'him'
Adding مْ changes the pronoun from singular (he/him) to plural (they/them). This pronoun is the subject of the verb يَحْزَنُونَ (they grieve) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changes past tense to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they feel sad (referring to أَوْلِيَآءَ ٱللَّهِ - the allies of Allah)
steps.n: Adds ون to show multiple people are feeling sad. This refers back to Allah's allies mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Added ن at the end to show we're talking about multiple people
Added ال at the beginning with a shadda on the following letter. This points to specific people - in this case, those who believed (ءَامَنُوا۟).
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added a long alif after the first letter to make the meaning stronger

steps.m: they believed (subject of يَتَّقُونَ - they fear Allah)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show that a group of people did the action. This word is connected to يَتَّقُونَ (they fear Allah) in the ayah.
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels
The suffix وا indicates masculine plural past tense ('they')

steps.m: and they were (who believed in the previous ayah)
steps.n: Adds و meaning 'and' to connect this word with ءَامَنُوا (believed) in the previous part
Added pattern for doing something to yourself. The و from root changes to ت and combines with the other ت to make تّ
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start. The final ى changes to ي because it's ongoing action

steps.m: they protect themselves from harm (subject of الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word describes 'those who believed' (الَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟) in the ayah
Added fatha to show the vowel sound
Added هُمُ which means 'them' to show who receives the action. This word connects to البشرى (good news) in this ayah, showing who receives the good news.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a basic past tense verb
Changed the vowels and added ى at the end to make it mean the news itself instead of the action of giving news
Added ال to make it specific and an alif to extend the final vowel sound. This word refers to the good news that the believers receive.
This is a preposition that shows where or when something happens. Here it connects to 'life' (ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ) to show where the good news exists.
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
The root letters for 'life' got vowel marks (َ) to make the word 'living' - like adding life to the basic letters
Added و and ة to make it into a noun meaning 'life'
Added ال to make it definite and ِ because it comes after the preposition في (in)
Root meaning 'near' became a feminine word meaning 'lower/worldly' - like how Earth is close to us
Adding damma makes it a noun meaning 'the worldly life' rather than just 'lower'
Adding ال makes it specific - 'the' worldly life rather than just any worldly life
Adding the fatha mark shows this word is being used as an object in the sentence
Basic preposition meaning 'in'
Added kasra under ف and changed ي to ى at the end for proper spelling
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to the previous mention of 'worldly life' (ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا)

steps.m: to come after, to be last

steps.n: Root letters got vowels and an alif at start to make it mean 'the last one' or 'the one that comes after'
Added ة to make it feminine, which helps create the specific meaning of 'the final life' or 'hereafter'
Added ال (the) at beginning and ِ at end because it comes after the word في (in). Together these changes make it specifically mean the afterlife or hereafter.
This is a negative particle that negates the word تَبْدِيلَ (change/alteration) that comes after it, making the meaning 'there is no change'
Added fatha mark on ل to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: to exchange or substitute something for something else

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Doubling the middle letter shows the action is done thoroughly or multiple times
Changed the verb into a noun that means 'the act of doing something'. This word describes what cannot happen to Allah's words.
Forms the singular noun 'word' from the root meaning 'to speak'
Forms the feminine plural of 'word'
Adds the preposition لِ (for/to) and the genitive case ending ِ because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) in the ayah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added ال to make it definite - 'the' instead of 'a'
The word 'Allah' comes from 'al-ilah' (the god), but became a special name. The -i ending shows it belongs to كَلِمٰتِ (words of Allah)
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show distance
The ل with kasra (لِ) is added to show something is far away
The كَ shows this is talking about something masculine. In this ayah, it points to الْفَوْزُ (the success/triumph) that comes after it.
This is a personal pronoun that refers back to ذٰلِكَ (that) in the previous part of the ayah. It connects 'that' with 'the great success' that comes after it.
Changed from action to noun meaning the result of succeeding. This word is part of the phrase ذٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ (that is the success).
Adding ال makes it specific, and the ُ shows it's the subject in the phrase ذٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ (that is the success)
Added ي between second and third root letters with vowels to create an adjective showing strong quality. This word describes 'the triumph' (ٱلْفَوْزُ) in this ayah.

steps.m: the magnificent, the mighty (describing ٱلْفَوْزُ - the triumph)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite since it's describing the definite noun 'the triumph'
This is a negative particle that stops something from happening. Here it stops the sadness mentioned in يَحْزُنكَ (grieving/sadness)
The و at the beginning connects this with what came before it, showing this is part of a sequence of ideas
Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he became sad'
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start and changing the vowels. Means 'he becomes sad' in both tenses.

steps.m: he makes you sad (affected by their words قَوْلُهُمْ)
steps.n: The كَ at the end shows you are receiving the action. This word is affected by their words (قَوْلُهُمْ) which cause the sadness.

steps.m: saying/speech (subject of يَحْزُنكَ - let it not grieve you)
steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'speech'. This word is what should not cause grief in the ayah.
Adds the nominative case marker to show this noun is the subject

steps.m: their speech (as the subject of يَحْزُنكَ - let it not grieve you)
steps.n: The word قَوْلُ (speech) gets هُمْ added at the end to mean 'their speech' - like adding 'their' in English
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle that introduces certainty to what follows

steps.m: indeed/truly/certainly (emphasizes ٱلْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ - might belongs to Allah)
steps.n: Adding the shadda (doubling mark) on ن changes this from a conditional 'if' to a strong emphasis particle. It makes what comes after it more certain and important.
Root letters compressed with shadda and kasra to make a strong noun meaning power
Added ة to make it a specific instance noun
Added definite article to specify this particular might/honor
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
This is God's proper name in Arabic. The shadda (ّ) shows emphasis on the letter ل.
Added لِ at the beginning to show something belongs or is directed to Allah. The first ل combines with the ل in Allah.
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this connects to the word العِزَّةَ (honor/might) in the ayah, indicating that all honor belongs to Allah.

steps.m: to join or bring things together

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show the quality of being gathered or complete

steps.m: all together (describes how something is done)
steps.n: Added ًا at the end to show this word describes how something happens in the sentence

steps.m: he/it (refers to Allah in this ayah)
steps.n: This is a pronoun that refers to Allah in this ayah, connecting to السَّمِيعُ (The All-Hearing) and العَلِيمُ (The All-Knowing) which describe Him
Changed the word to show someone who hears in a complete or perfect way

steps.m: The All-Hearing One (a name/attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it definite and specific. The ل combines with س to make a doubled letter. This is one of Allah's names showing His perfect ability to hear everything.

steps.m: The All-Hearing One (subject of the sentence)
steps.n: The ُ at the end shows this is the subject of the sentence
Added ي between second and third root letters to show intensive quality. This pattern is used for Allah's attributes to show completeness and perfection of the quality.

Adding ال makes it definite (like 'the' in English). The ُ shows this is one of Allah's names being used as a subject. This is one of Allah's 99 names, showing His perfect and complete knowledge of everything.
Starting with the basic negative particle لَا which means 'no' or 'not'
Adding أَ at the beginning changes this from a negative word to an attention-getting word, like saying 'Hey!' or 'Listen up!'

steps.m: Now behold! / Indeed! (used to draw attention to what follows)
steps.n: The alif is lengthened to show emphasis in pronunciation, like making your voice stronger when you want to get someone's attention
This is a particle that adds emphasis to what follows it. In this ayah, it emphasizes the statement about what people follow (ما يتبع).
This is the base word Allah, with shadda on ل to show emphasis

steps.m: to/for Allah (belongs مَن فِى ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ - whoever is in the heavens)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show ownership/belonging and ِ at end to connect it with what follows. This shows that everything in the heavens belongs to Allah.
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used to talk about people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. In this ayah, it refers to whoever is in the heavens.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. In this ayah, it connects to 'the heavens' to show who is in them.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Adds ء (hamza) at the end to change from verb 'rose up' to noun 'sky'
Changes from singular 'sky' to plural 'skies' by adding وات at the end
The word gets 'the' (ال) added to it, and since it starts with س, the ل sound blends into it making a stronger س sound
Changed to show it belongs to something (added kasra at the end)
This is a question word that became a conditional/relative pronoun meaning 'whoever' or 'who'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it in the ayah
This is a basic preposition that shows location. In this ayah, it connects 'whoever' (مَن) with 'the heavens' (ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ) to say 'whoever is in the heavens'
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters together
The kasra at the end shows this word comes after the word في (in/on) in the ayah
Added fatha to show proper pronunciation. This word asks about what follows/pursues in this ayah.
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it. This connects to the previous statement about who belongs to Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start and changing vowels - shows ongoing action
The ت and ب merged together to make a stronger/doubled letter, showing more intense following
Starting with the basic demonstrative word and changing it to show connection
Adding ن at the end makes it refer to many people instead of just one
Adding ال makes it point to specific people. The shadda shows emphasis. This word is the subject (doer) of يَدْعُونَ (they call) in this ayah.
The alif at the beginning is written with a special mark (waslah) because it connects with the previous word in pronunciation
Added vowels to connect letters. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added يَ at start to show present tense, changed vowels, and restored و at end

steps.m: from/of (connects دُونِ with what comes before)
steps.n: Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it belongs to. Here it connects the word دُونِ (besides/other than) to what came before it in the ayah.

steps.m: to be lower or beneath something

steps.n: Root letters got a 'u' sound (damma) to make a word meaning 'below' or 'besides'
Added kasra at the end because this word is connected to اللَّهِ (Allah) in the ayah, showing it means 'besides Allah' or 'other than Allah'
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. The shadda shows the ل is doubled.
Changed to show 'of Allah' by adding kasra (i) sound at the end, and uses special alif since it connects to لِ before it
Added vowels to connect the root letters in basic past tense form
Changed the verb 'to partner' into a noun meaning 'one partner' by adding ي in the middle
Changed the vowels from 'i' to 'u' and 'a', then added اء at the end to make it mean 'many partners' instead of just one partner
Added final fatha and elongated alif to show this is the object of the verb يَدْعُونَ (they call upon)
Basic conditional particle that shows uncertainty. In this ayah, it connects with يَتَّبِعُونَ (they follow) to show 'if they follow'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Converting to present tense/imperfect aspect
Doubling the ت indicates intensity or emphasis in the action of following
Adds plural marker to show multiple people doing the action. This word is connected to يَدْعُونَ (they call upon) in the ayah, describing what these people do.
This is a complete word that shows an exception. In this ayah, it shows that 'they do nothing except guess' - limiting their action to just guessing.

steps.m: to assume or make guesses without certainty

steps.n: Adding vowel marks to make it a complete past tense verb

steps.m: the assumption (object of يَتَّبِعُونَ - they follow)
steps.n: Changed from a verb (he assumed) to a noun (assumption) and added ٱل (the) to show it's the specific assumption being talked about. This is what is being followed in 'they follow assumption'.
Combination of و (and) and إن (if)
This is a pronoun that refers to multiple people (all male or mixed group). In this ayah, it's connected to the verb يَخْرُصُونَ (they guess/conjecture), showing who is doing the guessing.
This is a common word used to show exceptions. In this ayah, it shows that they only follow assumptions/guesses (ٱلظَّنَّ), nothing else.
The root carries the idea of making assumptions without proper evidence
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: they guess without knowledge (subject of هُمْ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. This word relates to هُمْ (they) in the ayah.
This is a basic pronoun used to refer to a male person or thing that was mentioned before
Added vowel marks to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence. Here it refers to Allah who 'made/placed' (جَعَلَ) the night for people.
This is the basic form of the word that connects a description to what is being described

steps.m: the one who (refers to هُوَ - He)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter to make it specific. This word connects 'He' (هُوَ) to what He did (made the night for you)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to show this happened in the past. This word follows 'He (is) the One who' in the ayah, showing Allah is the one who made.
This is the basic preposition that shows something is being given or directed to someone

steps.m: for you all (connected to جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Added the pronoun 'you all' (كُم) to show who is receiving the action. The ُ at the end helps connect it smoothly to the next word. This word connects to جَعَلَ (He made) to show that Allah made something for you all.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to form the basic noun. This word is what Allah made for us (object of جَعَلَ).
Added 'the' at the beginning and accusative case ending to show this is the object being acted upon. The ل gets a shadda because it merges with the ل from ٱلْ.
The ال (al) at the start makes 'night' specific - like saying 'the night' instead of just 'night'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense with تَ prefix and changed vowels to show ongoing action
Adding وا makes it refer to a group of people

steps.m: so that you all rest (purpose of جَعَلَ)
steps.n: Adding لِ shows this is the purpose of Allah making the night (جَعَلَ in the ayah)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment

steps.m: in it (refers to الَّيْلَ - the night)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show what we're talking about. In this ayah, it refers back to الَّيْلَ (the night) that was mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to flow brightly, to shine forth
daylight (the time when light flows forth)
steps.n: Added alif after second root letter to turn the action of light flowing into the noun for the time when this happens

steps.m: the daylight (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific, and the ending َ shows it's what Allah made (object of جَعَلَ)

steps.m: and the daylight (object of جَعَلَ - He made)
steps.n: Adding و connects this to الَّيْلَ (the night) mentioned before it, showing Allah made both night and day
Added مُ at the start and ِ in the middle to show this is something that actively makes things visible
Added ًا at the end to show this describes how Allah made the day - it gives visibility to things
Adding the shadda (ّ) changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it stronger, like adding an exclamation mark in English.
This is a preposition that shows where or when something happens. In this ayah, it shows where/when people rest - during the night.
This is the basic demonstrative word for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written as a small mark above the ذ to show extended distance
Adding ل with kasra changes it from pointing to something near to something far
The ك at the end makes it specifically point to something that was just talked about (in this case, the night and day mentioned before)

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: The root letters combine with a long 'aa' sound (آ) and end with 'ah' (ة) to make a noun meaning 'sign' or 'miracle'
Changes the word from singular (one sign) to plural (many signs)
The ٍ shows this word is being described by what comes after it
The لَ adds emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed'. This word describes what is in ذٰلِكَ (that)

steps.m: to stand firmly or rise up

steps.n: Changes verb 'to stand' into noun meaning 'people' - those who stand together
Adds the preposition 'for/to' before the word 'people'

steps.m: for a group of people who (connected to يَسْمَعُونَ)
steps.n: Added shadda (double L sound) and tanween (un sound) to show it means 'for any people' - connects to يَسْمَعُونَ (those who listen)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb

steps.m: they hear/listen (referring to a group of people)
steps.n: Adds masculine plural subject marker to indicate 'they' instead of 'he'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a weak letter
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he took' in the past
Added ت between first and second root letters to show doing something for oneself. The first letter changed from أ to اِ for easier pronunciation.

steps.m: he adopted/took for himself (subject of قَالُوا)
steps.n: The ت gets a shadda (doubled) and the initial alif becomes a wasla (ٱ) since it's not needed for pronunciation when the word follows another word. This word is what was said in قَالُوا (they said).
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
Adding the short vowels 'a' changes it from a root to 'he gave birth/bore'
The ًا ending shows this word is being acted upon by the verb اتَّخَذَ (took/adopted) in the phrase 'they took/adopted a child'

steps.m: to be perfect, free from faults, glorify

steps.n: Root meaning 'glorify' becomes a special noun meaning 'glory/perfection' - like changing 'praise' to 'praise be to'
Adding ان to سُبْحَ makes it mean 'completely perfect and pure' - it's a special way to praise Allah
Adding the fatha (َ) at the end shows this word is being used as a direct object
Added the pronoun 'his' to show this refers to Allah's glory. This is used as an exclamation rejecting the false claim mentioned in the ayah that Allah has taken a son.
This is the basic form of the independent pronoun meaning 'he'

steps.m: he (subject of سُبْحٰنَهُۥ - Glorified is He)
steps.n: Added diacritics to show proper pronunciation: damma (ُ) on ه and fatha (َ) on و. This pronoun is the subject being glorified in the phrase سُبْحٰنَهُۥ (Glorified is He).

steps.m: to be free from need, to be self-sufficient

steps.n: Root meaning 'free from need' becomes 'The Self-Sufficient One' - a special title for Allah showing He needs nothing from anyone
Adding ال makes it definite and specific. Here it's one of Allah's names/attributes.
Added fatha to show the vowel sound

steps.m: for him/to him (connected to سُبْحٰنَهُۥ - glorified is He)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُ (him) to show who the preposition is directed to. This word connects to سُبْحٰنَهُۥ (glorified is He) in the ayah, referring to Allah.

steps.m: for him/to him (connected to سُبْحٰنَهُۥ - glorified is He)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: what/that which (refers to everything in السَّمٰوٰتِ and الْأَرْضِ)
steps.n: This is a basic word that connects to what comes after it. Here it refers to everything that exists in the heavens and earth. It's like saying "whatever is in..." in English.
This is a basic preposition showing location or containment. It cannot be broken down further.
Added ى at the end to complete the word. In this ayah, it shows what exists within the heavens.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a basic past-tense verb
Added alif and hamza to make a noun meaning the place that rises up
Added feminine plural ending وات to show multiple heavens
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda
Changed to a different spelling of 'the heavens' with a special ending (-i) to show it connects to 'earth' in the verse
This is a basic particle that can mean either 'what' (asking about something) or 'not' (negating something). In this ayah, it means 'what' and connects to 'in the earth' (فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ)
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it, like saying 'and' in English
This is a basic preposition that shows location. Here it connects 'what' (ما) with 'the heavens' (السَّمٰوٰتِ) to say 'what is in the heavens'
Added basic vowel marks to connect the letters: fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter
Added ال to make it definite (the earth), and kasra at the end shows it follows the word فِي (in) from the ayah
This is a negation particle that denies what comes after it. In this ayah, it negates that they have any authority/evidence (سُلْطٰنٍۭ) for their claim.
Added vowel marks to make it a preposition. The kasra and sukun pattern is common for prepositions in Arabic.
Added كُم at the end to show this is addressing multiple people. This word appears in a question asking if they have any proof (سُلْطٰنٍ) for their claims.
Basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what it's made of
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning. This emphasis shows up because the word before it ends with a similar sound.

steps.m: to have power or control over something
steps.n: Root relates to power and authority
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning 'power' by adding the vowel marks ُ (u) and ْ (sukoon)
The suffix ان makes the word stronger - like going from 'power' to 'absolute power'

steps.m: any proof or authority (used in this ayah to challenge those who claim Allah has a son)
steps.n: Changed to mean 'any proof' by adding the tanween (two dots below) to make it indefinite - showing they have no real proof at all
This is the basic demonstrative pronoun meaning 'this'. The آ is a spelling variation of ا used at the end of words.
Added the preposition بِ at the beginning to show this is being used as a tool or means. This word connects to the verb أَتَقُولُونَ (do you say) in the ayah.
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Changed from basic root to a command meaning 'say!' by adding the vowels u-u. This makes it a direct order to one person.
Added تَ prefix to show present tense 'you' form
Added أَ at the beginning to make it a question
Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people are being addressed
Basic preposition showing opposition or speaking against something. In this ayah, it shows people are saying things against Allah without knowledge.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.
Added kasra at the end to show this is the object of the word عَلَى (upon/against) in the phrase 'speaking against Allah'
This is a relative pronoun that means 'whatever' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it's used to say that everything in the heavens belongs to Allah.
Basic negative particle that makes the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) into its opposite meaning (you do not know). This particle always comes before the word it negates.
This form turns the action of knowing into the noun knowledge
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed from past tense (he knew) to present tense (knows) by removing the first vowel mark and changing the last vowel mark
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action
Added ونَ at the end to show many people are doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to a command form by: 1) dropping the middle weak letter و, 2) adding damma on first letter, and 3) adding sukun on last letter. This is telling one person to speak.
Basic form of the emphasis particle
Added kasra under إ and shadda on ن to show strong emphasis. This particle emphasizes the following phrase about 'those who invent lies'.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English.
Added ال with a shadda to make it more specific, like adding 'the' in English.
Added ين at the end to make it plural. This word refers to the people who are doing the action in يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent/fabricate).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel
Added ت after first letter to show doing something deliberately or making effort
Changed to plural by adding ون at the end. The ي at the end drops because it's a weak letter.

steps.m: against (referring to inventing lies against اللَّهِ)
steps.n: This is a preposition showing opposition or hostility in this context, since it's used with يَفْتَرُونَ (they invent/forge) to show inventing lies against Allah.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Changed from the general word for 'god' to become Allah (the proper name of the one true God), with 'the' added at the start and a kasra mark at the end
Adding vowels to make it a complete past tense verb meaning 'he lied'
Changed from a verb (he lied) to a noun (the lie) and added ٱلْ to make it specific
Added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb يَفْتَرُونَ (they fabricate)
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُفْلِحُونَ (they will succeed) into 'they will not succeed'
Added fatha mark to show the correct pronunciation as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels. This verb comes after لا (not) in the ayah.
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who invent lies about Allah.

steps.m: to enjoy or benefit from something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters together
The alif after the second root letter changes the verb into a noun meaning the result or object of enjoying
Adding the double dammah makes it indefinite (like saying 'a' in English)
This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects 'enjoyment' (from previous word مَتٰعٌ) to 'worldly life' (الدُّنْيَا)
The ي at the end is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changed the root into a feminine noun meaning 'lower' or 'closer', often used to describe this life as opposed to the afterlife
Added ال to make it definite. The د gets a shadda because it's a 'sun letter' that combines with the ل of ال
This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time in between. Here it connects their worldly enjoyment (مَتٰعٌ) with their future punishment (نُذِيقُهُمُ).
Added the vowel mark ُ (damma) on ث and doubled the م with ّ (shadda) to show how it's pronounced.
This is a preposition showing movement or direction towards something
Adding نا shows this means 'to us'. In this ayah, it's connected to the word مَرْجِعُ (return), showing that the return is towards us/Allah.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb
Added مَ at start and changed vowels to make a noun meaning 'place where returning happens'
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence

steps.m: their place of return (to إِلَيْنَا/to Us)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show it belongs to them. This word relates to إِلَيْنَا (to Us) in the previous part of the ayah, showing where they will return.
This is the basic form of the connecting word that shows sequence

steps.m: then, next, afterwards (connects إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ to what came before)
steps.n: Basic 'then' becomes stronger 'then' with marks above to show clear order of events
Connected the root letters with basic past tense pattern
Added causative form to show making someone else taste
Changed from 'he made taste' (past) to 'we make taste' (present) - notice the نُ at the start means 'we'
Added 'them' as the object of the verb. This word connects to عَذَابَ (punishment) which is what they are made to taste.

steps.m: punishment (object of نُذِيقُهُمُ - we will make them taste)
steps.n: Forms a noun meaning the result of punishment. This word is what will be 'tasted' according to نُذِيقُهُمُ (we will make them taste) in the ayah.

steps.m: the punishment (object of نُذِيقُهُمُ - we will make them taste)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ (the) at the start and َ at the end to show it's receiving the action
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changed the word pattern to show a strong quality
Added 'the' at the beginning and doubled the ش with a shadda
Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of 'We will make them taste' (نُذِيقُهُمُ)
Added kasra to the letter ب to make it readable. This is a preposition that shows how or why something happens.

steps.m: because of what (connects to يَكْفُرُونَ - they disbelieve)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what'. Together بِمَا means 'because of what'. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَكْفُرُونَ to show they will taste punishment because of what they disbelieve.
Added vowel marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he was'
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's a special type of verb with a weak middle letter
Added وا at the end to change 'he' to 'they'. This word is connected to يَكْفُرُونَ (they disbelieve) in the ayah, showing who was doing the disbelieving.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changes past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'they' by adding ون at the end - shows multiple people rejecting instead of just one person

steps.m: to follow closely or recite carefully

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed to command form by adding alif at start, sukun on middle letter, and damma at end. The final weak letter و is dropped in command form.
Adding hamzat al-wasl (connecting alif) at start helps pronunciation of command form
The vowel marks help show this is a command form
Added و with fatha at beginning to mean 'and'
The ى changes to يْ when adding endings
Added هِمْ at the end to show this applies to a group being talked about (them)
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form a noun meaning 'news' or 'story'. This word is what is being recited (object of اتْلُ) in this ayah.
This is the basic form of the proper name Noah. Unlike most Arabic words, proper names often don't come from roots.
The ٍ (tanwin kasra) at the end shows this is being used as part of a phrase meaning 'of Noah' or 'Noah's'. In this ayah, it's part of the phrase 'news of Noah' (نَبَأَ نُوحٍ).
This word helps tell when something happened in the past. Here it introduces what Nuh said to his people.
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Added fathas to make it past tense meaning 'he said'
Changed weak letter و to ا which is common in hollow verbs
Changed verb into noun by adding vowel marks. A قَوْم is a group that stands together as one community.

steps.m: people (ready to be connected to something)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word will be connected to something after it
Added هِ to show these are Noah's people being talked about
